By default, IAM denies all access to all services for users, and policies must be applied to grant access to AWS services and resources. This signal could indicate a policy is granting additional access within your cloud environment.

LEGACY-S00207.description: The AWS Key Management Service (KMS) can be used to generate key pairs for encrypting and decrypting your data. Disabling or deleting keys can come with heavy destructive consequences as data encrypted with those keys cannot be decrypted. AWS forces users to either disable keys allowing them to be re-enabled at a later time or users must schedule a key deletion at a later time if the keys absolutely must be removed. The default time for scheduling a key deletion is 30 days.
LEGACY-S00207.expression:
metadata_vendor = 'Amazon AWS'
AND metadata_product = 'CloudTrail'
AND application = 'kms.amazonaws.com'
AND action IN ('DisableKey', 'ScheduleKeyDeletion')
LEGACY-S00207.name: AWS CloudTrail - Customer Master Key Disabled or Scheduled for Deletion
LEGACY-S00207.normalized_description: The AWS Key Management Service (KMS) can be used to generate key pairs for encrypting and decrypting your data. Disabling or deleting keys can come with heavy destructive consequences as data encrypted with those keys cannot be decrypted. AWS forces users to either disable keys allowing them to be re-enabled at a later time or users must schedule a key deletion at a later time if the keys absolutely must be removed. The default time for scheduling a key deletion is 30 days.
LEGACY-S00207.normalized_signal_name: AWS CloudTrail - Customer Master Key Disabled or Scheduled for Deletion

MATCH-S00068.description_expression: This is usually routine administrative activity, but should be monitored in case an administrative user has been compromised.
MATCH-S00068.expression:
metadata_vendor = 'Microsoft'
AND metadata_product = 'Office 365'
and metadata_deviceEventId in ('AzureActiveDirectory', '8')
and fields['Operation'] = 'Change user password.'
and targetUser_username != ''
MATCH-S00068.name: O365 - Users Password Changed
MATCH-S00068.normalized_description: This is usually routine administrative activity, but should be monitored in case an administrative user has been compromised.
MATCH-S00068.normalized_summary: The account {{targetUser_username}} had its password changed by the account {{user_username}}
MATCH-S00068.summary_expression: The account {{targetUser_username}} had its password changed by the account {{user_username}}

MATCH-S00078.description_expression: Proofpoint TAP detected an IP address sending an email with a malware score 75 or higher. Records indicating the email was permitted will have a higher signal score compared to those automatically blocked by Proofpoint.
MATCH-S00078.expression:
metadata_vendor = 'Proofpoint'
AND metadata_product = 'Targeted Attack Protection'
AND metadata_deviceEventId in ('MESSAGE_BLOCKED', 'MESSAGE_PERMITTED', 'MESSAGE_DELIVERED')
AND int(fields['malwareScore']) >= 75
AND srcDevice_ip not in ('0.0.0.0', '127.0.0.1', '')
AND srcDevice_ip is not null
MATCH-S00078.name: Proofpoint TAP - IP Sent Email with Malware
MATCH-S00078.name_expression: Proofpoint TAP - IP Sent Email with Malware

MATCH-S00078.normalized_summary: Malware from IP: {{srcDevice_ip}} {{action}} on IP: {{device_ip}} by user: {{user_username}}
MATCH-S00078.summary_expression: Malware from IP: {{srcDevice_ip}} {{action}} on IP: {{device_ip}} by user: {{user_username}}

MATCH-S00096.description_expression: This signal detects when a successful root account login occurred within an AWS account. This privileged account should seldomly be used within an AWS cloud environment. Amazon's best practices state you should only use the root account to create the initial local IAM users and assigned one of the accounts administrative privileges or to perform rare tasks only available to the root user. The security operations center should be aware when the AWS root account is accessed.
MATCH-S00096.expression:
metadata_vendor = 'Amazon AWS'
AND metadata_product = 'CloudTrail'
AND application = 'signin.amazonaws.com'
AND action = 'ConsoleLogin'
AND lower(fields['userIdentity.type']) = 'root'
AND success = true
MATCH-S00096.name: AWS CloudTrail - Root Console Successful Login Observed
MATCH-S00096.name_expression: AWS CloudTrail - Root Console Successful Login Observed from {{srcDevice_ip}}
MATCH-S00096.normalized_description: This signal detects when a successful root account login occurred within an AWS account. This privileged account should seldomly be used within an AWS cloud environment. Amazon's best practices state you should only use the root account to create the initial local IAM users and assigned one of the accounts administrative privileges or to perform rare tasks only available to the root user. The security operations center should be aware when the AWS root account is accessed.
MATCH-S00096.normalized_signal_name: AWS CloudTrail - Root Console Successful Login Observed from {{srcDevice_ip}}
MATCH-S00096.normalized_summary: User: {{user_username}} successfully logged on a root
MATCH-S00096.summary_expression: User: {{user_username}} successfully logged on a root

MATCH-S00099.description_expression: This rule identifies both 'attach' and 'put' actions with this privileged policy.  The difference between 'attach' and 'put' is that 'attach' actions apply a managed policy to an item, where a 'put' action indicates the policy is defined in-line and is part of the items definition. Applying privileged policies to items could indicate hostile action that attempts to increase the privilege level of a user or set of users.  There are legitimate times when this will occur, consider this signal in context of other activities that may indicate suspicious behavior.
MATCH-S00099.expression:
metadata_vendor = 'Amazon AWS'
AND metadata_product = 'CloudTrail'
AND application = 'iam.amazonaws.com'
AND action IN ('AttachUserPolicy', 
               'PutUserPolicy')
AND fields['requestParameters.policyArn'] IN (
    'arn:aws:iam::aws:policy/AdministratorAccess',
    'arn:aws:iam::aws:policy/AmazonSNSFullAccess',
    'arn:aws:iam::aws:policy/AmazonEC2FullAccess',
    'arn:aws:iam::aws:policy/AmazonS3FullAccess',
    'arn:aws:iam::aws:policy/IAMFullAccess',
    'arn:aws:iam::aws:policy/AmazonDynamoDBFullAccess',
    'arn:aws:iam::aws:policy/AWSCodeCommitPowerUser',
    'arn:aws:iam::aws:policy/AWSKeyManagementServicePowerUser',
    'arn:aws:iam::aws:policy/PowerUserAccess',
    'arn:aws:iam::aws:policy/DatabaseAdministrator',
    'arn:aws:iam::aws:policy/NetworkAdministrator',
    'arn:aws:iam::aws:policy/SystemAdministrator',
    'arn:aws:iam::aws:policy/Billing'
)
MATCH-S00099.name: AWS CloudTrail - IAM Privileged Policy Applied to User
MATCH-S00099.name_expression: AWS CloudTrail - IAM Privileged Policy Applied to User {{fields['requestParameters.userName']}}
MATCH-S00099.normalized_description: This rule identifies both 'attach' and 'put' actions with this privileged policy.  The difference between 'attach' and 'put' is that 'attach' actions apply a managed policy to an item, where a 'put' action indicates the policy is defined in-line and is part of the items definition. Applying privileged policies to items could indicate hostile action that attempts to increase the privilege level of a user or set of users.  There are legitimate times when this will occur, consider this signal in context of other activities that may indicate suspicious behavior.
MATCH-S00099.normalized_signal_name: AWS CloudTrail - IAM Privileged Policy Applied to User {{fields['requestParameters.userName']}}
MATCH-S00099.normalized_summary: Privileged Policy: [{{fields['requestParameters.policyArn']}}] an Amazon default policy that carries a high level of access was noticed being applied to user {{fields['requestParameters.userName']}}
MATCH-S00099.summary_expression: Privileged Policy: [{{fields['requestParameters.policyArn']}}] an Amazon default policy that carries a high level of access was noticed being applied to user {{fields['requestParameters.userName']}}

MATCH-S00105.description_expression: An AWS request occurred to either create a new public bucket or to add a bucket access control list (ACL) to an existing bucket to make it public. While there are some use cases for AWS S3 public buckets, most are generally private. The security operations center should have a strong understanding of which buckets are allowed to be public.
MATCH-S00105.expression:
metadata_vendor = 'Amazon AWS'
AND metadata_product = 'CloudTrail'
AND application = 's3.amazonaws.com'
AND action in ('CreateBucket', 'PutBucketAcl')
AND (
    lower(fields['requestParameters.x-amz-acl']) like '%public%'
    or fields['requestParameters.x-amz-grant-read'] like '%AllUsers%'
    or fields['requestParameters.x-amz-grant-read'] like '%AuthenticatedUsers%'
    or fields['requestParameters.x-amz-grant-read-acp'] like '%AllUsers%'
    or fields['requestParameters.x-amz-grant-read-acp'] like '%AuthenticatedUsers%'
    or fields['requestParameters.x-amz-grant-write'] like '%AllUsers%'
    or fields['requestParameters.x-amz-grant-write'] like '%AuthenticatedUsers%'
    or fields['requestParameters.x-amz-grant-write-acp'] like '%AllUsers%'
    or fields['requestParameters.x-amz-grant-write-acp'] like '%AuthenticatedUsers%'
    or fields['requestParameters.AccessControlPolicy.AccessControlList.Grant.1.Grantee.URI'] like '%AllUsers%'
    or fields['requestParameters.AccessControlPolicy.AccessControlList.Grant.1.Grantee.URI'] like '%AuthenticatedUsers%'
    or fields['requestParameters.AccessControlPolicy.AccessControlList.Grant.2.Grantee.URI'] like '%AllUsers%'
    or fields['requestParameters.AccessControlPolicy.AccessControlList.Grant.2.Grantee.URI'] like '%AuthenticatedUsers%'
    or fields['requestParameters.AccessControlPolicy.AccessControlList.Grant.3.Grantee.URI'] like '%AllUsers%'
    or fields['requestParameters.AccessControlPolicy.AccessControlList.Grant.3.Grantee.URI'] like '%AuthenticatedUsers%'
    or fields['requestParameters.AccessControlPolicy.AccessControlList.Grant.4.Grantee.URI'] like '%AllUsers%'
    or fields['requestParameters.AccessControlPolicy.AccessControlList.Grant.4.Grantee.URI'] like '%AuthenticatedUsers%'
    or fields['requestParameters.AccessControlPolicy.AccessControlList.Grant.5.Grantee.URI'] like '%AllUsers%'
    or fields['requestParameters.AccessControlPolicy.AccessControlList.Grant.5.Grantee.URI'] like '%AuthenticatedUsers%'
)
MATCH-S00105.name: AWS CloudTrail - Public S3 Bucket Exposed
MATCH-S00105.name_expression: AWS CloudTrail - Public S3 Bucket Exposed
MATCH-S00105.normalized_description: An AWS request occurred to either create a new public bucket or to add a bucket access control list (ACL) to an existing bucket to make it public. While there are some use cases for AWS S3 public buckets, most are generally private. The security operations center should have a strong understanding of which buckets are allowed to be public.
MATCH-S00105.normalized_signal_name: AWS CloudTrail - Public S3 Bucket Exposed
MATCH-S00105.normalized_summary: S3 Bucket {{changeTarget}} has been placed in an ACL allowing it to be publicly exposed
MATCH-S00105.summary_expression: S3 Bucket {{changeTarget}} has been placed in an ACL allowing it to be publicly exposed

metadata_vendor = 'Amazon AWS'
AND metadata_product = 'CloudTrail'
AND application = 'iam.amazonaws.com'

MATCH-S00111.name_expression: AWS CloudTrail - IAM CreateUser Action Observed

MATCH-S00113.description_expression: Changing the configuration of logging to any mission-critical service or platform should be closely monitored. For instance disabling/deleting logs is a higher severity than enabling logs.
MATCH-S00113.expression:
metadata_vendor = 'Amazon AWS'
  and metadata_product = 'CloudTrail'
  and metadata_deviceEventId IN ('AwsApiCall-CreateTrail', 
                    'AwsApiCall-UpdateTrail',
                    'AwsApiCall-DeleteTrail',
                    'AwsApiCall-StartLogging',
                    'AwsApiCall-StopLogging',
                    'AwsApiCall-DeleteLogGroup',
                    'AwsApiCall-DeleteLogStream',
                    'AwsApiCall-DeleteDestination')
MATCH-S00113.name: AWS CloudTrail - Logging Configuration Change Observed

MATCH-S00134.description_expression: 'changeTarget' should be populated with a reguar expression that matches the user naming convention. This rule detects a user account that has been created that does not fit the normal naming convention established. If an unauthorized account has been created, it could be used to maliciously access additional systems.
MATCH-S00134.expression:
metadata_vendor = 'Microsoft'
AND metadata_product = 'Windows'
AND metadata_deviceEventId = 'Security-4720'
AND changeTarget NOT RLIKE 'naming_convention'
MATCH-S00134.name: Windows User Account Created with Abnormal Naming Convention
MATCH-S00134.name_expression: Windows User Account Created with Abnormal Naming Convention
MATCH-S00134.normalized_description: 'changeTarget' should be populated with a reguar expression that matches the user naming convention. This rule detects a user account that has been created that does not fit the normal naming convention established. If an unauthorized account has been created, it could be used to maliciously access additional systems.
MATCH-S00134.normalized_signal_name: Windows User Account Created with Abnormal Naming Convention
MATCH-S00134.normalized_summary: Detected unusually named account creation with name: {{targetUser_username}} on host: {{device_hostname}}
MATCH-S00134.summary_expression: Detected unusually named account creation with name: {{targetUser_username}} on host: {{device_hostname}}

MATCH-S00135.description_expression: This rule detects modifications to registry keys commonly targeted to achieve persistence.
MATCH-S00135.expression:
metadata_vendor = 'Microsoft'
AND metadata_product = 'Windows'
AND metadata_deviceEventId IN ('Microsoft-Windows-Sysmon/Operational-12','Microsoft-Windows-Sysmon/Operational-13','Microsoft-Windows-Sysmon/Operational-14')
AND (
    file_path LIKE '%Software\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\Run%'
    OR file_path LIKE '%Software\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\Explorer\User Shell Folders%'
    OR file_path LIKE '%Software\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\Explorer\Shell Folders%'
    OR file_path LIKE '%Software\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\Policies\Explorer\Run%'
    OR file_path LIKE '%Software\Microsoft\Windows NT\CurrentVersion\Winlogon\Userinit%'
    OR file_path LIKE '%Software\Microsoft\Windows NT\CurrentVersion\Winlogon\Shell%'
    OR file_path LIKE '%Software\Microsoft\Windows NT\CurrentVersion\Windows%'
    OR file_path LIKE '%System\CurrentControlSet\Control\Session Manager%'
)
    AND NOT LOWER(file_path) LIKE '%ctfmon.exe%'
    AND NOT LOWER(file_path) LIKE '%application restart%'
    AND NOT LOWER(file_path) LIKE '%software\microsoft\windows\currentversion\runonce\uninstall%'
    AND NOT LOWER(file_path) LIKE '%squirrel%'
    AND NOT LOWER(file_path) LIKE '%\skype%'
    AND NOT LOWER(file_path) LIKE '%\lync%'
    AND NOT LOWER(file_path) LIKE '%\zoom%'
    AND NOT LOWER(file_path) LIKE '%\spotify%'
    AND NOT LOWER(file_path) LIKE '%\delete cache%'
    AND NOT LOWER(file_path) LIKE '%\gotomeeting%'
    AND NOT LOWER(file_path) LIKE '%\wab migrate%'
    AND NOT LOWER(file_path) LIKE '%\officesyncprocess%'
    AND NOT LOWER(file_path) LIKE '%\onedrivesetup%'
MATCH-S00135.name: Suspicious Registry Key Modification
MATCH-S00135.normalized_description: This rule detects modifications to registry keys commonly targeted to achieve persistence.
MATCH-S00135.normalized_summary: Suspicious registry key modification detected on host: {{device_hostname}}
MATCH-S00135.summary_expression: Suspicious registry key modification detected on host: {{device_hostname}}

MATCH-S00136.commit_message: TLAB-1247 PowerShell Encoded Command Rule Summary Change
MATCH-S00136.description_expression: PowerShell can execute encoded Base64 strings with the Encoded Command cmdlet. Attackers will often use Base64 to obfuscate their payloads until they can decode and execute it with PowerShell.
MATCH-S00136.expression:
(LOWER(baseImage) LIKE '%\powershell.exe' OR LOWER(baseImage) LIKE '%\powershell_ise.exe' OR LOWER(baseImage) LIKE '%\pwsh.exe')
AND (LOWER(commandLine) LIKE '%-enc %'
    OR LOWER(commandLine) LIKE '%-encodedcommand %'
    OR LOWER(commandLine) LIKE '%-en %'
    OR LOWER(commandLine) LIKE '%-ec %'
    OR LOWER(commandLine) LIKE '%-e %') AND NOT array_contains(listMatches,'verified_command_lines')
MATCH-S00136.name: PowerShell Encoded Command
MATCH-S00136.normalized_description: PowerShell can execute encoded Base64 strings with the Encoded Command cmdlet. Attackers will often use Base64 to obfuscate their payloads until they can decode and execute it with PowerShell.
MATCH-S00136.normalized_summary: Encoded Powershell command executed on host: {{device_hostname}}
MATCH-S00136.summary_expression: Encoded Powershell command executed on host: {{device_hostname}}

MATCH-S00137.commit_message: Fix typo in summary expression key
MATCH-S00137.description_expression: This alert detects a shell launched by an office product or browser that should not be spawning shell processes. Attackers may inject code into Office documents or abuse Windows utilities to spawn shells that will execute malicious commands.
MATCH-S00137.expression:
(
    LOWER(baseImage) LIKE '%\cmd.exe%'
    OR
    LOWER(baseImage) LIKE '%\powershell.exe%'
    OR
    LOWER(baseImage) LIKE '%\powershell_ise.exe%'
    OR
    LOWER(baseImage) LIKE '%\pwsh.exe%')
AND (
    LOWER(parentBaseImage) LIKE '%\winword.exe%'
    OR LOWER(parentBaseImage) LIKE '%\excel.exe%'
    OR LOWER(parentBaseImage) LIKE '%\powerpnt.exe%'
    OR LOWER(parentBaseImage) LIKE '%\chrome.exe%'
    OR LOWER(parentBaseImage) LIKE '%\firefox.exe%'
    OR LOWER(parentBaseImage) LIKE '%\iexplore.exe%'
    OR LOWER(parentBaseImage) LIKE '%\outlook.exe%'
    )
AND commandLine NOT LIKE '%chrome.nativeMessaging%'
MATCH-S00137.name: Office Application or Browser Launching Shell
MATCH-S00137.name_expression: Office Application or Browser Launching Shell
MATCH-S00137.normalized_description: This alert detects a shell launched by an office product or browser that should not be spawning shell processes. Attackers may inject code into Office documents or abuse Windows utilities to spawn shells that will execute malicious commands.
MATCH-S00137.normalized_signal_name: Office Application or Browser Launching Shell

MATCH-S00138.description_expression: Detects an interactive login using a service account. Service accounts should only be used by applications or services and not users. An interactive logon indicates a user has the service account credentials.
MATCH-S00138.expression:
metadata_vendor = 'Microsoft' 
AND metadata_product = 'Windows' 
AND metadata_deviceEventId = 'Security-4624' 
AND logonType IN ('Interactive', 'RemoteInteractive', 'CachedInteractive') 
AND (
    LOWER(user_username) LIKE '%svc%'
    OR
    LOWER(user_username) LIKE '%service%'
    ) 
AND application NOT LIKE '%Advapi%'
MATCH-S00138.name: Interactive Logon with Service Account
MATCH-S00138.normalized_description: Detects an interactive login using a service account. Service accounts should only be used by applications or services and not users. An interactive logon indicates a user has the service account credentials.
MATCH-S00138.normalized_summary: Interactive logon on host: {{device_hostname}} with account: {{user_username}}
MATCH-S00138.summary_expression: Interactive logon on host: {{device_hostname}} with account: {{user_username}}

MATCH-S00139.commit_message: TLAB-2229: tighten up match expression for process paths (#5866)
MATCH-S00139.description_expression: This alert detects a Windows process spawned by a parent process that does not normally spawn it.
MATCH-S00139.expression:
parentBaseImage IS NOT NULL
AND parentBaseImage != ''
AND commandLine IS NOT NULL
AND commandLine != ''AND (
    (LOWER(baseImage) MATCHES /\\wininit\.exe$/
    AND NOT LOWER(parentBaseImage)MATCHES /\\smss.exe$/
    AND parentBaseImage != '?')
    OR
    (LOWER(baseImage) MATCHES /\\services\.exe$/
    AND NOT LOWER(parentBaseImage) MATCHES /\\wininit\.exe$/
    )
    OR 
    (LOWER(baseImage) MATCHES /\\smss\.exe$/
    AND NOT LOWER(parentBaseImage) MATCHES /system/
    AND NOT LOWER(parentBaseImage) MATCHES /\\smss\.exe$/
    AND parentBaseImage != '?')
    OR
    (LOWER(baseImage) MATCHES /\\csrss\.exe$/
    AND NOT LOWER(parentBaseImage) MATCHES /\\smss\.exe$/
    AND NOT LOWER(parentBaseImage) MATCHES /\\csrss\.exe$/
    AND parentBaseImage != '?')
    OR
    (LOWER(baseImage) MATCHES /\\system\.exe$/)
    OR
    (LOWER(baseImage) MATCHES /\\svchost\.exe$/
    AND NOT LOWER(parentBaseImage) MATCHES /\\services\.exe$/
    AND NOT parentBaseImage MATCHES /\\MsMpEng\.exe$/
    AND NOT (LOWER(parentBaseImage)MATCHES /\\svchost\.exe$/
            AND LOWER(commandLine) like '% -k %'))
    )
MATCH-S00139.name: Abnormal Parent-Child Process Combination
MATCH-S00139.normalized_description: This alert detects a Windows process spawned by a parent process that does not normally spawn it.
MATCH-S00139.normalized_summary: Process: {{baseImage}} spawned by Parent Process: {{parentBaseImage}} on host: {{device_hostname}} by user: {{user_username}}
MATCH-S00139.summary_expression: Process: {{baseImage}} spawned by Parent Process: {{parentBaseImage}} on host: {{device_hostname}} by user: {{user_username}}

MATCH-S00146.commit_message: TLAB-1399 Update summary of WMI Process Call Create
MATCH-S00146.description_expression: An attacker can use WMI to create malicious processes on the local or remote host to bypass application whitelisting, since WMI is an authorized Windows tool.
MATCH-S00146.expression:
(LOWER(baseImage) LIKE '%\wmic.exe'
    OR LOWER(baseImage) LIKE '%\wmiprvse.exe')
AND LOWER(commandLine) LIKE '%process call create%'
MATCH-S00146.name: WMI Process Call Create
MATCH-S00146.normalized_description: An attacker can use WMI to create malicious processes on the local or remote host to bypass application whitelisting, since WMI is an authorized Windows tool.
MATCH-S00146.normalized_summary: Detected WMI process call creation on host: {{device_hostname}}
MATCH-S00146.summary_expression: Detected WMI process call creation on host: {{device_hostname}}

MATCH-S00147.description_expression: Attackers will often utilize The Managed Object Format (MOF) compiler to conceal and execute their malicious code within the WMI Repository.
MATCH-S00147.expression:
LOWER(commandLine) LIKE '%mofcomp%'
AND NOT LOWER(commandLine) LIKE '%microsoft policy platform%'
AND NOT LOWER(commandLine) LIKE '%windows defender%'
AND NOT LOWER(commandLine) LIKE '%intel(r) management engine components%'
AND NOT LOWER(parentBaseImage) LIKE '%program Files\cylance\desktop\cyupdate.exe'
AND NOT LOWER(commandLine) rlike '([a-z0-9]{8}\-[a-z0-9]{4}\-[a-z0-9]{4}\-[a-z0-9]{4}\-[a-z0-9]{12})\.mof'
AND NOT LOWER(commandLine) LIKE '%microsoft.configurationmanager.svprovider_v%.mof%'
AND NOT LOWER(commandLine) LIKE '%microsoft.configurationmanager.assetprovider_v%.mof%'
AND NOT LOWER(commandLine) LIKE '%microsoft.configurationmanager.defaultappsprovider_v%.mof%'
AND NOT LOWER(commandLine) LIKE '%microsoft.configurationmanager.winrtprovider_v%.mof%'
AND NOT LOWER(commandLine) LIKE '%\program files\microsoft configuration manager\%'
AND NOT LOWER(commandLine) LIKE '%cylance.host.wmiprovider_v%'
MATCH-S00147.name: WMI Managed Object Format (MOF) Process Execution
MATCH-S00147.name_expression: WMI Managed Object Format (MOF) Process Execution
MATCH-S00147.normalized_description: Attackers will often utilize The Managed Object Format (MOF) compiler to conceal and execute their malicious code within the WMI Repository.
MATCH-S00147.normalized_signal_name: WMI Managed Object Format (MOF) Process Execution
MATCH-S00147.normalized_summary: Detected WMI Managed Object Format Process execution on host: {{device_hostname}}
MATCH-S00147.summary_expression: Detected WMI Managed Object Format Process execution on host: {{device_hostname}}

MATCH-S00149.description_expression: PowerShell scripts are commonly used as droppers, which will download additional tools onto a compromised host.
MATCH-S00149.expression:
(LOWER(baseImage) LIKE '%\powershell.exe' OR LOWER(baseImage) LIKE '%\powershell_ise.exe' OR LOWER(baseImage) LIKE '%\pwsh.exe')
AND (LOWER(commandLine) LIKE '%.download%'
    OR LOWER(commandLine) LIKE '%invoke-webrequest%'
    OR LOWER(commandLine) LIKE '%start-bitstransfer%'
    OR LOWER(commandLine) LIKE '%net.webclient%'
    OR LOWER(commandLine) LIKE '%iwr %'
    OR LOWER(commandLine) LIKE '%wget %'
    OR LOWER(commandLine) LIKE '%curl %')
MATCH-S00149.name: PowerShell File Download
MATCH-S00149.normalized_description: PowerShell scripts are commonly used as droppers, which will download additional tools onto a compromised host.
MATCH-S00149.normalized_summary: Powershell file download command executed on host: {{device_hostname}}
MATCH-S00149.summary_expression: Powershell file download command executed on host: {{device_hostname}}

MATCH-S00150.description_expression: Observes for Windows Management Instrumentation (WMI) launching a shell
MATCH-S00150.expression:
(LOWER(parentBaseImage) LIKE '%\wmic.exe'
    OR LOWER(parentBaseImage) LIKE '%\wmiprvse.exe')
AND (LOWER(baseImage) LIKE '%\powershell.exe'
    OR LOWER(baseImage) LIKE '%\powershell_ise.exe%'
    OR LOWER(baseImage) LIKE '%\pwsh.exe%'
    OR LOWER(baseImage) LIKE '%\cmd.exe')
AND NOT LOWER(commandLine) like '%nessus%'
AND NOT LOWER(commandLine) like '%endgame%'
AND NOT LOWER(commandLine) like '%ccm\%'
AND NOT LOWER(user_username) like '%snow%'
AND NOT LOWER(user_username) like '%forescout%'
MATCH-S00150.name: WMI Launching Shell
MATCH-S00150.normalized_description: Observes for Windows Management Instrumentation (WMI) launching a shell
MATCH-S00150.normalized_summary: A WMI process has been used to launch a shell on host: {{device_hostname}}
MATCH-S00150.summary_expression: A WMI process has been used to launch a shell on host: {{device_hostname}}

MATCH-S00151.description_expression: An attacker can use WMI to execute scripts on a host by pointing to malicious XSL files.
MATCH-S00151.expression:
(LOWER(baseImage) LIKE '%\wmic.exe'
    OR LOWER(baseImage) LIKE '%\wmiprvse.exe')
AND LOWER(commandLine) LIKE '%process get brief%'
MATCH-S00151.name: WMI Process Get Brief
MATCH-S00151.normalized_description: An attacker can use WMI to execute scripts on a host by pointing to malicious XSL files.
MATCH-S00151.normalized_summary: Detected WMI process get brief on host: {{device_hostname}}
MATCH-S00151.summary_expression: Detected WMI process get brief on host: {{device_hostname}}

MATCH-S00152.description_expression: A new or renamed user account which starts with a $ following machine account naming conventions.  Attackers could use this to bypass detection logic where machine names are filtered from rules.
MATCH-S00152.expression:
metadata_vendor = 'Microsoft' 
AND metadata_product = 'Windows'
AND (        
    (metadata_deviceEventId = 'Security-4720' AND changeTarget like '%$%')
    OR (metadata_deviceEventId = 'Security-4781' AND fields['EventData.NewTargetUserName'] like '%$%' AND NOT fields['EventData.OldTargetUserName'] like '%$%')
)
MATCH-S00152.name: New or Renamed Windows User Account Mimicking a Machine Account
MATCH-S00152.name_expression: New or Renamed Windows User Account Mimicking a Machine Account
MATCH-S00152.normalized_description: A new or renamed user account which starts with a $ following machine account naming conventions.  Attackers could use this to bypass detection logic where machine names are filtered from rules.

MATCH-S00152.normalized_summary: User: {{user_username}} may be masquerading as a machine account
MATCH-S00152.summary_expression: User: {{user_username}} may be masquerading as a machine account

MATCH-S00153.description_expression: Attackers have been known to leverage PowerShell for scheduled task creation for the purpose of maintaining persistence in a Windows based environment.
MATCH-S00153.expression:
(LOWER(baseImage) LIKE '%\powershell.exe' OR LOWER(baseImage) LIKE '%\powershell_ise.exe' OR LOWER(baseImage) LIKE '%\pwsh.exe')
AND (LOWER(commandLine) LIKE '%new-scheduledtask%'
    OR LOWER(commandLine) LIKE '%set-scheduledtask%'
    OR LOWER(commandLine) LIKE '%schtasks%')
MATCH-S00153.name: Scheduled Task Created via PowerShell
MATCH-S00153.normalized_description: Attackers have been known to leverage PowerShell for scheduled task creation for the purpose of maintaining persistence in a Windows based environment.

MATCH-S00153.summary_expression: Scheduled task created with Powershell on host: {{device_hostname}}

MATCH-S00156.description_expression: The Service Control Manager, or services.exe, has no legitimate reason to launch commands like cmd.exe, powershell.exe, or regedit.exe. Incidentally, a common way for malware to masquerade as something legitimate is to call itself service.exe.
MATCH-S00156.expression:
parentBaseImage like '%\services.exe' AND 
(baseImage like '%\cmd.exe' OR baseImage like '%\regedit.exe' OR baseImage like '%\powershell.exe' OR baseImage like '%\pwsh.exe' OR baseImage like '%\powershell_ise.exe')
MATCH-S00156.name: New Suspicious cmd.exe / regedit.exe / powershell.exe Service Launch
MATCH-S00156.normalized_description: The Service Control Manager, or services.exe, has no legitimate reason to launch commands like cmd.exe, powershell.exe, or regedit.exe. Incidentally, a common way for malware to masquerade as something legitimate is to call itself service.exe.

MATCH-S00157.description_expression: This alert detects a process executing with a name that closely resembles a default Windows process. Malware will often attempt to disguise its execution by using a similar name to blend in with standard processes.
MATCH-S00157.expression:
(LOWER(baseImage) LIKE '%\isass.exe'
    OR LOWER(baseImage) LIKE '%\svch0st.exe'
    OR LOWER(baseImage) LIKE '%\lexplore.exe'
    OR LOWER(baseImage) LIKE '%\lexplorer.exe'
    OR LOWER(baseImage) LIKE '%\svchosts.exe'
    OR LOWER(baseImage) LIKE '%\csrsr.exe'
    OR LOWER(baseImage) LIKE '%\explore.exe'
    OR LOWER(baseImage) LIKE '%\iexplorer.exe')
MATCH-S00157.name: Windows Process Name Impersonation
MATCH-S00157.normalized_description: This alert detects a process executing with a name that closely resembles a default Windows process. Malware will often attempt to disguise its execution by using a similar name to blend in with standard processes.

MATCH-S00157.summary_expression: Detected Windows process name impersonation on host: {{device_hostname}}

MATCH-S00158.description_expression: Detects the creation of suspicious accounts similar to ANONYMOUS LOGON.  These accounts could be created as a covering detection vs network type 3 logons for shared resources, such as folders or printers.
MATCH-S00158.expression:
metadata_vendor = 'Microsoft'
AND metadata_product = 'Windows'
AND metadata_deviceEventId = 'Security-4720'
AND lower(changeTarget) like '%anonymous%logon%'
MATCH-S00158.name: Suspicious Windows ANONYMOUS LOGON Account Created
MATCH-S00158.name_expression: Suspicious Windows ANONYMOUS LOGON Account Created

MATCH-S00159.description_expression: Microsoft’s Net.exe can be used for multiple Discovery tactics, including Password Policy, Permissions, Account and Domain Trust Discovery. This detection identifies the use net.exe related commands on a system related to these discovery tactics. It is recommended to investigate the host and user to determine if this is authorized admin activity or needs further inspection.
MATCH-S00159.expression:
(
 lower(baseImage) like '%\net.exe' OR lower(baseImage) like '%\net1.exe'
)
 AND 
(
 lower(commandLine) like '%group%' OR lower(commandLine) like '%localgroup%'
 OR lower(commandLine) like '%accounts%' OR lower(commandLine) like '%use%' 
 OR lower(commandLine) like '%stop%'
 OR lower(commandLine) like '%domain%computers%' 
 OR lower(commandLine) like '%domain%admins%'
 OR lower(commandLine) like '%administrators%'
)
AND lower(commandLine) NOT LIKE '%sumologic%'
AND NOT array_contains(listMatches,'vuln_scanners')
AND user_username is not null
MATCH-S00159.name: Windows - Permissions Group Discovery
MATCH-S00159.name_expression: Detected Discovery using net.exe on host
MATCH-S00159.normalized_description: Microsoft’s Net.exe can be used for multiple Discovery tactics, including Password Policy, Permissions, Account and Domain Trust Discovery. This detection identifies the use net.exe related commands on a system related to these discovery tactics. It is recommended to investigate the host and user to determine if this is authorized admin activity or needs further inspection.
MATCH-S00159.normalized_signal_name: Detected Discovery using net.exe on host
MATCH-S00159.normalized_summary: Detected permission group discovery using net.exe on host: {{device_hostname}}
MATCH-S00159.summary_expression: Detected permission group discovery using net.exe on host: {{device_hostname}}

MATCH-S00161.description_expression: This rule detects commandlets from common PowerShell exploitation frameworks.
MATCH-S00161.expression:
(LOWER(baseImage) LIKE '%\powershell.exe' OR LOWER(baseImage) LIKE '%\powershell_ise.exe' OR LOWER(baseImage) LIKE '%\pwsh.exe')
AND (lower(commandLine) LIKE '%get-gpppassword%'
    OR lower(commandLine) LIKE '%get-keystrokes%'
    OR lower(commandLine) LIKE '%get-timedscreenshot%'
    OR lower(commandLine) LIKE '%get-vaultcredential%'
    OR lower(commandLine) LIKE '%get-serviceunquoted%'
    OR lower(commandLine) LIKE '%get-servicefilepermission%'
    OR lower(commandLine) LIKE '%get-servicepermission%' 
    OR lower(commandLine) LIKE '%get-regautologon%'
    OR lower(commandLine) LIKE '%get-vulnautorun%'
    OR lower(commandLine) LIKE '%get-vulnschtask%'
    OR lower(commandLine) LIKE '%get-unattendedinstallfile%'
    OR lower(commandLine) LIKE '%get-applicationhost%'
    OR lower(commandLine) LIKE '%get-regalwaysinstallelevated%'
    OR lower(commandLine) LIKE '%get-unconstrained%'
    OR lower(commandLine) LIKE '%get-lsasecret%'
    OR lower(commandLine) LIKE '%get-passhashes%'
    OR lower(commandLine) LIKE '%get-chromedump%'
    OR lower(commandLine) LIKE '%get-clipboardcontents%'
    OR lower(commandLine) LIKE '%get-foxdump%'
    OR lower(commandLine) LIKE '%get-indexeditem%'
    OR lower(commandLine) LIKE '%get-screenshot%'
    OR lower(commandLine) LIKE '%get-securitypackages%'
    OR lower(commandLine) LIKE '%get-sitelistpassword%'
    OR lower(lower(commandLine)) LIKE '%get-system%'
    OR lower(commandLine) LIKE '%get-rickastley%')
MATCH-S00161.name: Malicious PowerShell Get Commands
MATCH-S00161.normalized_description: This rule detects commandlets from common PowerShell exploitation frameworks.
MATCH-S00161.normalized_summary: Powershell executed exploitation commandlet observed on command line
MATCH-S00161.summary_expression: Powershell executed exploitation commandlet observed on command line

MATCH-S00162.description_expression: Detects capture of a network trace via the netsh.exe trace functionality
MATCH-S00162.expression:
(
 lower(commandLine) like '%netsh%' 
 AND lower(commandLine) like '%trace%' 
 AND lower(commandLine) like '%start%'
)
AND lower(parentBaseImage) like '%cmd.exe'
AND NOT array_contains(listMatches,'vuln_scanners')
and device_hostname is not null
MATCH-S00162.name: Windows - Network trace capture using netsh.exe

MATCH-S00164.description_expression: Web servers that spawn shell processes could be the result of a successfully placed web shell or an other attack
MATCH-S00164.expression:
(
  parentBaseImage like '%w3wp.exe' 
  OR parentBaseImage like '%httpd.exe' 
  OR parentBaseImage like '%nginx.exe' 
  OR parentBaseImage like '%php-cgi.exe'
  OR parentBaseImage like '%tomcat.exe' 
) 
AND (
  baseImage like '%cmd.exe' 
  OR baseImage like '%sh.exe' 
  OR baseImage like '%bash.exe' 
  OR baseImage like '%powershell.exe' 
  OR baseImage like '%bitsadmin.exe'
OR baseImage like '%pwsh.exe'
OR baseImage like '%powershell_ise.exe')
MATCH-S00164.name: Suspicious Shells Spawned by Web Servers
MATCH-S00164.name_expression: Suspicious Shells Spawned by Web Servers

MATCH-S00167.description_expression: Detects a set of commands often used in recon stages by different attack groups
MATCH-S00167.expression:
(
lower(commandLine) LIKE '%tasklist%'
 or lower(commandLine) LIKE '%systeminfo%'
 or lower(commandLine) LIKE '%nbtstat%'
 or lower(commandLine) LIKE '%qprocess%'
 or lower(commandLine) LIKE '%nslookup%'
 or lower(commandLine) LIKE '%hostname.exe%'
 or lower(commandLine) LIKE '%netstat -an%')
 and not array_contains(listMatches,'vuln_scanners')
 and device_hostname is not null
AND
(
   parentBaseImage rlike '\.(acm|ax|cpl|dll|drv|efi|exe|mui|ocx|scr|sys|tsp)$'
OR baseImage rlike '\.(acm|ax|cpl|dll|drv|efi|exe|mui|ocx|scr|sys|tsp)$'
OR file_path rlike '\.(acm|ax|cpl|dll|drv|efi|exe|mui|ocx|scr|sys|tsp)$'
)
AND NOT lower(parentBaseImage) LIKE '%\qualys\qualysagent\qualysagent.exe'
MATCH-S00167.name: Recon Using Common Windows Commands
MATCH-S00167.normalized_description: Detects a set of commands often used in recon stages by different attack groups

MATCH-S00168.description_expression: Local system account - Suspicious System Owner/User Discovery Activity - T1033 - requires commandline auditing 4688
MATCH-S00168.expression:
lower(baseImage) like '%whoami.exe'

AND lower(parentBaseImage) like '%cmd.exe'

MATCH-S00169.description_expression: This rule detects when a Microsoft Office Add-In is created by monitoring certain directories with specific file extensions. This rule requires the setup of file creation auditing.
MATCH-S00169.expression:
metadata_vendor = 'Microsoft'
AND metadata_product = 'Windows'
AND metadata_deviceEventId = 'Microsoft-Windows-Sysmon/Operational-11'
AND (
    file_path LIKE '%\Microsoft\Word\Startup\%'
    OR file_path LIKE '%\Microsoft\Excel\Startup\%'
    OR file_path LIKE '%\Microsoft\Addins\%'
)
AND (
    file_path LIKE '%.wll'
    OR file_path LIKE '%.xll'
    OR file_path LIKE '%.xlam'
    OR file_path LIKE '%.xla'
)

MATCH-S00170.description_expression: A scheduled task was created in Windows or Azure. It is common for system administrators and approved software to create scheduled tasks, but adversaries are known to use them for persistence within a Windows environment. This rule is disabled by default due to the volume of events it can produce. Users should filter/exclude allowed scheduled tasks according to their environment before enabling the rule. The scheduled task name is logged in the "commandLine" field.

It is common for system administrators and approved software to create scheduled tasks, but adversaries are known to use them for persistence within a Windows environment. The scheduled task name is logged in the "commandLine" field.

MATCH-S00171.description_expression: This rule detects the creation of a Windows scheduled task via PowerSploit or the default configuration of Empire.
MATCH-S00171.expression:
(
  lower(parentBaseImage) LIKE '%powershell.exe%'
  or lower(parentBaseImage) LIKE '%powershell_ise.exe%'
  or lower(parentBaseImage) LIKE '%pwsh.exe%'
)
AND (
  lower(commandLine) LIKE '%schtasks%/create%/sc %onlogon%/tn %updater%/tr %powershell%'
  OR lower(commandLine) LIKE '%schtasks%/create%/sc %daily%/tn %updater%/tr %powershell%'
  OR lower(commandLine) LIKE '%schtasks%/create%/sc %onidle%/tn %updater%/tr %powershell%'
  OR lower(commandLine) LIKE '%schtasks%/create%/sc %updater%/tn %updater%/tr %powershell%'
)

MATCH-S00174.description_expression: This rule looks for web server executables attempting to use commands commonly associated with adversaries utilizing a successfully uploaded web shell.
MATCH-S00174.expression:
(
  lower(parentBaseImage) LIKE '%w3wp.exe%'
  OR lower(parentBaseImage) LIKE '%httpd.exe%'
  OR lower(parentBaseImage) LIKE '%nginx.exe%'
  OR lower(parentBaseImage) LIKE '%php-cgi.exe%'
  OR lower(parentBaseImage) LIKE '%tomcat.exe%'

)

  OR lower(commandLine) LIKE '%arp -a%'

MATCH-S00176.description_expression: RDP login with a localhost source address may indicate a tunneled login and an attacker attempting to move through the environment.
MATCH-S00176.expression:
objectType = 'Authentication'
AND logonType IN ('RemoteInteractive','CachedRemoteInteractive')
AND (device_ip = '::1'
    OR device_ip = '127.0.0.1'
    OR srcDevice_ip = '::1'
    OR srcDevice_ip = '127.0.0.1')
MATCH-S00176.name: RDP Login from Localhost

MATCH-S00176.summary_expression: RDP connection from localhost on host: {{device_hostname}}

MATCH-S00178.description_expression: Adversaries may interact with the Windows Registry to gather information about the system, configuration, and installed software.
MATCH-S00178.expression:
lower(commandLine) like '%reg%'
AND 
(
 lower(commandLine) like '%currentversion\windows%'
 or lower(commandLine) like '%currentversion\runservicesonce%'
 or lower(commandLine) like '%currentversion\runservices%'
 or lower(commandLine) like '%winlogon\%'
 or lower(commandLine) like '%currentversion\shellserviceobjectdelayload%'
 or lower(commandLine) like '%currentversion\runonce%'
 or lower(commandLine) like '%currentversion\runonceex%'
 or lower(commandLine) like '%currentversion\run%'
 or lower(commandLine) like '%currentversion\policies\explorer\run%'
 or lower(commandLine) like '%currentcontrolset\services%')
and not array_contains(listMatches,'vuln_scanners')
and device_hostname is not null

MATCH-S00179.description_expression: Network sniffing refers to using the network interface on a system to monitor or capture information sent over a wired or wireless connection. MATCH-S00179.expression:
(
 lower(commandLine) LIKE '%tshark%'
 or lower(commandLine) LIKE '%windump%'
)
and not array_contains(listMatches,'vuln_scanners')
and device_hostname is not null

MATCH-S00189.description_expression: Detects remote logins by Administrative users. Administrative users are identified using your local naming convention. Because each environment controls their user naming convention, this rule's expression must first be tailored around your environment and enabled.  Adjust the section that reads:  "LIKE '%admin%'" to your environment's administrator naming convention.
MATCH-S00189.expression:
metadata_vendor = 'Microsoft'
and metadata_product = 'Windows'
and objectType = 'Authentication'
and logonType = 'RemoteInteractive'
and fields['EventData.AuthenticationPackageName'] = 'Negotiate'
and user_username LIKE '%admin%'
MATCH-S00189.name: Windows Admin User Remote Logon
MATCH-S00189.normalized_description: Detects remote logins by Administrative users. Administrative users are identified using your local naming convention. Because each environment controls their user naming convention, this rule's expression must first be tailored around your environment and enabled.  Adjust the section that reads:  "LIKE '%admin%'" to your environment's administrator naming convention.
MATCH-S00189.normalized_summary: Remote logon by administrative user: {{user_username}} on host: {{device_hostname}}
MATCH-S00189.summary_expression: Remote logon by administrative user: {{user_username}} on host: {{device_hostname}}

MATCH-S00190.description_expression: Detects Commandlet names from well-known PowerShell exploitation frameworks.
MATCH-S00190.expression:
(LOWER(baseImage) LIKE '%\powershell.exe' OR LOWER(baseImage) LIKE '%\powershell_ise.exe' OR LOWER(baseImage) LIKE '%\pwsh.exe')
AND (lower(commandLine) LIKE '%invoke-dllinjection%'
    OR lower(commandLine) LIKE '%invoke-shellcode%'
    OR lower(commandLine) LIKE '%invoke-wmicommand%'
    OR lower(commandLine) LIKE '%invoke-mimikatz%'
    OR lower(commandLine) LIKE '%invoke-ninjacopy%'
    OR lower(commandLine) LIKE '%invoke-tokenmanipulation%'
    OR lower(commandLine) LIKE '%invoke-reflectivepeinjection%' 
    OR lower(commandLine) LIKE '%invoke-userhunter%'
    OR lower(commandLine) LIKE '%invoke-aclscanner%'
    OR lower(commandLine) LIKE '%invoke-downgradeaccount%'
    OR lower(commandLine) LIKE '%invoke-serviceabuse%'
    OR lower(commandLine) LIKE '%invoke-adsbackdoor%'
    OR lower(commandLine) LIKE '%invoke-psuacme%'
    OR lower(commandLine) LIKE '%invoke-poshrathttp%'
    OR lower(commandLine) LIKE '%invoke-powershelltcp%'
    OR lower(commandLine) LIKE '%invoke-powershellwmi%'
    OR lower(commandLine) LIKE '%invoke-inveigh%'
    OR lower(commandLine) LIKE '%invoke-netripper%'
    OR lower(commandLine) LIKE '%invoke-egresscheck%'
    OR lower(commandLine) LIKE '%invoke-postexfil%'
    OR lower(commandLine) LIKE '%invoke-psinject%'
    OR lower(commandLine) LIKE '%invoke-runas%'
    OR lower(commandLine) LIKE '%invoke-dcsync%'
    OR lower(commandLine) LIKE '%invoke-powerdump%'
    OR lower(commandLine) LIKE '%invoke-thunderstruck%'
    OR lower(commandLine) LIKE '%invoke-voicetroll%'
    OR lower(commandLine) LIKE '%invoke-inveighrelay%'
    OR lower(commandLine) LIKE '%invoke-psexec%'
    OR lower(commandLine) LIKE '%invoke-sshcommand%'
    OR lower(commandLine) LIKE '%invoke-backdoorlnk%'
    OR lower(commandLine) LIKE '%invoke-bypassuac%'
    OR lower(commandLine) LIKE '%invoke-tater%'
    OR lower(commandLine) LIKE '%invoke-wscriptbypassuac%'
    OR lower(commandLine) LIKE '%invoke-paranoia%'
    OR lower(commandLine) LIKE '%invoke-winenum%'
    OR lower(commandLine) LIKE '%invoke-arpscan%'
    OR lower(commandLine) LIKE '%invoke-portscan%'
    OR lower(commandLine) LIKE '%invoke-reversednslookup%'
    OR lower(commandLine) LIKE '%invoke-smbscanner%'
    OR lower(commandLine) LIKE '%invoke-mimikittenz%'
    OR lower(commandLine) LIKE '%invoke-allchecks%')

MATCH-S00190.normalized_description: Detects Commandlet names from well-known PowerShell exploitation frameworks.

MATCH-S00190.summary_expression: Powershell executed exploitation commandlet on host: {{device_hostname}}

MATCH-S00191.description_expression: Detects keywords that could indicate the use of a PowerShell exploitation framework.
MATCH-S00191.expression:
(LOWER(baseImage) LIKE '%\powershell.exe' OR LOWER(baseImage) LIKE '%\powershell_ise.exe' OR LOWER(baseImage) LIKE '%\pwsh.exe')
AND (lower(commandLine) LIKE '%system.reflection.assembly.load%'
    OR lower(commandLine) LIKE '%[system.reflection.assembly]::load%'
    OR lower(commandLine) LIKE '%[reflection.assembly]::load%'
    OR lower(commandLine) LIKE '%system.reflection.assemblyname%'
    OR lower(commandLine) LIKE '%reflection.emit.assemblybuilderaccess%'
    OR lower(commandLine) LIKE '%runtime.interopservices.dllimportattribute%'
    OR lower(commandLine) LIKE '%suspendthread%')
MATCH-S00191.name: Suspicious PowerShell Keywords
MATCH-S00191.normalized_description: Detects keywords that could indicate the use of a PowerShell exploitation framework.
MATCH-S00191.normalized_summary: Powershell exploitation framework keywords detected on host: {{device_hostname}}
MATCH-S00191.summary_expression: Powershell exploitation framework keywords detected on host: {{device_hostname}}

MATCH-S00192.description_expression: Suspicious System Network Configuration Discovery Activity - T1016
MATCH-S00192.expression:
lower(commandLine) RLIKE '^ipconfig\s+'
AND NOT array_contains(listMatches,'vuln_scanners')
and device_hostname is not null
AND NOT lower(parentBaseImage) LIKE '%\qualys\qualysagent\qualysagent.exe'
MATCH-S00192.name: Windows - System Network Configuration Discovery
MATCH-S00192.normalized_description: Suspicious System Network Configuration Discovery Activity - T1016
MATCH-S00192.normalized_summary: Detected System Network Configuration using IPCONFIG on host: {{device_hostname}}
MATCH-S00192.summary_expression: Detected System Network Configuration using IPCONFIG on host: {{device_hostname}}

MATCH-S00194.description_expression: Identifies use of various commands to query a system's time. This technique may be used before executing a scheduled task or to discover the time zone of a target system.
MATCH-S00194.expression:
lower(commandLine) = 'powershell get-date'
OR
(    
    (
    lower(baseImage) LIKE '%net.exe' 
    OR lower(baseImage) LIKE '%net1.exe'
    )

AND 
    (
    lower(commandLine) LIKE '%w32time%'
    OR lower(commandLine) LIKE '%w32tm%'
    OR lower(commandLine) LIKE '%net time%'
    )
)
AND NOT array_contains(listMatches,'vuln_scanners')
MATCH-S00194.name: Windows - System Time Discovery
MATCH-S00194.normalized_description: Identifies use of various commands to query a system's time. This technique may be used before executing a scheduled task or to discover the time zone of a target system.
MATCH-S00194.normalized_summary: Detected system time discovery on host: {{device_hostname}}
MATCH-S00194.summary_expression: Detected system time discovery on host: {{device_hostname}}

MATCH-S00196.description_expression: Identifies a suspicious windows logon of type 9 (NewCredentials).  This signal is suspicious due to its similarity to the behavior observed when using Mimikatz's sekurlsa::pth tool.
MATCH-S00196.expression:
objectType = 'Authentication'
and logonType = 'NewCredentials' 
and application = 'seclogo'
and (
   fields['EventData.AuthenticationPackageName'] = 'Negotiate'
OR fields['EventData.AuthenticationPackage'] = 'Negotiate'
)
MATCH-S00196.name: Successful Overpass the Hash Attempt
MATCH-S00196.normalized_description: Identifies a suspicious windows logon of type 9 (NewCredentials).  This signal is suspicious due to its similarity to the behavior observed when using Mimikatz's sekurlsa::pth tool.
MATCH-S00196.normalized_summary: Successful Overpass the Hash attempted on host: {{device_hostname}}
MATCH-S00196.summary_expression: Successful Overpass the Hash attempted on host: {{device_hostname}}

MATCH-S00198.description_expression: This rule detects well known keywords from PowerShell exploitation frameworks.
MATCH-S00198.expression:
(LOWER(baseImage) LIKE '%\powershell.exe' OR LOWER(baseImage) LIKE '%\powershell_ise.exe' OR LOWER(baseImage) LIKE '%\pwsh.exe')
AND (LOWER(commandLine) LIKE '%adjusttokenprivileges%'
    OR LOWER(commandLine) LIKE '%image_nt_optional_hdr64_magic%'
    OR LOWER(commandLine) LIKE '%microsoft.win32.unsafenativemethods%'
    OR LOWER(commandLine) LIKE '%readprocessmemory.invoke%'
    OR LOWER(commandLine) LIKE '%se_privilege_enabled%'
    OR LOWER(commandLine) LIKE '%lsa_unicode_string%'
    OR LOWER(commandLine) LIKE '%minidumpwritedump%'
    OR LOWER(commandLine) LIKE '%page_execute_read%'
    OR LOWER(commandLine) LIKE '%security_delegation%'
    OR LOWER(commandLine) LIKE '%token_adjust_privileges%'
    OR LOWER(commandLine) LIKE '%token_all_access%'
    OR LOWER(commandLine) LIKE '%token_assign_primary%'
    OR LOWER(commandLine) LIKE '%token_duplicate%'
    OR LOWER(commandLine) LIKE '%token_elevation%'
    OR LOWER(commandLine) LIKE '%token_impersonate%'
    OR LOWER(commandLine) LIKE '%token_information_class%'
    OR LOWER(commandLine) LIKE '%token_privileges%'
    OR LOWER(commandLine) LIKE '%token_query%'
    OR LOWER(commandLine) LIKE '%metasploit%'
    OR LOWER(commandLine) LIKE '%mimikatz%')
MATCH-S00198.name: Malicious PowerShell Keywords
MATCH-S00198.normalized_description: This rule detects well known keywords from PowerShell exploitation frameworks.
MATCH-S00198.normalized_summary: Powershell exploitation keywords observed on host: {{device_hostname}}
MATCH-S00198.summary_expression: Powershell exploitation keywords observed on host: {{device_hostname}}

MATCH-S00200.description_expression: The behavior discovered here loosely matches the behavior of known pass the hash tools.  A Pass the Hash (PtH) attack is a way for an attacker to move laterally through a type of credential theft.  Because this behavior is known to occur in some environments during normal activity, tuning is recommended and attention paid to a possible spike in signals after enabling this rule.
MATCH-S00200.expression:
metadata_vendor = 'Microsoft' AND
metadata_product = 'Windows' AND
objectType = 'Authentication' AND
((user_userId = 'S-1-0-0' 
        AND logonType = 'Network'
        AND application = 'NtLmSsp' 
        AND fields['EventData.KeyLength'] = '0') 
    OR (logonType = 'NewCredentials' AND application = 'seclogo'))
and user_username != 'anonymous logon'
MATCH-S00200.name: Potential Pass the Hash Activity
MATCH-S00200.normalized_description: The behavior discovered here loosely matches the behavior of known pass the hash tools.  A Pass the Hash (PtH) attack is a way for an attacker to move laterally through a type of credential theft.  Because this behavior is known to occur in some environments during normal activity, tuning is recommended and attention paid to a possible spike in signals after enabling this rule.
MATCH-S00200.normalized_summary: Potential pass the hash activity detected on host: {{device_hostname}}
MATCH-S00200.summary_expression: Potential pass the hash activity detected on host: {{device_hostname}}

MATCH-S00202.description_expression: CVE-2020-1472 can be exploited by attackers to hijack enterprise servers due to Netlogon cryptographic weaknesses. The vulnerability allows an attacker to set a password for the computer account of an Active Directory Domain Controller, which can then be abused to pull credentials from the Domain Controller. This rule detects an incoming network connection made from the attacking machine to the victim Domain Controller to the LSASS process.
MATCH-S00202.expression:
metadata_vendor = 'Microsoft'
AND metadata_product = 'Windows'
AND metadata_deviceEventId = 'Microsoft-Windows-Sysmon/Operational-3'
AND LOWER(file_path) like '%\lsass.exe'
AND (fields['insertionstrings_f08'] = 'false' OR fields['EventData.Initiated']= 'false')
MATCH-S00202.name: Windows - Incoming LSASS Network Connection - Zerologon Behavior(CVE-2020-1472)
MATCH-S00202.name_expression: Incoming LSASS Network Connection - Zerologon Behavior(CVE-2020-1472)
MATCH-S00202.normalized_description: CVE-2020-1472 can be exploited by attackers to hijack enterprise servers due to Netlogon cryptographic weaknesses. The vulnerability allows an attacker to set a password for the computer account of an Active Directory Domain Controller, which can then be abused to pull credentials from the Domain Controller. This rule detects an incoming network connection made from the attacking machine to the victim Domain Controller to the LSASS process.
MATCH-S00202.normalized_signal_name: Incoming LSASS Network Connection - Zerologon Behavior(CVE-2020-1472)

MATCH-S00203.description_expression: Detects a possible exploitation of CVE-2020-1350 (aka SIGRED) using rare HTTP requests over port 53. HTTP should rarely (if ever) hosted on port 53. Technique:  T1068.  Derived from SOC Prime logic.
MATCH-S00203.expression: not http_method is null and dstPort = 53
MATCH-S00203.name: HTTP activity over port 53 - Possible SIGRED
MATCH-S00203.name_expression: HTTP activity over port 53 - Possible SIGRED
MATCH-S00203.normalized_description: Detects a possible exploitation of CVE-2020-1350 (aka SIGRED) using rare HTTP requests over port 53. HTTP should rarely (if ever) hosted on port 53. Technique:  T1068.  Derived from SOC Prime logic.
MATCH-S00203.normalized_signal_name: HTTP activity over port 53 - Possible SIGRED
MATCH-S00203.normalized_summary: Possible SIGRED HTTP request to port 53 from IP: {{srcDevice_ip}}
MATCH-S00203.summary_expression: Possible SIGRED HTTP request to port 53 from IP: {{srcDevice_ip}}

MATCH-S00208.description_expression: Actions observed that create, import and delete access keys to EC2 could indicate an advisary is taking action on their objective to extend or otherwise manipulate access to EC2 instance(s).
MATCH-S00208.expression:
metadata_vendor = 'Amazon AWS'
AND metadata_product = 'CloudTrail'
AND application = 'ec2.amazonaws.com'
AND action in ('DeleteKeyPair', 'ImportKeyPair', 'CreateKeyPair')
MATCH-S00208.name: AWS CloudTrail - EC2 Access Key Action Detected
MATCH-S00208.name_expression: AWS CloudTrail - EC2 Access Key Action Detected {{action}}

MATCH-S00208.normalized_summary: {{action}} performed by user: {{user_username}} with role: {{user_username_role}} on target: {{changeTarget}}
MATCH-S00208.summary_expression: {{action}} performed by user: {{user_username}} with role: {{user_username_role}} on target: {{changeTarget}}

MATCH-S00209.description_expression: Apache Log4j2 <=2.14.1 using JNDI features has an exploit allowing attackers to perform unauthenticated, remote code execution on a Java application that logs user input. This CSE rule looks for attackers attempting to input the string "jndi:" into a field that the application may log to trigger the payload download and execution. This does NOT indicate the application is vulnerable and downloaded the malicious payload, but it does show a hostile actor attempting the attack method.
MATCH-S00209.name: CVE-2021-44228 Log4j2 Java Library 0-Day Attempt
MATCH-S00209.normalized_description: Apache Log4j2 <=2.14.1 using JNDI features has an exploit allowing attackers to perform unauthenticated, remote code execution on a Java application that logs user input. This CSE rule looks for attackers attempting to input the string "jndi:" into a field that the application may log to trigger the payload download and execution. This does NOT indicate the application is vulnerable and downloaded the malicious payload, but it does show a hostile actor attempting the attack method.
MATCH-S00209.normalized_summary: The string "jndi:" was detected inside a CSE field that could be used to attempt CVE-2021-44228.
MATCH-S00209.summary_expression: The string "jndi:" was detected inside a CSE field that could be used to attempt CVE-2021-44228.

MATCH-S00210.description_expression: This event sourced from AWS SQS occurs rarely.  It could indicate that an adversary is attempting to collect information for later attack.  When successful, the List Queues event returns all SQS queues that may be valid targets for further probing/attack.
MATCH-S00210.expression:
metadata_vendor = 'Amazon AWS'
AND metadata_product = 'CloudTrail'
AND application = 'sqs.amazonaws.com'
AND action = 'ListQueues'
MATCH-S00210.name: AWS CloudTrail - SQS List Queues Event
MATCH-S00210.name_expression: AWS CloudTrail - SQS List Queues Event
MATCH-S00210.normalized_description: This event sourced from AWS SQS occurs rarely.  It could indicate that an adversary is attempting to collect information for later attack.  When successful, the List Queues event returns all SQS queues that may be valid targets for further probing/attack.
MATCH-S00210.normalized_signal_name: AWS CloudTrail - SQS List Queues Event

MATCH-S00211.description_expression: With very few exceptions, the DNS.EXE program should not spawn other processes.  This could be an indication that the process is a trojan, or has been compromised.  This behavior against DNS has been shown as a behavioral indicator after successful attacks (i.e. SigRED).
MATCH-S00211.expression:
lower(parentBaseImage) like '%dns.exe' 
and not baseImage is null
MATCH-S00211.name: DNS.EXE Observed as Parent Process
MATCH-S00211.normalized_description: With very few exceptions, the DNS.EXE program should not spawn other processes.  This could be an indication that the process is a trojan, or has been compromised.  This behavior against DNS has been shown as a behavioral indicator after successful attacks (i.e. SigRED).

MATCH-S00213.commit_message: TRIDENT-18592 AWS CloudTrail - Reconnaissance related event dynamic name causing insights
MATCH-S00213.description_expression: This signal identifies a small number of CloudTrail API actions that when observed could indicate an actors intent to enumerate the environment.  These events are generally benign, and occur during normal operations.  Use this signal as context around an unfolding security story.
MATCH-S00213.expression:
metadata_deviceEventId IN (
    'AwsApiCall-GetAccessKeyLastUsed',
    'AwsApiCall-GetAccountAuthorizationDetails',
    'AwsApiCall-GetAccountPasswordPolicy',
    'AwsApiCall-GetGroupPolicy',
    'AwsApiCall-GetUserPolicy',
    'AwsApiCall-ListAccessKeys',
    'AwsApiCall-ListGroupsForUser',
    'AwsApiCall-ListPolicies'
)
and NOT array_contains(listMatches, 'AWS_admin_ips')
and NOT array_contains(listMatches, 'AWS_admin_users')
MATCH-S00213.name: AWS CloudTrail - Reconnaissance related event
MATCH-S00213.normalized_description: This signal identifies a small number of CloudTrail API actions that when observed could indicate an actors intent to enumerate the environment.  These events are generally benign, and occur during normal operations.  Use this signal as context around an unfolding security story.

MATCH-S00214.description_expression: Attackers may create scheduled tasks to execute commands in various scenarios. Inclusion of commonly abused or high risk Windows executables may be an indication of an attack.
MATCH-S00214.expression:
metadata_vendor = 'Microsoft'
AND metadata_product = 'Windows'
AND metadata_deviceEventId = 'Security-4698'
AND (
	lower(fields['EventData.TaskContent']) rlike '(?:[^<]+)?\b(?:at|bitsadmin|certutil|cmd|cmstp|cscript|ftp|hh|ieexec|installutil|mavinject|msbuild|mshta|msiexec|odbcconf|pcalua|powershell|regasm|regsvcs|regsvr32|rundll32|wmic|wscript)\.exe'
    OR 
    lower(fields['insertionstrings_f06']) rlike '(?:[^<]+)?\b(?:at|bitsadmin|certutil|cmd|cmstp|cscript|ftp|hh|ieexec|installutil|mavinject|msbuild|mshta|msiexec|odbcconf|pcalua|powershell|regasm|regsvcs|regsvr32|rundll32|wmic|wscript)\.exe'
)
MATCH-S00214.name: Scheduled Task Creation with Suspicious Task Executable
MATCH-S00214.name_expression: Scheduled Task Creation with Suspicious Task Executable
MATCH-S00214.normalized_description: Attackers may create scheduled tasks to execute commands in various scenarios. Inclusion of commonly abused or high risk Windows executables may be an indication of an attack.
MATCH-S00214.normalized_signal_name: Scheduled Task Creation with Suspicious Task Executable
MATCH-S00214.normalized_summary: Scheduled task created on host: {{device_hostname}} with task content: {{fields['EventData.TaskContent']}}
MATCH-S00214.summary_expression: Scheduled task created on host: {{device_hostname}} with task content: {{fields['EventData.TaskContent']}}

MATCH-S00215.description_expression: Passthrough rule for high risk events generated by Auth0

MATCH-S00215.name: Auth0 - High Risk Event

MATCH-S00220.commit_message: TLAB:2307- Updated Mapping for Azure Custom Mapper (#5878)
MATCH-S00220.description_expression:
Privileged Identity Management (PIM) allows administrators to provide users privileged access with greater oversight of activties undertaken  while said access is granted as well as control over the duration of access.

Adding a user to a role, especially one with administrative privileges, outside of PIM may indicate a threat actor attempting to persist privileged access.
MATCH-S00220.expression:
metadata_vendor = 'Microsoft'
AND metadata_product = 'Azure'
AND metadata_deviceEventId = 'AuditLogs'
AND changeType = 'Add member to role'
AND fields['identity'] != 'MS-PIM'
MATCH-S00220.name: Azure - Add Member to Role Outside of PIM
MATCH-S00220.normalized_description:
Privileged Identity Management (PIM) allows administrators to provide users privileged access with greater oversight of activties undertaken  while said access is granted as well as control over the duration of access.

Adding a user to a role, especially one with administrative privileges, outside of PIM may indicate a threat actor attempting to persist privileged access.

OR lower(fields['cat']) LIKE '%malicious%')
MATCH-S00222.name: ZScaler Proxy-Traffic to Malicious Categorized Domain

MATCH-S00222.summary_expression: ZScaler Detected Traffic to Malicious Categorized Domain from {{srcDevice_ip}}

MATCH-S00227.commit_message: Add alias for Google Workspace to (hopefully) support G Suite as a valid Product name (#5890)
MATCH-S00227.description_expression: Alert when a non-approved OAuth Application has been identified on Google Workspace. This rule is disabled by default as a list of approved OAuth applications is required to be enabled. The approved applications should be added to the rule logic under the 'application not in' condition.
MATCH-S00227.expression:
metadata_vendor = 'Google'
AND metadata_product = 'Google Workspace'

AND (
    metadata_deviceEventId like 'token-%-activity'
    OR
    metadata_deviceEventId like 'token.%.authorize' OR metadata_deviceEventId = 'token.authorize' OR metadata_deviceEventId = 'token.activity'
)
    
AND application not in (
    'AquaMail',
    'Chorus',
    'Concur Calendar',
    'Credentials backport to SF',
    'Culture Amp',
    'Google Chrome',
    'Gem',
    'G-Connector for Salesforce',
    'Google Analytics',
    'iOS Account Manager',
    'Lucidchart',
    'macOS',
    'MongoDB: Internal Tools',
    'Recorded Macros (PG Workstream Template)',
    'Slack',
    'Trello for Gmail',
    'Zoom',
    'Zoom for Google Workspace',
    'Zoom for G Suite')
MATCH-S00227.name: Google Workspace - Unauthorized OAuth Application
MATCH-S00227.normalized_description: Alert when a non-approved OAuth Application has been identified on Google Workspace. This rule is disabled by default as a list of approved OAuth applications is required to be enabled. The approved applications should be added to the rule logic under the 'application not in' condition.

MATCH-S00233.expression:

MATCH-S00238.description_expression: AWS KMS is an encryption and key management web service.  Besides encrypting and decrypting data, users and administrators can use this service to create keys, manage keys etc.  This signal indicates activity that enables and disables keys explicitly.  This activity has been surveyed to be a low volume event and could be considered suspicious given other activity involving the entity.  Additionally, monitoring for these events is required to achieve certain industry audit compliance.
MATCH-S00238.expression:
metadata_vendor = 'Amazon AWS'
AND metadata_product = 'CloudTrail'
AND metadata_deviceEventId IN ('AwsApiCall-EnableKey', 
                           'AwsApiCall-DisableKey')
and NOT array_contains(listMatches, 'AWS_admin_ips')
and NOT array_contains(listMatches, 'AWS_admin_users')
MATCH-S00238.name: AWS CloudTrail - sensitive activity in KMS
MATCH-S00238.name_expression: AWS CloudTrail - Sensitive activity in KMS {{metadata_deviceEventId}}
MATCH-S00238.normalized_description: AWS KMS is an encryption and key management web service.  Besides encrypting and decrypting data, users and administrators can use this service to create keys, manage keys etc.  This signal indicates activity that enables and disables keys explicitly.  This activity has been surveyed to be a low volume event and could be considered suspicious given other activity involving the entity.  Additionally, monitoring for these events is required to achieve certain industry audit compliance.
MATCH-S00238.normalized_signal_name: AWS CloudTrail - Sensitive activity in KMS {{metadata_deviceEventId}}

MATCH-S00240.description_expression: Deleting cryptographic key material managed by KMS can be risky. The risk is that after key material is deleted, cypher text may remain that is now indecipherable. Because of this risk, AWS enforces a minimum 7 day waiting period. A key cannot be deleted, it must first be scheduled for deletion by the system. This signal indicates that a key has been scheduled or canceled for deletion. This signal in context of other signals around this entity may describe a hostile pattern of attack.
MATCH-S00240.expression:
metadata_vendor = 'Amazon AWS'
AND metadata_product = 'CloudTrail'
AND metadata_deviceEventId  IN ('AwsApiCall-ScheduleKeyDeletion', 'AwsApiCall-CancelKeyDeletion')
MATCH-S00240.name: AWS CloudTrail - ScheduleKeyDeletion in KMS
MATCH-S00240.normalized_description: Deleting cryptographic key material managed by KMS can be risky. The risk is that after key material is deleted, cypher text may remain that is now indecipherable. Because of this risk, AWS enforces a minimum 7 day waiting period. A key cannot be deleted, it must first be scheduled for deletion by the system. This signal indicates that a key has been scheduled or canceled for deletion. This signal in context of other signals around this entity may describe a hostile pattern of attack.

MATCH-S00246.description_expression: The secrets manager service is commonly used by cloud components to retrieve secrets (connection strings etc) while performing routine functions. and device_ip is not null

and NOT array_contains(listMatches, 'AWS_admin_ips')
AND NOT srcDevice_ip_location LIKE 'AWS%'

MATCH-S00247.description_expression: Administrative changes to the AWS Secrets Manager aren't overtly hostile, but are generally low volume and can be considered sensitive.  These signals highlight when these actions occur and can be used in context of other suspicious activity to raise the risk of a hostile entity.  Several Secrets Manager API actions are included and assessed as sensitive.
MATCH-S00247.expression:
metadata_vendor = 'Amazon AWS'
AND metadata_product = 'CloudTrail'
AND metadata_deviceEventId IN
(
    'AwsApiCall-UpdateSecret',
    'AwsApiCall-UpdateSecretVersionStage',
    'AwsApiCall-RestoreSecret',
    'AwsApiCall-PutResourcePolicy',
    'AwsApiCall-DeleteSecret'
)
MATCH-S00247.name: AWS CloudTrail - Secrets Manager sensitive admin action observed
MATCH-S00247.name_expression: AWS CloudTrail - Secrets Manager sensitive admin action observed
MATCH-S00247.normalized_description: Administrative changes to the AWS Secrets Manager aren't overtly hostile, but are generally low volume and can be considered sensitive.  These signals highlight when these actions occur and can be used in context of other suspicious activity to raise the risk of a hostile entity.  Several Secrets Manager API actions are included and assessed as sensitive.
MATCH-S00247.normalized_signal_name: AWS CloudTrail - Secrets Manager sensitive admin action observed

MATCH-S00276.description_expression: The Squiblydoo technique is a way for unapproved scripts to run on a machine that is setup to allow only approved scripts to run. Squiblydoo utilizes regsvr32.exe to download an XML file that contains scriptlets for executing code on the victim machine.
MATCH-S00276.expression:
((lower(baseImage) like '%regsvr32.exe%'
AND lower(commandLine) like '%scrobj.dll%')
OR (lower(commandLine) like '%scrobj.dll%'
AND lower(commandLine) like '%regsvr32%'))
MATCH-S00276.name: Windows - Possible Squiblydoo Technique Observed

MATCH-S00276.summary_expression: Detected possible Squiblydoo activity on host: {{device_hostname}}

(commandLine like '%MyStart' AND

MATCH-S00288.description_expression: Detects NotPetya ransomware activity by identifying one of these occurring: the extracted passwords are passed back to the main module via named pipe, the file system journal of drive C is deleted or windows eventlogs are cleared using wevtutil.
MATCH-S00288.expression:
commandLine like '%\AppData\Local\Temp\\% \\.\pipe\\%' 
OR
(baseImage like '%\rundll32.exe' AND
 commandLine like '%.dat,#1')
OR
commandLine like '%\perfc.dat%'
MATCH-S00288.name: NotPetya Ransomware Activity
MATCH-S00288.normalized_description: Detects NotPetya ransomware activity by identifying one of these occurring: the extracted passwords are passed back to the main module via named pipe, the file system journal of drive C is deleted or windows eventlogs are cleared using wevtutil.

MATCH-S00288.summary_expression: NotPetya ransomware activity detected on host: {{device_hostname}}

 parentBaseImage like '%\services.exe')

MATCH-S00301.description_expression: Alert when an OAuth application has requested a high number of permissions to aspects of Google Workspace.
MATCH-S00301.expression:
metadata_vendor = 'Google'
AND metadata_product = 'Google Workspace'
AND (
    metadata_deviceEventId like 'token-%-activity'
    OR metadata_deviceEventId like 'token.%.authorize' 
    OR metadata_deviceEventId = 'token.authorize' 
    OR metadata_deviceEventId = 'token.activity'
)
AND fields['event.parameters.scope.10'] IS NOT NULL 
AND NOT application like '%CloudLock for Google Drive%'
AND NOT application like '%Google Workspace Sync for Microsoft Outlook%'
AND NOT application like '%Google Cloud Shell%'

MATCH-S00307.description_expression: Alert when an OAuth application has requested a high number of permissions to aspects of AWS.
MATCH-S00307.expression:
metadata_vendor = 'Amazon AWS'
AND metadata_product = 'CloudTrail'
AND metadata_deviceEventId IN (
    'AwsApiCall-CreateUserPoolClient',
    'AwsApiCall-UpdateUserPoolClient'
)
AND (
    fields['requestParameters.readAttributes.10'] IS NOT NULL
    OR fields['requestParameters.writeAttributes.10'] IS NOT NULL
)
MATCH-S00307.name: AWS - Excessive OAuth Application Permissions Scope
MATCH-S00307.name_expression: AWS - Excessive OAuth Application Permissions Scope - "{{application}}"
MATCH-S00307.normalized_description: Alert when an OAuth application has requested a high number of permissions to aspects of AWS.

MATCH-S00308.description_expression: This event sourced from AWS OpsWorks occurs rarely.  It could indicate that an adversary is attempting to collect information for later attack.  When successful, the Describe Permissions event returns information regarding a specified stack's permissions for access.
MATCH-S00308.expression:
metadata_vendor = 'Amazon AWS'
AND metadata_product = 'CloudTrail'
AND application = 'opsworks.amazonaws.com'
AND action = 'DescribePermissions'
MATCH-S00308.name: AWS CloudTrail - OpsWorks Describe Permissions Event
MATCH-S00308.name_expression: AWS CloudTrail - OpsWorks Describe Permissions Event
MATCH-S00308.normalized_description: This event sourced from AWS OpsWorks occurs rarely.  It could indicate that an adversary is attempting to collect information for later attack.  When successful, the Describe Permissions event returns information regarding a specified stack's permissions for access.
MATCH-S00308.normalized_signal_name: AWS CloudTrail - OpsWorks Describe Permissions Event

MATCH-S00310.description_expression: Detects potential CMSTP.exe abuse. Adversaries use CMSTP.exe to load and execute DLLs or COM scriptlets from remote servers to bypass application control defenses.
MATCH-S00310.expression:
metadata_deviceEventId = 'Microsoft-Windows-Sysmon/Operational-3'
AND lower(file_path) like '%cmstp.exe'
AND dstDevice_ip_isInternal = false
MATCH-S00310.name: Windows - Network Connection from CMSTP
MATCH-S00310.normalized_description: Detects potential CMSTP.exe abuse. Adversaries use CMSTP.exe to load and execute DLLs or COM scriptlets from remote servers to bypass application control defenses.
MATCH-S00310.normalized_summary: Network connected from CMSTP on host: {{device_hostname}}
MATCH-S00310.summary_expression: Network connected from CMSTP on host: {{device_hostname}}

MATCH-S00313.description_expression: Detects a removable media device attached to a host. External media can be used to exfiltrate sensitive data and is also a common source of infections, so some organizations block their use. Attempts to use these devices could indicate the intent for malicious activity. Customer should populate a list of devices that should not have external media installed on them.
MATCH-S00313.expression:
metadata_vendor = 'Microsoft'
AND metadata_product = 'Windows'
AND metadata_deviceEventId = 'Security-6416'
AND array_contains(listMatches, 'unauthorized_external_media')
MATCH-S00313.name: Unauthorized External Device Installation
MATCH-S00313.normalized_description: Detects a removable media device attached to a host. External media can be used to exfiltrate sensitive data and is also a common source of infections, so some organizations block their use. Attempts to use these devices could indicate the intent for malicious activity. Customer should populate a list of devices that should not have external media installed on them.
MATCH-S00313.normalized_summary: Unauthorized external device installation on host: {{device_hostname}}
MATCH-S00313.summary_expression: Unauthorized external device installation on host: {{device_hostname}}

MATCH-S00316.description_expression: Uses data from process creation events to detect indicators of the WannaCry Ransomware.
MATCH-S00316.expression:
lower(baseImage) like '%\tasksche.exe' OR
lower(baseImage) like '%\mssecsvc.exe' OR
lower(baseImage) like '%\taskdl.exe' OR
lower(baseImage) like '%\@wanadecryptor@%' OR
lower(baseImage) like '%\wanadecryptor%' OR
lower(baseImage) like '%\taskhsvc.exe' OR
lower(baseImage) like '%\taskse.exe' OR
baseImage like '%\111.exe' OR
lower(baseImage) like '%\lhdfrgui.exe' OR
lower(baseImage) like '%\linuxnew.exe' OR
lower(baseImage) like '%\wannacry.exe' OR
commandLine like '%icacls % /grant Everyone:F /T /C /Q%' OR
lower(commandLine) like '%bcdedit /set {default} recoveryenabled no%' OR
commandLine like '%@Please_Read_Me@.txt%'
MATCH-S00316.name: WannaCry Ransomware
MATCH-S00316.normalized_description: Uses data from process creation events to detect indicators of the WannaCry Ransomware.
MATCH-S00316.normalized_summary: WannaCry ransomware indicator detected on host: {{device_hostname}}
MATCH-S00316.summary_expression: WannaCry ransomware indicator detected on host: {{device_hostname}}
