MATCH-S00319.normalized_summary: A suspicious command execution was observed on host: {{device_hostname}}
MATCH-S00319.summary_expression: A suspicious command execution was observed on host: {{device_hostname}}

MATCH-S00322.description_expression: Detects Russian group activity as described in Global Threat Report 2019 by Crowdstrike
MATCH-S00322.expression:
(
    (
        lower(baseImage) like '%\xcopy.exe' AND
        lower(commandLine) like '% /s /e /c /q /h *%'
    )
    OR
    (
        lower(baseImage) like '%\adexplorer.exe' AND
        lower(commandLine) like '% -snapshot "" c:\users*%'
    )
)
MATCH-S00322.name: Judgement Panda Credential Access Activity
MATCH-S00322.normalized_description: Detects Russian group activity as described in Global Threat Report 2019 by Crowdstrike
MATCH-S00322.normalized_summary: Judgement Panda credential access activity detected from process: {{baseImage}} on host: {{device_hostname}}
MATCH-S00322.summary_expression: Judgement Panda credential access activity detected from process: {{baseImage}} on host: {{device_hostname}}

MATCH-S00325.description_expression: Detects tools and process executions as observed in a Greenbug campaign in May 2020
MATCH-S00325.expression:
(
    lower(commandLine) like '%bitsadmin /transfer%' AND
    lower(commandLine) like '%csidl_appdata%'
)
OR
(
    lower(commandLine) like '%csidl_appdata%'
)
OR
(
        lower(commandLine) like '%\msf.ps1%' OR
        lower(commandLine) like '%8989 -e cmd.exe%' OR
        lower(commandLine) like '%system.data.sqlclient.sqldataadapter($cmd); [void]$da.fill%' OR
        lower(commandLine) like '%-nop -w hidden -c $k=new-object%' OR
        lower(commandLine) like '%[net.credentialcache]::defaultcredentials;iex %' OR
        lower(commandLine) like '% -nop -w hidden -c $m=new-object net.webclient;$m%' OR
        lower(commandLine) like '%-noninteractive -executionpolicy bypass whoami%' OR
        lower(commandLine) like '%-noninteractive -executionpolicy bypass netstat -a%' OR
        commandLine like '%L3NlcnZlc%'

)
OR
(
        lower(baseImage) like '%\adobe\adobe.exe' OR
        lower(baseImage) like '%\oracle\local.exe' OR
        lower(baseImage) like '%\revshell.exe' OR
        lower(baseImage) like '%infopagesbackup\ncat.exe' OR
        lower(baseImage) like '%csidl_system\cmd.exe' OR
        lower(baseImage) like '%\programdata\oracle\java.exe' OR
        lower(baseImage) like '%csidl_common_appdata\comms\comms.exe' OR
        lower(baseImage) like '%\programdata\vmware\vmware.exe'
)
MATCH-S00325.name: Greenbug Campaign Indicators
MATCH-S00325.normalized_description: Detects tools and process executions as observed in a Greenbug campaign in May 2020
MATCH-S00325.normalized_summary: Observed suspicious command execution on host: {{device_hostname}}
MATCH-S00325.summary_expression: Observed suspicious command execution on host: {{device_hostname}}

MATCH-S00328.summary_expression: Rubeus hack tool indicator detected on host: {{device_hostname}}

MATCH-S00331.summary_expression: Process injection indicator in command on host: {{device_hostname}}

MATCH-S00334.description_expression: Detects Judgement Panda activity as described in Global Threat Report 2019 by Crowdstrike
MATCH-S00334.expression:
(
    lower(commandLine) like '%\ldifde.exe -f -n %' OR
    lower(commandLine) like '%\7za.exe a 1.7z %' OR
    lower(commandLine) like '% eprod.ldf' OR
    lower(commandLine) like '%\aaaa\procdump64.exe%' OR
    lower(commandLine) like '%\aaaa\netsess.exe%' OR
    lower(commandLine) like '%\aaaa\7za.exe%' OR
    lower(commandLine) like '%copy .\1.7z *' OR
    lower(commandLine) like '%copy \\client\c$\aaaa*'
)
    OR
baseImage =  'C:\Users\Public\7za.exe'
MATCH-S00334.name: Judgement Panda Exfil Activity
MATCH-S00334.normalized_description: Detects Judgement Panda activity as described in Global Threat Report 2019 by Crowdstrike
MATCH-S00334.normalized_summary: Judgement Panda exfiltration activity detected from process: {{baseImage}} on host: {{device_hostname}}
MATCH-S00334.summary_expression: Judgement Panda exfiltration activity detected from process: {{baseImage}} on host: {{device_hostname}}

MATCH-S00337.description_expression: It is extremely abnormal for svchost.exe to spawn without any CLI arguments and is normally observed when a malicious process spawns the process and injects code into the process memory space.
MATCH-S00337.expression:
(lower(commandLine) like '%svchost.exe' AND
 lower(baseImage) like '%\svchost.exe') 
AND NOT
(lower(parentBaseImage) like '%\rpcnet.exe' OR
 lower(parentBaseImage) like '%\rpcnetp.exe')
MATCH-S00337.name: Suspect Svchost Activity
MATCH-S00337.normalized_description: It is extremely abnormal for svchost.exe to spawn without any CLI arguments and is normally observed when a malicious process spawns the process and injects code into the process memory space.
MATCH-S00337.normalized_summary: svchost spawned without arguments on host: {{device_hostname}}
MATCH-S00337.summary_expression: svchost spawned without arguments on host: {{device_hostname}}

MATCH-S00340.description_expression:
Detects memory dumping from LSASS.

For this rule to work, Microsoft SysInternal Sysmon must be running on the endpoint.
MATCH-S00340.expression:
metadata_vendor = 'Microsoft'
AND metadata_product = 'Windows'
AND metadata_deviceEventId = 'Microsoft-Windows-Sysmon/Operational-10'
AND fields['EventData.TargetImage'] like '%lsass.exe'
AND 
(
    fields['EventData.CallTrace'] like '%dbgcore.dll%'
    OR
    fields['EventData.CallTrace'] like '%dbghelp.dll%'
)
MATCH-S00340.name: LSASS Memory Dump
MATCH-S00340.normalized_description:
Detects memory dumping from LSASS.

For this rule to work, Microsoft SysInternal Sysmon must be running on the endpoint.
MATCH-S00340.normalized_summary: LSASS memory dump detected on host: {{device_hostname}} from user: {{user_username}}
MATCH-S00340.summary_expression: LSASS memory dump detected on host: {{device_hostname}} from user: {{user_username}}

MATCH-S00342.description_expression: DevToolsLauncher.exe has a switch 'LaunchForDeploy' that takes the location of another binary to launch.  Attackers have abused this ability to launch their own non-trusted code.
MATCH-S00342.name: Suspicious use of Dev-Tools-Launcher
MATCH-S00342.normalized_description: DevToolsLauncher.exe has a switch 'LaunchForDeploy' that takes the location of another binary to launch.  Attackers have abused this ability to launch their own non-trusted code.

MATCH-S00342.summary_expression: Suspicious use of DevToolsLauncher on host: {{device_hostname}}

MATCH-S00344.description_expression:
Detects an unsigned image being loaded by LSASS as this may indicate attempted credential access. This rule may also detect persistence activity via LSASS driver loads.

Note that this rule requires Microsoft SysInternals Sysmon installed with Image Loaded (Event Id 7) logging enabled.
MATCH-S00344.expression:
metadata_vendor = 'Microsoft'
AND metadata_product = 'Windows'
AND metadata_deviceEventId = 'Microsoft-Windows-Sysmon/Operational-7'
AND parentBaseImage like '%lsass.exe'
AND fields['EventData.Signed'] = 'false'
MATCH-S00344.name: Unsigned Image Loaded by LSASS
MATCH-S00344.normalized_description:
Detects an unsigned image being loaded by LSASS as this may indicate attempted credential access. This rule may also detect persistence activity via LSASS driver loads.

Note that this rule requires Microsoft SysInternals Sysmon installed with Image Loaded (Event Id 7) logging enabled.

MATCH-S00350.description_expression: Detects programs that connect to typical malware back connect ports based on statistical analysis from two different sandbox system databases.
MATCH-S00350.expression:
metadata_vendor = 'Microsoft'
AND metadata_product = 'Windows'
AND metadata_deviceEventId = 'Microsoft-Windows-Sysmon/Operational-3'
AND dstPort IN (4443,2448,8143,1777,1443,243,65535,13506,3360,200,198,49180,13507,6625,4444,4438,1904,13505,13504,12102,9631,5445,2443,777,13394,13145,12103,5552,3939,3675,666,473,5649,4455,4433,1817,100,65520,1960,1515,743,700,14154,14103,14102,12322,10101,7210,4040,9943)
AND NOT lower(file_path) like '%program files%'
AND (
    dstDevice_ip_isInternal = false
    OR array_contains(listMatches, 'proxy_servers_dst')
    )
AND (fields['insertionstrings_f08'] = 'true' OR fields['EventData.Initiated']= 'true')
AND NOT dstDevice_ip = '127.0.0.1'
MATCH-S00350.name: Suspicious Typical Malware Back Connect Ports
MATCH-S00350.name_expression: Suspicious Typical Malware Back Connect Ports
MATCH-S00350.normalized_description: Detects programs that connect to typical malware back connect ports based on statistical analysis from two different sandbox system databases.
MATCH-S00350.normalized_signal_name: Suspicious Typical Malware Back Connect Ports
MATCH-S00350.normalized_summary: Malware back port connection detected from IP: {{srcDevice_ip}} to IP: {{dstDevice_ip}} on port: {{dstPort}}
MATCH-S00350.summary_expression: Malware back port connection detected from IP: {{srcDevice_ip}} to IP: {{dstDevice_ip}} on port: {{dstPort}}

MATCH-S00352.description_expression: Detection for mshta.exe suspicious execution patterns sometimes involving file polyglotism.
MATCH-S00352.expression:
lower(baseImage) like '%mshta.exe'
AND (lower(commandLine) like '%vbscript%'
OR lower(commandLine) like '%javascript%'
OR lower(commandLine) like '%.jpg%'
OR lower(commandLine) like '%.png%'
OR lower(commandLine) like '%.lnk%'
OR lower(commandLine) like '%.xls%'
OR lower(commandLine) like '%.doc%'
OR lower(commandLine) like '%.zip%'
OR lower(commandLine) like '%.sct%'
OR lower(commandLine) like '%.hta%')
MATCH-S00352.name: MSHTA Suspicious Execution
MATCH-S00352.normalized_description: Detection for mshta.exe suspicious execution patterns sometimes involving file polyglotism.

MATCH-S00355.description_expression: Detects the use of getsystem Meterpreter/Cobalt Strike command by detecting a specific service starting.
MATCH-S00355.expression:
lower(parentBaseImage) like '%services.exe'
AND ((lower(commandLine) like '%cmd%'
AND lower(commandLine) like '%/c%'
AND lower(commandLine) like '%echo%'
AND lower(commandLine) like '%\pipe\%')
OR
(lower(commandLine) like '%comspec%'
AND lower(commandLine) like '%/c%'
AND lower(commandLine) like '%echo%'
AND lower(commandLine) like '%\pipe\%')
OR
(lower(commandLine) like '%rundll32%'
AND lower(commandLine) like '%.dll,a%'
AND lower(commandLine) like '%/p:%'))
AND NOT lower(commandLine) like '%mpcmdrun%'
MATCH-S00355.name: Meterpreter or Cobalt Strike Getsystem Service Start
MATCH-S00355.name_expression: Meterpreter or Cobalt Strike Getsystem Service Start
MATCH-S00355.normalized_description: Detects the use of getsystem Meterpreter/Cobalt Strike command by detecting a specific service starting.

MATCH-S00355.normalized_summary: Meterpreter/Cobalt Strike command detected on host: {{device_hostname}}
MATCH-S00355.summary_expression: Meterpreter/Cobalt Strike command detected on host: {{device_hostname}}

MATCH-S00356.description_expression: Detects suspicious command line arguments of common data compression tools.
MATCH-S00356.expression:
(lower(baseImage) like '7z%.exe'
    OR lower(baseImage) like 'rar.exe'
    OR lower(baseImage) like '%\7z%.exe'
    OR lower(baseImage) like '%\rar.exe'
    OR lower(baseImage) like '%command%line%rar%')
AND (lower(commandLine) like '% -p%'
OR lower(commandLine) like '% -ta%'
OR lower(commandLine) like '% -tb%'
OR lower(commandLine) like '% -sdel%'
OR lower(commandLine) like '% -dw%'
OR lower(commandLine) like '% -hp%')
AND NOT parentBaseImage like '%C:\Program%'
MATCH-S00356.name: Suspicious Compression Tool Parameters
MATCH-S00356.normalized_description: Detects suspicious command line arguments of common data compression tools.
MATCH-S00356.normalized_summary: Suspicious compression tools use on host: {{device_hostname}}
MATCH-S00356.summary_expression: Suspicious compression tools use on host: {{device_hostname}}

MATCH-S00359.description_expression: Detects a suspicious Microsoft certutil execution with sub commands like 'decode' sub command, which is sometimes used to decode malicious code with the built-in certutil utility.
MATCH-S00359.expression:
lower(baseImage) like '%certutil.exe'
AND
    (lower(commandLine) like '% -decode %'
    OR lower(commandLine) like '% /decode %'
    OR lower(commandLine) like '% -decodehex %'
    OR lower(commandLine) like '% /decodehex %'
    OR lower(commandLine) like '% -urlcache %'
    OR lower(commandLine) like '% /urlcache %'
    OR lower(commandLine) like '% -verifyctl %'
    OR lower(commandLine) like '% /verifyctl %'
    OR lower(commandLine) like '% -encode %'
    OR lower(commandLine) like '% /encode %'
    OR lower(commandLine) like '%certutil* -URL%'
    OR lower(commandLine) like '%certutil* /URL%'
    OR lower(commandLine) like '%certutil* -ping%'
    OR lower(commandLine) like '%certutil* /ping%')
MATCH-S00359.name: Suspicious Certutil Command
MATCH-S00359.normalized_description: Detects a suspicious Microsoft certutil execution with sub commands like 'decode' sub command, which is sometimes used to decode malicious code with the built-in certutil utility.
MATCH-S00359.normalized_summary: Suspicious Certutil command observed on host: {{device_hostname}}
MATCH-S00359.summary_expression: Suspicious Certutil command observed on host: {{device_hostname}}

MATCH-S00388.description_expression: Potential adversaries stopping ETW providers recording loaded .NET assemblies.

MATCH-S00388.normalized_description: Potential adversaries stopping ETW providers recording loaded .NET assemblies.
MATCH-S00388.normalized_summary: COMPlus ETWEnabled commands observed from user: {{user_username}} on host: {{device_hostname}}

MATCH-S00390.description_expression:
Monitors for use of reg.exe with parameters indicating the attempted export of hashed credentials.

Audit Object Access (success & failure) must be enabled for this rule to function.

Monitoring of the following registry keys is necessary:
HKLM\Security
HKLM\Security\Cache
HKLM\System
HKLM\Security\Policy\Secrets
HKLM\Sam
MATCH-S00390.expression:
metadata_vendor = 'Microsoft'
AND metadata_product = 'Windows'
AND metadata_deviceEventId = 'Security-4656'
AND fields['EventData.ProcessName'] like '%reg.exe'
AND
(
    file_path like '%HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE\Security%'
    OR
    file_path like '%HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE\SAM%'
    OR
    file_path like '%HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE\System%'
    OR
    file_path like '%HKLM\Security%'
    OR
    file_path like '%HKLM\SAM%'
    OR
    file_path like '%HKLM\SYSTEM%'
)
MATCH-S00390.name: Attempted Credential Dump From Registry Via Reg.Exe
MATCH-S00390.name_expression: Attempted Credential Dump From Registry Via Reg.exe
MATCH-S00390.normalized_description:
Monitors for use of reg.exe with parameters indicating the attempted export of hashed credentials.

Audit Object Access (success & failure) must be enabled for this rule to function.

Monitoring of the following registry keys is necessary:
HKLM\Security
HKLM\Security\Cache
HKLM\System
HKLM\Security\Policy\Secrets
HKLM\Sam
MATCH-S00390.normalized_signal_name: Attempted Credential Dump From Registry Via Reg.exe
MATCH-S00390.normalized_summary: Attempt to export credentials detected on host: {{device_hostname}} by user: {{user_username}}
MATCH-S00390.summary_expression: Attempt to export credentials detected on host: {{device_hostname}} by user: {{user_username}}

MATCH-S00392.description_expression: Detects a file or folder permissions modifications.
MATCH-S00392.expression:
(((lower(baseImage) like '%takeown.exe'
    OR lower(baseImage) like '%cacls.exe'
    OR lower(baseImage) like '%icacls.exe')
    AND lower(commandLine) like '%/grant')
OR (lower(baseImage) like '%attrib.exe'
    AND lower(commandLine) like '%-r%'))
MATCH-S00392.name: File or Folder Permissions Modifications
MATCH-S00392.normalized_description: Detects a file or folder permissions modifications.
MATCH-S00392.normalized_summary: User: {{user_username}} executed file or folder modification on host: {{device_hostname}}
MATCH-S00392.summary_expression: User: {{user_username}} executed file or folder modification on host: {{device_hostname}}

MATCH-S00394.description_expression: Detects usage of findstr to identify and execute a lnk file as seen within the HHS redirect attack.
MATCH-S00394.expression:
lower(baseImage) like '%findstr.exe'
AND (lower(commandLine) like '%.lnk'
    OR lower(commandLine) like '%.lnk %')
MATCH-S00394.name: Findstr Launching .lnk File
MATCH-S00394.normalized_description: Detects usage of findstr to identify and execute a lnk file as seen within the HHS redirect attack.

MATCH-S00396.description_expression: A typical ping packet will be very small. A large ICMP packet may indicate the presence of command and control traffic.
MATCH-S00396.expression:
ipProtocol in ('1','ICMP')
AND srcDevice_ip_isInternal = true
AND (
    dstDevice_ip_isInternal = false
    OR array_contains(listMatches, 'proxy_servers_dst')
    )
AND bytesOut > 1000
MATCH-S00396.name: Large Outbound ICMP Packets
MATCH-S00396.normalized_description: A typical ping packet will be very small. A large ICMP packet may indicate the presence of command and control traffic.
MATCH-S00396.normalized_summary: ICMP packet of size: {{bytesOut}} sent from IP: {{srcDevice_ip}}
MATCH-S00396.summary_expression: ICMP packet of size: {{bytesOut}} sent from IP: {{srcDevice_ip}}

MATCH-S00397.description_expression: Observes for image accesses of images used for credential dumping via Mimikatz
MATCH-S00397.expression:
metadata_vendor = 'Microsoft'
AND metadata_product = 'Windows'
AND metadata_deviceEventId = 'Microsoft-Windows-Sysmon/Operational-7'
AND
(
       baseImage like '%WinSCard.dll'
    OR baseImage like '%cryptdll.dll'
    OR baseImage like '%hid.dll'
    OR baseImage like '%samlib.dll'
    OR baseImage like 'vaultcli.dll'
)
MATCH-S00397.name: Mimikatz Loaded Images Detected
MATCH-S00397.normalized_description: Observes for image accesses of images used for credential dumping via Mimikatz

MATCH-S00398.description_expression: Detect the use of Windows Defender to download payloads.
MATCH-S00398.expression:

MATCH-S00398.summary_expression: Observed payload download using Windows Defender on host: {{device_hostname}}

MATCH-S00400.description_expression: Detects downloaded payloads from remote server.

MATCH-S00404.description_expression: Observes for eventID 4703 with SeDebugPrivileges added by powershell. May indicate the presence of Mimikatz credential dumping.
MATCH-S00404.expression:
metadata_vendor = 'Microsoft'
AND metadata_product = 'Windows'
AND metadata_deviceEventId = 'Security-4703'
AND lower(baseImage) like '%powershell.exe'
and fields['EventData.EnabledPrivilegeList'] like '%SeDebugPrivilege%'
MATCH-S00404.name: Mimikatz via Powershell and EventID 4703

MATCH-S00404.summary_expression: Potential Mimikatz credential dumping activity on host: {{device_hostname}}

MATCH-S00406.description_expression: Observes for ntdsutil, vssadmin, wmic, or powershell creating shadow copies. This is another method to extract credentials.
MATCH-S00406.expression:
(
       lower(baseImage) like '%ntdsutil.exe'
    OR lower(baseImage) like '%vssadmin.exe'
    OR lower(baseImage) like '%wmic.exe'
    OR lower(baseImage) like '%powershell.exe'
)
AND
(
       lower(commandLine) like '%ntds%create%'
    OR lower(commandLine) like '%create%shadow%'
    OR lower(commandLine) like '%shadowcopy%create%'
)
MATCH-S00406.name: Shadow Copy Creation
MATCH-S00406.normalized_description: Observes for ntdsutil, vssadmin, wmic, or powershell creating shadow copies. This is another method to extract credentials.
MATCH-S00406.normalized_summary: Shadow copy creation observed on host: {{device_hostname}}
MATCH-S00406.summary_expression: Shadow copy creation observed on host: {{device_hostname}}

MATCH-S00414.description_expression: Dump sam, system or security hives using REG.exe utility.
MATCH-S00414.expression:
lower(baseImage) like '%reg.exe'
AND (lower(commandLine) like '%save%'
    OR lower(commandLine) like '%export%')
AND (lower(commandLine) like '%hklm%'
    OR lower(commandLine) like '%hkey_local_machine%')
AND (lower(commandLine) like '%\system%'
    OR lower(commandLine) like '%\sam%'
    OR lower(commandLine) like '%\security%')
AND NOT lower(commandLine) like '%desktopcentral_agent%'
AND NOT lower(commandLine) like '%rapid7%'

MATCH-S00415.description_expression:
Observes for attempts to clear Windows event logs using wevtutil.

Command line auditing is necessary for this rule to function.
MATCH-S00415.expression:
metadata_vendor = 'Microsoft'
AND metadata_product = 'Windows'
AND lower(baseImage) like '%wevtutil.exe'
AND
(       
          lower(commandLine) like '% cl %'
       OR lower(commandLine) like '%clear-log%'
)
AND  
(
       lower(commandLine) like '%system%'
    OR lower(commandLine) like '%security%'
    OR lower(commandLine) like '%setup%'
    OR lower(commandLine) like '%application%'  
)
MATCH-S00415.name: Attempt to Clear Windows Event Logs Using Wevtutil
MATCH-S00415.name_expression: Attempt to Clear Windows Event Logs Using Wevtutil

MATCH-S00419.description_expression: Observes for common file extensions appearing before the actual file extension (ex. totallynotmalware.pdf.exe)
MATCH-S00419.expression:
lower(baseImage) rlike '\.(pdf|docx?|html?|txt|zip|xlsx?|pptx?|jpe?g|gif|png)\.\w+$'
AND
(
    lower(baseImage) not like '%.old'
 OR lower(baseImage) not like '%.tmp'
)

MATCH-S00427.description_expression: Detects a process memory dump performed via ordinal function 24 in comsvcs.dll.

MATCH-S00429.description_expression: Detect creation of dump files containing the memory space of lsass.exe, which contains sensitive credentials. Identifies usage of Sysinternals procdump.exe to export the memory space of lsass.exe which contains sensitive credentials.
MATCH-S00429.expression:
(lower(commandLine) like '%lsass%'
AND lower(commandLine) like '%.dmp%')
AND NOT lower(baseImage) like '%werfault.exe%'
AND NOT (lower(baseImage) like '%procdump%'
        AND lower(baseImage) like '%.exe'
        AND lower(commandLine) like '%lsass%')
MATCH-S00429.name: LSASS Memory Dumping
MATCH-S00429.normalized_description: Detect creation of dump files containing the memory space of lsass.exe, which contains sensitive credentials. Identifies usage of Sysinternals procdump.exe to export the memory space of lsass.exe which contains sensitive credentials.
MATCH-S00429.normalized_summary: LSASS memory dump detected on host: {{device_hostname}} from user: {{user_username}}
MATCH-S00429.summary_expression: LSASS memory dump detected on host: {{device_hostname}} from user: {{user_username}}

MATCH-S00431.description_expression: Detects suspicious uses of the SysInternals Procdump utility by using a special command line parameter in combination with the lsass.exe process. This way we're also able to catch cases in which the attacker has renamed the procdump executable.
MATCH-S00431.expression:
((lower(commandLine) like '% -ma %'
AND lower(commandLine) like '% lsass%')
OR lower(commandLine) like '% -ma ls%')
MATCH-S00431.name: Suspicious Use of Procdump
MATCH-S00431.normalized_description: Detects suspicious uses of the SysInternals Procdump utility by using a special command line parameter in combination with the lsass.exe process. This way we're also able to catch cases in which the attacker has renamed the procdump executable.

MATCH-S00433.description_expression: Observes for Okta user account lock out events

MATCH-S00433.name: Okta Account Lockout
MATCH-S00433.normalized_description: Observes for Okta user account lock out events

MATCH-S00435.description_expression: Extensible Stylesheet Language (XSL) files are commonly used to describe the processing and rendering of data within XML files, rule detects when adversaries abuse this functionality to execute arbitrary files while potentially bypassing application whitelisting defenses.
MATCH-S00435.expression:
((lower(baseImage) like '%wmic.exe'
AND lower(commandLine) like '%/format%')
OR lower(baseImage) like '%msxsl.exe')
AND NOT lower(commandLine) rlike '/format:(?:csv|hform|htable|htable-sortby(?:\.xsl)?|list|mof|rawxml|table|texttablewsys(?:\.xsl)?|value|wmicli(?:mof|table|value)format(?:nosys)?(?:\.xsl)?|xml)'
MATCH-S00435.name: XSL Script Processing
MATCH-S00435.normalized_description: Extensible Stylesheet Language (XSL) files are commonly used to describe the processing and rendering of data within XML files, rule detects when adversaries abuse this functionality to execute arbitrary files while potentially bypassing application whitelisting defenses.
MATCH-S00435.normalized_summary: Detected XSL script processing on host: {{device_hostname}}
MATCH-S00435.summary_expression: Detected XSL script processing on host: {{device_hostname}}

metadata_vendor = 'Microsoft'
AND metadata_product = 'Windows'
AND metadata_deviceEventId IN ('Security-4656','Security-4663','Security-4658')
AND (file_path like '%.AAA'
    OR file_path like '%.ZZZ')

MATCH-S00449.description_expression:

(

    OR lower(commandLine) like '% bypass %'

MATCH-S00451.normalized_summary: Potential malicious Nirsoft tool usage found on host: {{device_hostname}} in command: {{commandline}}
MATCH-S00451.summary_expression: Potential malicious Nirsoft tool usage found on host: {{device_hostname}} in command: {{commandline}}

MATCH-S00454.description_expression: Observes for SMB traffic allowed through the firewall.
MATCH-S00454.expression:
(
  (srcDevice_ip_isInternal = false AND dstDevice_ip_isInternal = true)
OR
(srcDevice_ip_isInternal = true AND dstDevice_ip_isInternal = false)
)
AND (
    (   
        dstPort in (139,445)
    AND lower(ipProtocol) in ('6','tcp')
    )
OR
    (
       lower(tcpProtocol) = 'smb'
    OR lower(application) = 'smb'
    )
)    
AND
(
        objectType like 'Network%'
    AND  lower(action) rlike '(allow|accept)'
)
MATCH-S00454.name: Firewall Allowed SMB Traffic
MATCH-S00454.normalized_description: Observes for SMB traffic allowed through the firewall.
MATCH-S00454.normalized_summary: Firewall allowed SMB traffic from IP {{srcDevice_ip}} to {{dstDevice_ip}}
MATCH-S00454.summary_expression: Firewall allowed SMB traffic from IP {{srcDevice_ip}} to {{dstDevice_ip}}

MATCH-S00455.description_expression: Detects a successful authentication to Office 365 where the user agent string contains PowerShell. By default, PowerShell will appear in the user agent string when used in authentication attempts for 0365. This can be an indication of a PowerShell Exploitation framework being used to authenticate.
MATCH-S00455.expression:
metadata_vendor = 'Microsoft'
AND metadata_product = 'Office 365'
AND metadata_deviceEventId IN ('AzureActiveDirectoryStsLogon','15')
AND lower(fields['ExtendedProperties.1.Value']) like '%powershell%'
AND success = true
MATCH-S00455.name: O365 - Successful Authentication with PowerShell User Agent
MATCH-S00455.name_expression: O365 - Successful Authentication with PowerShell User Agent
MATCH-S00455.normalized_description: Detects a successful authentication to Office 365 where the user agent string contains PowerShell. By default, PowerShell will appear in the user agent string when used in authentication attempts for 0365. This can be an indication of a PowerShell Exploitation framework being used to authenticate.
MATCH-S00455.normalized_signal_name: O365 - Successful Authentication with PowerShell User Agent

MATCH-S00457.description_expression: Observes for file uploads above 50MB in size. It is recommended to tune this rule to desired file size threshold for your organization as well as to exclude users/systems typically sending large outbound files.
MATCH-S00457.expression:
objectType like 'Network%'
AND (
    dstDevice_ip_isInternal = false
    OR array_contains(listMatches, 'proxy_servers_dst')
    )
AND bytesOut > 50000000
MATCH-S00457.name: Large File Upload
MATCH-S00457.normalized_description: Observes for file uploads above 50MB in size. It is recommended to tune this rule to desired file size threshold for your organization as well as to exclude users/systems typically sending large outbound files.
MATCH-S00457.normalized_summary: File of size: {{bytesOut}} uploaded from IP: {{srcDevice_ip}}
MATCH-S00457.summary_expression: File of size: {{bytesOut}} uploaded from IP: {{srcDevice_ip}}

MATCH-S00458.description_expression:
From FireEye Red Team Tool Countermeasures:
This IOC detects indicators associated with the ADPassHunt Tool. This tool is used to hunt for AD credentials and used via execute-assembly that looks for passwords in GPP, Autoruns and AD objects
MATCH-S00458.expression:
baseImage like '%.exe'
AND lower(commandLine) like '%-dc%'
AND lower(commandLine) like '%-domain%'
AND lower(commandLine) like '%-action%'
AND 
(
   lower(commandLine) like '%gpp'
OR lower(commandLine) like '%ad'
)
MATCH-S00458.name: ADPassHunt Tool
MATCH-S00458.normalized_description:
From FireEye Red Team Tool Countermeasures:
This IOC detects indicators associated with the ADPassHunt Tool. This tool is used to hunt for AD credentials and used via execute-assembly that looks for passwords in GPP, Autoruns and AD objects
MATCH-S00458.normalized_summary: ADPassHunt (credential stealer) tool associated command line arguments detected on host: {{device_hostname}} by user: {{user_username}} using process: {{baseImage}}
MATCH-S00458.summary_expression: ADPassHunt (credential stealer) tool associated command line arguments detected on host: {{device_hostname}} by user: {{user_username}} using process: {{baseImage}}

MATCH-S00459.description_expression: From FireEye Red Team Tool Countermeasures: This IOC detects indicators associated with cobalt strike beacon network activity.
MATCH-S00459.expression:
(http_userAgent like '%Mozilla/5.0 (Windows NT 6.1; WOW64; Trident/7.0; rv:11.0) like Gecko%'
    AND
    ((lower(http_url) like '%/bunsen/api/bunsen/events/v1%'
    OR lower(http_url) like '%/assets/img/svg_illustrations/delivery_takeout_109x86_v2.svg%'
    OR lower(http_url) like '%/b/ss/globaldesktopmobilesite/1/JS-2.11.0/s%')
    OR
    (lower(http_url) like '%/gql/batch%'
    AND lower(http_url) like '%/sit_rep%'
    AND lower(http_url) like '%/ad_acknowledgment%'))
    )
OR
    (metadata_vendor = 'Microsoft'
    AND metadata_product = 'Windows'
    AND metadata_deviceEventId = 'Microsoft-Windows-Sysmon/Operational-7'
    AND parentBaseImage like '%SearchProtocolHost.exe'
    AND baseImage like '%Updater.dll'
    )
MATCH-S00459.name: Potential Cobalt Strike Profile
MATCH-S00459.normalized_description: From FireEye Red Team Tool Countermeasures: This IOC detects indicators associated with cobalt strike beacon network activity.
MATCH-S00459.normalized_summary: Cobalt Strike Indicators detected on host: {{srcDevice_ip}}
MATCH-S00459.summary_expression: Cobalt Strike Indicators detected on host: {{srcDevice_ip}}

MATCH-S00461.description_expression:
From FireEye Red Team Tool Countermeasures:
LNKSmasher embeds an arbitrary payload in an LNK that can be executed by the embedded command. This IOC will detect the commands executed by both the new and old version of LNKSmasher.
MATCH-S00461.expression:
lower(commandLine) like '%dir *si.lnk /b /a-d%'
    OR
    (
        lower(commandLine) like '%tokens=1 delims=[]%'
    AND lower(commandLine) like '%lnk /b /a-d%'
    AND lower(commandLine) like '%findstr /r /c%'
    AND lower(commandLine) like '%do cmd /c \%d'
    AND lower(commandLine) like '%shell32.dll,shellexec_rundll%'
    AND lower(commandLine) rlike '%h in \(\x27dir \*'
    )
    OR
    (
        lower(commandLine) like '%/c copy /b /y *%'
    AND lower(commandLine) like '%system32\\*rtutil.exe%'
    AND lower(commandLine) like '%&&echo 00>>%'
    AND lower(commandLine) like '%-f -enc""odehex%'
    AND lower(commandLine) like '%more +69%'
    AND lower(commandLine) like '%-f -decod""ehex%'
    )
MATCH-S00461.name: LNKSmasher Utility Commands
MATCH-S00461.normalized_description:
From FireEye Red Team Tool Countermeasures:
LNKSmasher embeds an arbitrary payload in an LNK that can be executed by the embedded command. This IOC will detect the commands executed by both the new and old version of LNKSmasher.
MATCH-S00461.normalized_summary: Command with LNKSmasher Utility indicators detected on host: {{device_hostname}}
MATCH-S00461.summary_expression: Command with LNKSmasher Utility indicators detected on host: {{device_hostname}}

MATCH-S00464.description_expression:
From FireEye Red Team Tool Countermeasures:
This alert looks for evidence of the native signed Windows binary InstallUtil.exe being used to load PE files. This technique can be used to bypass application whitelisting and has been observed used in the wild.
MATCH-S00464.expression:
((
   parentBaseImage like '%:\Windows\System32\msiexec.exe'
OR parentBaseImage like '%\Windows\SysWOW64\msiexec.exe'
OR parentBaseImage rlike '^[A-Z]:\\Windows\\winsxs\\[^\\]+\\msiexec\.exe$'
OR parentBaseImage rlike '^.:\\BUILD\\[^\\]+\\Nant\\NAnt\.exe$'
)
AND
(
   baseImage like '%:\Program Files\KARDEX\Power Pick Global\InstallUtil.exe'
OR baseImage like '%:\Program Files (x86)\KARDEX\Power Pick Global\InstallUtil.exe'
)
AND
((
   commandLine like '%:\Program Files (x86)\IBM\WinCollect Console\bin\WinCollectUserInterface.dll%'
OR commandLine like '%\Microsoft.Workflow.Compiler.exe.lib%'
OR commandLine like '%\UIAutomationClientsideProviders.dll.rsp%'
OR commandLine like '%\AppData\sbscmp20_mscorwks.dll.rsp'
OR commandLine like '%/showcallstack%'
OR commandLine like '% /user=%'
OR commandLine rlike '[A-Z]:\\Program Files( \(x86\))?\\'
OR commandLine rlike '[-/]logfile= '
OR commandLine rlike '[-/]u'
OR commandLine rlike 'team\s*foundation'
OR commandLine rlike '^.:\\BUILD\\[^\\]+\\Nant\\NAnt\.exe$'
OR commandLine rlike '[-/]LogToConsole=false'
OR commandLine rlike '[A-Z]:\\Applications\\[^\\]+\\Private\\[^\\]+\\.*\.exe')
OR
    (
        commandLine rlike '[-/]LogToConsole=true'
    AND parentBaseImage like '%:\Program Files\%'
    AND parentBaseImage like ':\Program Files (x86)\'
    ))
)
MATCH-S00464.name: Suspicious Non-Standard InstallUtil Execution
MATCH-S00464.normalized_description:
From FireEye Red Team Tool Countermeasures:
This alert looks for evidence of the native signed Windows binary InstallUtil.exe being used to load PE files. This technique can be used to bypass application whitelisting and has been observed used in the wild.
MATCH-S00464.normalized_summary: Suspicious InstallUtil Execution - Parent process: {{parentBaseImage}} spawned process: {{baseImage}} on host: {{device_hostname}}
MATCH-S00464.summary_expression: Suspicious InstallUtil Execution - Parent process: {{parentBaseImage}} spawned process: {{baseImage}} on host: {{device_hostname}}

MATCH-S00465.description_expression:
From FireEye Red Team Tool Countermeasures:
PXELoot (PAL) is a C# tool designed to aid in the discovery and exploitation of misconfigurations in Windows Deployment Services (WDS).
MATCH-S00465.expression:
(
	file_path rlike '^.:\\Windows\\Temp\\\{[0-9a-f]{8}-[0-9a-f]{4}-[0-9a-f]{4}-[0-9a-f]{4}-[0-9a-f]{12}\}:\$WIMMOUNTDATA$'
AND baseImage like '%dism.exe'
)
OR
(
	   lower(commandLine) like '%--dhcp-recon%'
	OR lower(commandLine) rlike '\s-(u|-unmount-path)\s+"?.:\\Windows\\Temp\\\{[0-9a-f]{8}-[0-9a-f]{4}-[0-9a-f]{4}-[0-9a-f]{4}-[0-9a-f]{12}\}"?$'
	OR 
	(
		    lower(commandLine) like '%--targets%'
		AND lower(commandLine) like '%--rpc%'
	)
)
OR
(
		lower(commandLine) rlike '\s-(w|-wim)\s.*?\\reminst\\.*?\.wim'
	AND lower(commandLine) rlike '\s-(U|-Username)\s'
	AND lower(commandLine) rlike '\s-(P|-Password)\s'
	AND lower(commandLine) rlike '\s-(D|-Domain)\s'
)
MATCH-S00465.name: PXELoot Utility
MATCH-S00465.normalized_description:
From FireEye Red Team Tool Countermeasures:
PXELoot (PAL) is a C# tool designed to aid in the discovery and exploitation of misconfigurations in Windows Deployment Services (WDS).

MATCH-S00470.description_expression:
From FireEye Sunburst Countermeasures:
This rule identifies writes of specific file types associated with activity related to the SUNBURST backdoored version of the SolarWinds.Orion.Core.BusinessLayer.dll process. This rule may generate false positives depending on the configuration of SolarWinds in a given environment, and may require tuning to exclude legitimate activity. SUNBURST is a backdoor that has the ability to spawn and kill processes, write and delete files, set and create registry keys, gather system information, and disable a set of forensic analysis tools and services.
MATCH-S00470.expression:
(
baseImage like '%solarwinds.businesslayerhost.exe'
OR baseImage like '%solarwinds.businesslayerhostx64.exe'
)
AND file_path rlike '\.exe|\.dll|\.ps1|\.jpg|\.png'
MATCH-S00470.name: Sunburst Suspicious File Writes
MATCH-S00470.normalized_description:
From FireEye Sunburst Countermeasures:
This rule identifies writes of specific file types associated with activity related to the SUNBURST backdoored version of the SolarWinds.Orion.Core.BusinessLayer.dll process. This rule may generate false positives depending on the configuration of SolarWinds in a given environment, and may require tuning to exclude legitimate activity. SUNBURST is a backdoor that has the ability to spawn and kill processes, write and delete files, set and create registry keys, gather system information, and disable a set of forensic analysis tools and services.

MATCH-S00471.description_expression:
From FireEye Red Team Tool Countermeasures:
This IOC detects windows persistence activity performed by the Sharpersist utility. It has multiple persistence functionalities such as Keepass, hotkey, new schedule task, Startup Folder and Scheduled Task Backdoor.
MATCH-S00471.expression:
baseImage like '%sharperist.exe'
OR
(
        commandLine like '% -t %'
    AND commandLine like '% -c %'
    AND commandLine rlike '\s\-m\s+?(add|check|remove|list)\s'   
)
OR
(
    (
            commandLine like '% hotkey %'
        AND commandLine like '% -k %'
        AND commandLine like '% -f %'
    )
    OR
    (
            commandLine like '% schtaskbackdoor %'
        AND commandLine like '% -n %'
    )
    OR
    (
            commandLine like '% wmi %'
        AND commandLine like '% -n %'
    )
    OR
    (
            commandLine like '% comhijack %'
        AND commandLine like '%.dll'
    )
)
MATCH-S00471.name: SharPersist A Utility
MATCH-S00471.normalized_description:
From FireEye Red Team Tool Countermeasures:
This IOC detects windows persistence activity performed by the Sharpersist utility. It has multiple persistence functionalities such as Keepass, hotkey, new schedule task, Startup Folder and Scheduled Task Backdoor.

MATCH-S00472.description_expression:
From FireEye Red Team Tool Countermeasures:
SHARPIVOT is a .NET console application that can be used to perform command execution against a remote target for the purposes of lateral movement.
MATCH-S00472.expression:
lower(file_hash_md5) = 'e4efa759d425e2f26fbc29943a30f5bd'
OR
(
        lower(commandLine) rlike '\s-(t|technique)\s+?(wmi|rpc|schtask|servicefailure|com|winrm|schtaskmod|poisonhandler)(\s|$)'
    AND 
    (
           lower(commandLine) like '% -remotehost %'
        OR lower(commandLine) like '% -r %'
    )
    AND
    (
           lower(commandLine) rlike '\s-(m|method)\s+?list(\s|$)'
        OR 
           (
                   lower(commandLine) rlike '\s-(m|method)\s+?(execute|check)(\s|$)'
               AND 
                (
                       lower(commandLine) like '% -command %'
                    OR lower(commandLine) like '% -c %'
                )
               AND
                (
                       lower(commandLine) like '% -argument %'
                    OR lower(commandLine) like '% -a %'
                )
           )

    
    )
)
MATCH-S00472.name: SharPivot Utility
MATCH-S00472.normalized_description:
From FireEye Red Team Tool Countermeasures:
SHARPIVOT is a .NET console application that can be used to perform command execution against a remote target for the purposes of lateral movement.
MATCH-S00472.normalized_summary: SharPivot Utility indicator detected on host: {{device_hostname}}
MATCH-S00472.summary_expression: SharPivot Utility indicator detected on host: {{device_hostname}}

MATCH-S00474.description_expression:
From FireEye Red Team Tool Countermeasures:
Network detection rule that looks for specific HTTP header and URI values. These are related to the HTTP GET request content designated within the Cobalt Strike malleable C2 profile.
MATCH-S00474.expression:
objectType = 'NetworkHTTP'
AND lower(http_method) = 'get'
AND lower(http_url_path) LIKE '%parent_request_id=%'
AND lower(http_url_path) LIKE '%request_origin=user%'
AND bro_http_request_headers['SEC-FETCH-DEST'] = 'empty'
AND lower(http_url) RLIKE 'parent_request_id=[A-Za-z0-9_/\+\-%]{128,1024}={0,2}'
MATCH-S00474.name: Backdoor.HTTP.BEACON.[Yelp GET]
MATCH-S00474.normalized_description:
From FireEye Red Team Tool Countermeasures:
Network detection rule that looks for specific HTTP header and URI values. These are related to the HTTP GET request content designated within the Cobalt Strike malleable C2 profile.
MATCH-S00474.normalized_summary: Suspicious HTTP Request Headers to URL: {{http_url}} on destination host: {{dstDevice_ip}}
MATCH-S00474.summary_expression: Suspicious HTTP Request Headers to URL: {{http_url}} on destination host: {{dstDevice_ip}}

MATCH-S00475.description_expression: From FireEye Red Team Tool Countermeasures: This alert looks for renamed msbuild.exe process executions based on common command line arguments used for msbuild.exe. Attackers frequently use msbuild.exe (or renamed versions of this binary) to execute arbitrary CSharp payloads written to disk most commonly as .csproj files (though any file with an extension ending in "proj" will work) either referenced on the command line or located in the same directory as the msbuild.exe binary. The XML payload on disk should be acquired and examined to determine the functionality of the payload.
MATCH-S00475.expression:
(lower(baseImage) like '%msbuild.exe'
AND (lower(commandLine) rlike '[^a-z0-9]msbuild(\.exe)?[^a-z0-9]')
AND (lower(parentBaseImage) like '%.0\bin\nact.exe'
    OR lower(parentBaseImage) like '%msbuild\15.0\bin\amd64\tracker.exe'
    OR lower(parentBaseImage) = 'nact.exe'
    OR lower(parentBaseImage) = 'tracker.exe'))
OR ((file_path like '%\Program Files\dotnet\dotnet.exe'
    OR baseImage like '%\Program Files\dotnet\dotnet.exe')
AND (lower(commandLine) like '%noconsolelogger%'
    OR lower(commandLine) like '%noconlog%'))
MATCH-S00475.name: Renamed MSBUILD.EXE by Arguments
MATCH-S00475.normalized_description: From FireEye Red Team Tool Countermeasures: This alert looks for renamed msbuild.exe process executions based on common command line arguments used for msbuild.exe. Attackers frequently use msbuild.exe (or renamed versions of this binary) to execute arbitrary CSharp payloads written to disk most commonly as .csproj files (though any file with an extension ending in "proj" will work) either referenced on the command line or located in the same directory as the msbuild.exe binary. The XML payload on disk should be acquired and examined to determine the functionality of the payload.
MATCH-S00475.normalized_summary: Detected MSBUILD.exe command line arguments on host: {{device_hostname}}
MATCH-S00475.summary_expression: Detected MSBUILD.exe command line arguments on host: {{device_hostname}}

MATCH-S00476.description_expression: From FireEye Red Team Tool Countermeasures: This IOC detects suspicious execution of searchindexer. This technique is known to be used by Cobaltstrike which inject malicious code into a newly spawned searchindexer process to evade detection.
MATCH-S00476.expression:
(lower(baseImage) = 'c:\windows\system32\searchindexer.exe'
    OR lower(file_path) = 'c:\windows\system32\searchindexer.exe')
AND user_username RLIKE '^(?:nt authority\\)?system$'
AND lower(parentBaseImage) like '%explorer.exe'
MATCH-S00476.name: Suspicious Execution of Search Indexer
MATCH-S00476.normalized_description: From FireEye Red Team Tool Countermeasures: This IOC detects suspicious execution of searchindexer. This technique is known to be used by Cobaltstrike which inject malicious code into a newly spawned searchindexer process to evade detection.

MATCH-S00477.description_expression: From FireEye Red Team Tool Countermeasures: This IOC detects indicators associated with suspicious execution of Microsoft WorkFlow Compiler. This is known to be used by Cobaltstrike for lateral movement with specially crafted XLM and OXLM files.
MATCH-S00477.expression:
((lower(parentBaseImage) like '%cmd.exe'
    OR lower(parentBaseImage) like '%msbuild.exe')
AND (lower(commandLine) like '%invoke-wmimethod -computername%'
    OR commandLine like '%-path win32_process -name create -argumentlist C:\Windows\Microsoft.NET\Framework64\v4.0.30319\Microsoft.Workflow.Compiler.exe C:\Windows\Microsoft.NET\Framework64\v4.0.30319\%'
    OR commandLine like'%.xml C:\Windows\Microsoft.NET\Framework64\v4.0.30319\results.txt%'))
OR
((commandLine like '%cmd.exe /c C:\Windows\Microsoft.NET\Framework64\v4.0.30319\Microsoft.Workflow.Compiler.exe C:\ProgramData\MSUpdate\%'
    OR commandLine like '%.xml C:\ProgramData\MSUpdate\results.txt%')
AND lower(parentBaseImage) like '%wmic%')
MATCH-S00477.name: Suspicious Use of Workflow Compiler for Payload Execution
MATCH-S00477.name_expression: Suspicious Use of Workflow Compiler for Payload Execution
MATCH-S00477.normalized_description: From FireEye Red Team Tool Countermeasures: This IOC detects indicators associated with suspicious execution of Microsoft WorkFlow Compiler. This is known to be used by Cobaltstrike for lateral movement with specially crafted XLM and OXLM files.
MATCH-S00477.normalized_signal_name: Suspicious Use of Workflow Compiler for Payload Execution
MATCH-S00477.normalized_summary: Parent process: {{parentBaseImage}} spawned process: {{baseImage}} on host: {{device_hostname}}
MATCH-S00477.summary_expression: Parent process: {{parentBaseImage}} spawned process: {{baseImage}} on host: {{device_hostname}}

MATCH-S00480.description_expression: From FireEye Red Team Tool Countermeasures: This rule identifies suspicious child processes of SolarWinds.Orion.Core.BusinessLayer.dll that may be evidence of the SUNBURST backdoor. The behavior of SolarWinds.Orion.Core.BusinessLayer.dll is dependent on per-enterprise configuration, so additional tuning may be required to exclude legitimate activity in a given environment. SUNBURST is a backdoor that has the ability to spawn and kill processes, write and delete files, set and create registry keys, gather system information, and disable a set of forensic analysis tools and services.
MATCH-S00480.expression:
(lower(parentBaseImage) like '%solarwinds.businesslayerhost.exe'
OR lower(parentBaseImage) like '%solarWinds.businesslayerhostx64.exe')
AND NOT(lower(baseImage) like '%\solarwinds\orion\apm\apmservicecontrol.exe'
    OR lower(file_path) like '%\solarwinds\orion\apm\apmservicecontrol.exe'
    OR lower(baseImage) like '%\solarwinds\orion\exporttopdfcmd.exe'
    OR lower(file_path) like '%\solarwinds\orion\exporttopdfcmd.exe'
    OR lower(baseImage) like '%\solarwinds.credentials\solarwinds.credentials.orion.webapi.exe'
    OR lower(file_path) like '%\solarwinds.credentials\solarwinds.credentials.orion.webapi.exe'
    OR lower(baseImage) like '%\solarwinds\orion\topology\solarwinds.orion.topology.calculator.exe'
    OR lower(file_path) like '%\solarwinds\orion\topology\solarwinds.orion.topology.calculator.exe'
    OR lower(baseImage) like '%\solarwinds\orion\database-maint.exe'
    OR lower(file_path) like '%\solarwinds\orion\database-maint.exe'
    OR lower(baseImage) like '%\solarwinds.orion.apipoller.service\solarwinds.orion.apipoller.service.exe'
    OR lower(file_path) like '%\solarwinds.orion.apipoller.service\solarwinds.orion.apipoller.service.exe'
    OR lower(baseImage) like '%\windows\syswow64\werfault.exe'
    OR lower(file_path) like '%\windows\syswow64\werfault.exe')
MATCH-S00480.name: Solarwinds Suspicious Child Processes
MATCH-S00480.normalized_description: From FireEye Red Team Tool Countermeasures: This rule identifies suspicious child processes of SolarWinds.Orion.Core.BusinessLayer.dll that may be evidence of the SUNBURST backdoor. The behavior of SolarWinds.Orion.Core.BusinessLayer.dll is dependent on per-enterprise configuration, so additional tuning may be required to exclude legitimate activity in a given environment. SUNBURST is a backdoor that has the ability to spawn and kill processes, write and delete files, set and create registry keys, gather system information, and disable a set of forensic analysis tools and services.
MATCH-S00480.normalized_summary: SUNBURST backdoor indicator detected on host: {{device_hostname}}
MATCH-S00480.summary_expression: SUNBURST backdoor indicator detected on host: {{device_hostname}}

MATCH-S00481.description_expression: From FireEye Red Team Tool Countermeasures: This rule identifies URL requests mimicking SolarWinds network traffic, to non-SolarWinds domains. This rule will only match on instances where communication does not occur over SSL/TLS. These requests may be evidence of the SUNBURST backdoor. SUNBURST is a backdoor that has the ability to spawn and kill processes, write and delete files, set and create registry keys, gather system information, and disable a set of forensic analysis tools and services.

MATCH-S00502.description_expression:
Observes for RDP traffic to hosts not within an allow list.

Note that this rule requires the creation and population of a match list for known OK hosts named 'RDP_Hosts'
MATCH-S00502.expression:
dstPort = '3389'
AND dstDevice_ip_isInternal = true
AND success = true
AND NOT array_contains(listMatches,'RDP_Hosts')
MATCH-S00502.name: RDP Traffic to Unexpected Host
MATCH-S00502.normalized_description:
Observes for RDP traffic to hosts not within an allow list.

Note that this rule requires the creation and population of a match list for known OK hosts named 'RDP_Hosts'

MATCH-S00506.description_expression: Observes for command line arguments with sc.exe indicating Windows services being modified
MATCH-S00506.expression:
lower(baseImage) like '%sc.exe'
AND 
(
       lower(commandLine) like '% create %'
    OR lower(commandLine) like '% config %'
)
MATCH-S00506.name: SC Exe Manipulating Windows Services
MATCH-S00506.normalized_description: Observes for command line arguments with sc.exe indicating Windows services being modified
MATCH-S00506.normalized_summary: SC.exe manipulating Windows services observed on host: {{device_hostname}}
MATCH-S00506.summary_expression: SC.exe manipulating Windows services observed on host: {{device_hostname}}

MATCH-S00507.description_expression: Observes for Spoolsv launching unexpected child processes. This may be related to behavior in CVE-2018-8440
MATCH-S00507.expression:
lower(parentBaseImage) LIKE '%spoolsv.exe'
AND NOT lower(baseImage) LIKE '%regsvr32.exe'
AND NOT lower(baseImage) LIKE '%splwow64.exe'
MATCH-S00507.name: Spoolsv Child Process Created
MATCH-S00507.normalized_description: Observes for Spoolsv launching unexpected child processes. This may be related to behavior in CVE-2018-8440

MATCH-S00508.description_expression: Observes for Zoom creating child processes
MATCH-S00508.expression:
(lower(parentBaseImage) = 'zoom.exe'
OR lower(parentBaseImage) = 'zoom.us'
OR lower(parentBaseImage) LIKE '%\zoom.exe'
OR lower(parentBaseImage) LIKE '%\zoom.us')
AND NOT lower(baseImage) = 'zoom.exe'
AND NOT lower(baseImage) = 'zoom.us'
AND NOT lower(baseImage) LIKE '%\zoom.exe'
AND NOT lower(baseImage) LIKE '%\zoom.us'
AND NOT lower(baseImage) = 'cpthost.exe'
AND NOT lower(baseImage) LIKE '%\cpthost.exe'
MATCH-S00508.name: Zoom Child Process
MATCH-S00508.normalized_description: Observes for Zoom creating child processes
MATCH-S00508.normalized_summary: Detected Zoom spawning child process: {{baseImage}} on host: {{device_hostname}}
MATCH-S00508.summary_expression: Detected Zoom spawning child process: {{baseImage}} on host: {{device_hostname}}

MATCH-S00509.description_expression: Observes for unexpected local logins. This rule includes 2 match lists to be populated with the active directory domains and permitted local accounts.
MATCH-S00509.expression:
(
    (metadata_deviceEventId = 'Security-4624'
    AND logonType in ('Interactive','RemoteInteractive'))
    OR
    (metadata_deviceEventId = 'Security-4776'
    AND errorCode = '0x0')
)
AND device_hostname = user_authDomain
AND NOT
(
       array_contains(listMatches,'allowed_local_accounts')
    OR array_contains(listMatches,'allowed_domains')
)
MATCH-S00509.name: Logon with Local Credentials
MATCH-S00509.normalized_description: Observes for unexpected local logins. This rule includes 2 match lists to be populated with the active directory domains and permitted local accounts.
MATCH-S00509.normalized_summary: Local logon from user: {{user_username}} on host: {{device_hostname}}
MATCH-S00509.summary_expression: Local logon from user: {{user_username}} on host: {{device_hostname}}

MATCH-S00513.description_expression: This rule looks for an intrusion product detecting a critical severity intrusion signature sourcing from an internal IP.
MATCH-S00513.expression:
threat_ruleType = 'intrusion'
AND normalizedSeverity = 10
AND srcDevice_ip_isInternal = true
AND NOT array_contains(listMatches, 'vuln_scanners')
MATCH-S00513.name: Critical Severity Intrusion Signature
MATCH-S00513.normalized_description: This rule looks for an intrusion product detecting a critical severity intrusion signature sourcing from an internal IP.

MATCH-S00513.summary_expression: Detected a critical severity intrusion: {{threat_name}} from IP: {{srcDevice_ip}}

MATCH-S00516.description_expression: Malware detected that is determined to be ransomware based on the signature/virus name.
MATCH-S00516.expression:
threat_ruleType = 'malware'
AND lower(threat_name) rlike ('ransom|crypt|lock')
AND NOT lower(threat_name) rlike ('block|currency')
MATCH-S00516.name: Antivirus Ransomware Detection
MATCH-S00516.normalized_description: Malware detected that is determined to be ransomware based on the signature/virus name.

MATCH-S00518.description_expression: Detects malware that failed to be cleaned.
MATCH-S00518.expression:
threat_ruleType = 'malware'
AND success = false
AND NOT lower(threat_name) like '%pua%'
AND NOT lower(threat_name) like '%pup%'
MATCH-S00518.name: Malware Not Cleaned
MATCH-S00518.normalized_description: Detects malware that failed to be cleaned.

MATCH-S00519.expression:
threat_ruleType = 'malware'
AND success = true
AND NOT lower(threat_name) like '%pua%'
AND NOT lower(threat_name) like '%pup%'
MATCH-S00519.name: Malware Cleaned

This could indicate an attacker attempting to bypass defenses on the target system to conduct further post-exploitation activity.

MATCH-S00522.description_expression: This rule looks for the creation of WMI temporary event subscriptions.
MATCH-S00522.expression:
metadata_vendor = 'Microsoft'
AND metadata_product = 'Windows'
AND metadata_deviceEventId = 'Microsoft-Windows-WMI-Activity/Operational-5860'
AND NOT (commandLine LIKE '%SELECT * FROM Win32_ProcessStartTrace WHERE ProcessName%'
        AND commandLine LIKE '%wsmprovhost.exe%')
AND NOT (commandLine LIKE '%SELECT * FROM __InstanceOperationEvent WHERE TargetInstance ISA %'
        AND (commandLine LIKE '%AntiVirusProduct%'
            OR commandLine LIKE '%FirewallProduct%'
            OR commandLine LIKE '%AntiSpywareProduct%'))
MATCH-S00522.name: WMI Temporary Event Subscription
MATCH-S00522.normalized_description: This rule looks for the creation of WMI temporary event subscriptions.
MATCH-S00522.normalized_summary: Detected WMI temporary event subscription on host: {{device_hostname}}
MATCH-S00522.summary_expression: Detected WMI temporary event subscription on host: {{device_hostname}}

MATCH-S00523.name: WMI Permanent Event Subscription

MATCH-S00525.description_expression: This rule detects credential dumping using copy command from a shadow copy.
MATCH-S00525.expression:
lower(baseImage) LIKE '%cmd.exe'
AND (lower(commandLine) LIKE '%\system32\config\sam\%'
    OR lower(commandLine) LIKE '%\system32\config\security\%'
    OR lower(commandLine) LIKE '%\system32\config\system\%'
    OR lower(commandLine) LIKE '%windows\ntds\ntds.dit%')
MATCH-S00525.name: Credential Dumping Via Copy Command From Shadow Copy

MATCH-S00525.summary_expression: User: {{user_username}} executed a command on host: {{device_hostname}}

MATCH-S00526.description_expression: This rule detects the creation of a symlink to a shadow copy.
MATCH-S00526.expression:
lower(baseImage) LIKE '%cmd.exe'
AND lower(commandLine) LIKE '%mklink%'
AND lower(commandLine) LIKE '%harddiskvolumeshadowcopy%'
MATCH-S00526.name: Credential Dumping Via Symlink To Shadow Copy

MATCH-S00526.normalized_description: This rule detects the creation of a symlink to a shadow copy.

MATCH-S00527.description_expression: The rule detects email files created outside the normal Outlook directory.
MATCH-S00527.expression:
(lower(baseImage) LIKE '%.pst'
    OR lower(baseImage) LIKE '%.ost'
    OR lower(file_basename) LIKE '%.pst'
    OR lower(file_basename) LIKE '%.ost') 
AND NOT lower(baseImage) LIKE '%\outlook files\%'
AND NOT lower(baseImage) LIKE '%appdata\local\microsoft\outlook%'
AND NOT lower(file_path) LIKE '%\outlook files\%'
AND NOT lower(file_path) LIKE '%appdata\local\microsoft\outlook%'
AND NOT lower(file_path) LIKE '%/outlook files/%'
AND NOT lower(file_path) LIKE '%documents/outlook%'
MATCH-S00527.name: Email Files Written Outside Of The Outlook Directory
MATCH-S00527.name_expression: Email Files Written Outside Of The Outlook Directory
MATCH-S00527.normalized_description: The rule detects email files created outside the normal Outlook directory.
MATCH-S00527.normalized_signal_name: Email Files Written Outside Of The Outlook Directory
MATCH-S00527.normalized_summary: Email file: {{file_basename}} written outside Outlook directory on host {{device_hostname}}
MATCH-S00527.summary_expression: Email file: {{file_basename}} written outside Outlook directory on host {{device_hostname}}

MATCH-S00528.description_expression: The rule looks for a file named "test.txt" written to the windows system directory tree, which is consistent with Samsam propagation.
MATCH-S00528.expression:
(lower(baseImage) LIKE '%\windows\system32\test.txt'
OR lower(file_basename) LIKE '%\windows\\system32\test.txt'
OR lower(file_path) LIKE '%\windows\system32\test.txt')
MATCH-S00528.name: Samsam Test File Write
MATCH-S00528.normalized_description: The rule looks for a file named "test.txt" written to the windows system directory tree, which is consistent with Samsam propagation.
MATCH-S00528.normalized_summary: Samsam ransomware indicator detected on host: {{device_hostname}}
MATCH-S00528.summary_expression: Samsam ransomware indicator detected on host: {{device_hostname}}

MATCH-S00530.expression:
lower(baseImage) LIKE '%schtasks.exe'
AND lower(commandLine) LIKE '%/create%'
AND lower(commandLine) LIKE '%shutdown%'

MATCH-S00531.description_expression: Attackers often disable security tools to avoid detection. This rule looks for the usage of process fltMC.exe to unload a Sysmon Driver that will stop sysmon from collecting the data.
MATCH-S00531.expression:
lower(baseImage) LIKE '%fltmc.exe'
AND lower(commandLine) LIKE '%unload%'
AND lower(commandLine) LIKE '%sysmondrv%'
MATCH-S00531.name: Unload Sysmon Filter Driver
MATCH-S00531.normalized_description: Attackers often disable security tools to avoid detection. This rule looks for the usage of process fltMC.exe to unload a Sysmon Driver that will stop sysmon from collecting the data.
MATCH-S00531.normalized_summary: Attempt to unload Sysmon filter driver on host: {{device_hostname}}
MATCH-S00531.summary_expression: Attempt to unload Sysmon filter driver on host: {{device_hostname}}

MATCH-S00532.description_expression: This rule looks for the execution of adfind.exe with command-line arguments that it uses by default. Specifically the filter or search functions. This has been seen used before by Wizard Spider, FIN6 and actors whom also launched SUNBURST. AdFind.exe is usually used a recon tool to enumare a domain controller.
MATCH-S00532.expression:
(lower(commandLine) LIKE '% -f %' OR lower(commandLine) LIKE '% -b %')
AND (lower(commandLine) LIKE '%objectcategory%'
    OR lower(commandLine) LIKE '% -gcb %'
    OR lower(commandLine) LIKE '% -sc %')
MATCH-S00532.name: Windows Adfind Exe
MATCH-S00532.normalized_description: This rule looks for the execution of adfind.exe with command-line arguments that it uses by default. Specifically the filter or search functions. This has been seen used before by Wizard Spider, FIN6 and actors whom also launched SUNBURST. AdFind.exe is usually used a recon tool to enumare a domain controller.

MATCH-S00533.description_expression: The rule looks for a Windows Security Account Manager (SAM) was stopped via command-line. This is consistent with Ryuk infections across a fleet of endpoints.
MATCH-S00533.expression:
(lower(baseImage) LIKE '%net.exe'
    OR lower(baseImage) LIKE '%net1.exe')
AND (lower(commandLine) LIKE '%stop samss%'
    OR lower(commandLine) LIKE '%stop "samss"%')
MATCH-S00533.name: Windows Security Account Manager Stopped
MATCH-S00533.normalized_description: The rule looks for a Windows Security Account Manager (SAM) was stopped via command-line. This is consistent with Ryuk infections across a fleet of endpoints.
MATCH-S00533.normalized_summary: Detected Windows Security Account Manager being stopped on host: {{device_hostname}}
MATCH-S00533.summary_expression: Detected Windows Security Account Manager being stopped on host: {{device_hostname}}

MATCH-S00534.description_expression: This rule looks for processes referencing the plist files that determine which applications are re-opened when a user reboots their machine. This may also detect commands associated with login hooks being executed.
MATCH-S00534.expression:
lower(commandLine) LIKE '%com.apple.loginwindow%'
AND NOT (
   lower(commandLine) LIKE '% read %'
OR lower(commandLine) LIKE '%lwweeklymessagetracer%'
)
MATCH-S00534.name: MacOS - Re-Opened Applications
MATCH-S00534.normalized_description: This rule looks for processes referencing the plist files that determine which applications are re-opened when a user reboots their machine. This may also detect commands associated with login hooks being executed.

MATCH-S00535.description_expression:
Observes for multiple methods of certificate export which may indicate that an attacker is attempting to bypass multifactor authentication using a stolen certificate.

This rule utilizes indicators from Windows command line auditing, PowerShell auditing, and Sysmon named pipe connections.
MATCH-S00535.expression:
commandLine like '%Export-PfxCertificate%'
OR commandLine like '%certutil -exportPFX%'
OR baseImage like '%\microsoft##wid\tsql\query%'
MATCH-S00535.name: Golden SAML Indicator : Certificate Export
MATCH-S00535.normalized_description:
Observes for multiple methods of certificate export which may indicate that an attacker is attempting to bypass multifactor authentication using a stolen certificate.

This rule utilizes indicators from Windows command line auditing, PowerShell auditing, and Sysmon named pipe connections.
MATCH-S00535.normalized_summary: Certificate export observed on host: {{device_hostname}} by user: {{user_username}}
MATCH-S00535.summary_expression: Certificate export observed on host: {{device_hostname}} by user: {{user_username}}

MATCH-S00536.summary_expression: Windows Firewall rule modified on host: {{device_hostname}}

MATCH-S00539.description_expression: This rule looks for suspicious processes on all systems labeled as web servers. A list of web servers should be populated in order to enable the rule.
MATCH-S00539.expression:
array_contains(listMatches, 'web_servers')
AND (lower(commandLine) LIKE '%whoami%'
    OR lower(commandLine) LIKE '%ping%'
    OR lower(commandLine) LIKE '%iptables%'
    OR lower(commandLine) LIKE '%wget%'
    OR lower(commandLine) LIKE '%service%'
    OR lower(commandLine) LIKE '%curl%')
MATCH-S00539.name: Web Servers Executing Suspicious Processes
MATCH-S00539.normalized_description: This rule looks for suspicious processes on all systems labeled as web servers. A list of web servers should be populated in order to enable the rule.
MATCH-S00539.normalized_summary: Suspicious process detected on host: {{device_hostname}} designated a web server
MATCH-S00539.summary_expression: Suspicious process detected on host: {{device_hostname}} designated a web server

MATCH-S00540.description_expression: Enforcing network-access controls is one of the defensive mechanisms used by cloud administrators to restrict access to a cloud instance. After the attacker has gained control of the console by compromising an admin account, they can delete a network ACL and gain access to the instance from anywhere.
MATCH-S00540.expression:
metadata_vendor = 'Amazon AWS'
AND metadata_product = 'CloudTrail'
AND (lower(metadata_deviceEventId) LIKE 'deletenetworkacl%'
    OR lower(action) LIKE 'deletenetworkacl%')
MATCH-S00540.name: AWS CloudTrail Network Access Control List Deleted
MATCH-S00540.name_expression: AWS CloudTrail Network Access Control List Deleted
MATCH-S00540.normalized_description: Enforcing network-access controls is one of the defensive mechanisms used by cloud administrators to restrict access to a cloud instance. After the attacker has gained control of the console by compromising an admin account, they can delete a network ACL and gain access to the instance from anywhere.
MATCH-S00540.normalized_signal_name: AWS CloudTrail Network Access Control List Deleted

MATCH-S00541.description_expression: The rule looks for a batch file (.bat) written to the Windows system directory tree.
MATCH-S00541.expression:
metadata_vendor = 'Microsoft'
AND metadata_product = 'Windows'
AND metadata_deviceEventId = 'Microsoft-Windows-Sysmon/Operational-11'
AND lower(file_path) like '%system32%'
AND lower(file_path) like '%.bat'
MATCH-S00541.name: Batch File Write To System32
MATCH-S00541.normalized_description: The rule looks for a batch file (.bat) written to the Windows system directory tree.

MATCH-S00542.description_expression: Most login failures are due to failed passwords. Login failure to sensitive systems where the users simply aren't authorized, though, can indicate malicious intent.
MATCH-S00542.expression:
metadata_vendor = 'Microsoft'
AND metadata_product = 'Windows'
AND (errorCode = '0xc000015b'
    OR lower(fields['insertionstrings_f10']) = '0xc000015b')
MATCH-S00542.name: Unauthorized Access Attempt Detected
MATCH-S00542.normalized_description: Most login failures are due to failed passwords. Login failure to sensitive systems where the users simply aren't authorized, though, can indicate malicious intent.
MATCH-S00542.normalized_summary: Unauthorized access attempts from user: {{user_username}} on host: {{device_hostname}}
MATCH-S00542.summary_expression: Unauthorized access attempts from user: {{user_username}} on host: {{device_hostname}}

MATCH-S00543.description_expression: This rule looks for events where PsExec.exe is run with the accepteula flag in the command line. PsExec is a built-in Windows utility that enables you to execute processes on other systems. It is fully interactive for console applications. This tool is widely used for launching interactive command prompts on remote systems. Threat actors leverage this extensively for executing code on compromised systems. If an attacker is running PsExec for the first time, they will be prompted to accept the end-user license agreement (EULA), which can be passed as the argument accepteula within the command line.
MATCH-S00543.name: Detect Psexec With Accepteula Flag
MATCH-S00543.normalized_description: This rule looks for events where PsExec.exe is run with the accepteula flag in the command line. PsExec is a built-in Windows utility that enables you to execute processes on other systems. It is fully interactive for console applications. This tool is widely used for launching interactive command prompts on remote systems. Threat actors leverage this extensively for executing code on compromised systems. If an attacker is running PsExec for the first time, they will be prompted to accept the end-user license agreement (EULA), which can be passed as the argument accepteula within the command line.
MATCH-S00543.normalized_summary: PSExec has been executed on host: {{device_hostname}} by user: {{user_username}}
MATCH-S00543.summary_expression: PSExec has been executed on host: {{device_hostname}} by user: {{user_username}}

MATCH-S00544.description_expression: The rule looks for modifications to registry keys that control the enforcement of Windows User Account Control (UAC).
MATCH-S00544.expression:
metadata_vendor = 'Microsoft'
AND metadata_product = 'Windows'
AND metadata_deviceEventId IN ('Microsoft-Windows-Sysmon/Operational-13','Security-4657')
AND lower(file_path) LIKE '%microsoft\windows\currentversion\policies\system\enablelua%'
AND (fields['insertionstrings_f08'] = 'DWORD (0x00000000)' OR fields['EventData.Details'] = 'DWORD (0x00000000)' OR fields['EventData.ObjectValueName'] = 'DWORD (0x00000000)' OR (fields['EventData.NewValueType'] = 'DWORD' AND fields['EventData.NewValue'] = '0x00000000'))

MATCH-S00556.commit_message: Added missing parenthesis to match expression
MATCH-S00556.description_expression: This rule detects commonly used data transfer protocols being used over non-standard ports, which could indicate an attempt to obfuscate exfiltration or command and control activity.
MATCH-S00556.expression:
(
   (
    dstDevice_ip_isInternal = false
    OR array_contains(listMatches, 'proxy_servers_dst')
   )
AND  (   srcDevice_ip_isInternal = true
     AND NOT array_contains(listMatches, 'business_ips'))    )
AND (
((lower(application) = 'ssh' OR lower(application) = 'sftp') AND NOT dstPort = 22)
OR (lower(application) = 'telnet' AND NOT dstPort = 23)
OR (lower(application) = 'irc' AND NOT (dstPort = 7000 OR (dstPort >= 6665 AND dstPort <=6669)))
OR (lower(application) = 'smtp' AND NOT (dstPort = 25 OR dstPort = 587))
)
MATCH-S00556.name: Outbound Data Transfer Protocol Over Non-standard Port
MATCH-S00556.name_expression: Outbound Data Transfer Protocol Over Non-standard Port
MATCH-S00556.normalized_description: This rule detects commonly used data transfer protocols being used over non-standard ports, which could indicate an attempt to obfuscate exfiltration or command and control activity.
MATCH-S00556.normalized_signal_name: Outbound Data Transfer Protocol Over Non-standard Port
MATCH-S00556.normalized_summary: Source IP {{srcDevice_ip}} communicated with {{dstDevice_ip}} using {{application}} over non-standard port {{dstPort}}.
MATCH-S00556.summary_expression: Source IP {{srcDevice_ip}} communicated with {{dstDevice_ip}} using {{application}} over non-standard port {{dstPort}}.

MATCH-S00557.description_expression: This rule detects HTTP requests sent directly to IP addresses, bypassing DNS. This could indicate an attacker is trying to circumvent detection mechanisms.
MATCH-S00557.expression:
lower(http_method) IN ('get','post','put','options')
AND lower(http_url) rlike '^(https?:\/\/)?(?<![0-9])(?:(?:25[0-5]|2[0-4][0-9]|[0-1]?[0-9]{1,2})[.](?:25[0-5]|2[0-4][0-9]|[0-1]?[0-9]{1,2})[.](?:25[0-5]|2[0-4][0-9]|[0-1]?[0-9]{1,2})[.](?:25[0-5]|2[0-4][0-9]|[0-1]?[0-9]{1,2}))(?![0-9])\/'
AND srcDevice_ip_isInternal = true
AND (
        dstDevice_ip_isInternal = false
        OR array_contains(listMatches, 'proxy_servers_dst')
    )
AND NOT (array_contains(listMatches, 'guest_networks') OR array_contains(listMatches, 'business_ips') OR array_contains(listMatches, 'verified_ips'))
MATCH-S00557.name: Web Request to IP Address
MATCH-S00557.normalized_description: This rule detects HTTP requests sent directly to IP addresses, bypassing DNS. This could indicate an attacker is trying to circumvent detection mechanisms.

MATCH-S00558.description_expression: Observes for successful TCP traffic to default VNC ports or explicit VNC/RFB traffic detected
MATCH-S00558.expression:
objectType like 'Network%'
AND srcDevice_ip_isInternal = False 
AND success = True 
AND 
(
  (
  lower(ipProtocol) in ('tcp','6') and dstPort in (5500,5800,5900)
  )
OR
  (
     lower(application) like '%vnc%'
  OR lower(application) = 'rfb'
  )
)

MATCH-S00560.description_expression: Observes for SMTP traffic on non-SMTP hosts. This may be indicative of spambot activity. This rule requires that a match list of known OK SMTP servers is populated.

        lower(ipProtocol) in ('6','tcp')

    )
)
AND NOT array_contains(listMatches,'smtp_servers')

MATCH-S00579.summary_expression: Observed potential malicious pipe with name {{resource}}  on {{device_hostname}}

MATCH-S00580.description_expression: Backdooring domain object to grant the rights associated with DCSync to a regular user or machine account using Powerview\Add-DomainObjectAcl DCSync Extended Right cmdlet, will allow to re-obtain the pwd hashes of any user/computer
MATCH-S00580.expression:
metadata_vendor = 'Microsoft'
AND metadata_product = 'Windows'
AND metadata_deviceEventId = 'Security-5136'
AND fields['EventData.AttributeLDAPDisplayName'] = 'ntSecurityDescriptor'
AND 
(
       fields['EventData.AttributeValue'] like '%1131f6ad-9c07-11d1-f79f-00c04fc2dcd2%'
   AND fields['EventData.AttributeValue'] like '%1131f6aa-9c07-11d1-f79f-00c04fc2dcd2%'
   AND fields['EventData.AttributeValue'] like '%89e95b76-444d-4c62-991a-0facbeda640c%'
)
MATCH-S00580.name: Powerview Add-DomainObjectAcl DCSync AD Extend Right
MATCH-S00580.name_expression: Powerview Add-DomainObjectAcl DCSync AD Extend Right
MATCH-S00580.normalized_description: Backdooring domain object to grant the rights associated with DCSync to a regular user or machine account using Powerview\Add-DomainObjectAcl DCSync Extended Right cmdlet, will allow to re-obtain the pwd hashes of any user/computer
MATCH-S00580.normalized_signal_name: Powerview Add-DomainObjectAcl DCSync AD Extend Right
MATCH-S00580.normalized_summary: Detected Powerview Add-DomainObjectAcl DCSync AD Extend Right use on {{device_hostname}}
MATCH-S00580.summary_expression: Detected Powerview Add-DomainObjectAcl DCSync AD Extend Right use on {{device_hostname}}

MATCH-S00586.description_expression: Detects LSASS process access by LaZagne for credential dumping
MATCH-S00586.expression:
metadata_vendor = 'Microsoft'
AND metadata_product = 'Windows'
AND metadata_deviceEventId = 'Microsoft-Windows-Sysmon/Operational-10'
AND fields['EventData.TargetImage'] like '%\lsass.exe'
AND fields['EventData.CallTrace'] like '%:\Windows\SYSTEM32\ntdll.dll+%|%:\Windows\System32\KERNELBASE.dll+%_ctypes.pyd+%python27.dll+%'
AND fields['EventData.GrantedAccess'] = '0x1FFFFF'

MATCH-S00587.description_expression: Observes for several command line parameters associated with Empire PowerShell
MATCH-S00587.expression:
lower(commandLine) like '% -nop -sta -noni -w hidden -enc %'
OR lower(commandLine) like '% -noo -sta -w 1 -enc %'
OR lower(commandLine) like '% -nop -noni -w hidden -enc %'
OR lower(commandLine) like '% -nop -sta -w 1 -enc%'
OR lower(commandLine) like '% -enc  sqb%'
OR lower(commandLine) like '% -nop -exec bypass -encodedcommand sqb%'

MATCH-S00587.normalized_summary: Observed Empire PowerShell launch parameters on {{device_hostname}}

MATCH-S00594.description_expression: Alibaba KMS is an encryption and key management web service.  Besides encrypting and decrypting data, users and administrators can use this service to create keys, manage keys etc.  This signal indicates activity that enables and disables keys explicitly.  This activity has been surveyed to be a low volume event and could be considered suspicious given other activity involving the entity.  Additionally, monitoring for these events is required to achieve certain industry audit compliance.
MATCH-S00594.expression:
metadata_vendor = 'Alibaba'
AND metadata_product = 'ActionTrail'
AND action IN ('EnableKey','DisableKey')
and NOT array_contains(listMatches, 'Alibaba_admin_ips')
and NOT array_contains(listMatches, 'Alibaba_admin_users')
MATCH-S00594.name: Alibaba ActionTrail KMS Activity
MATCH-S00594.normalized_description: Alibaba KMS is an encryption and key management web service.  Besides encrypting and decrypting data, users and administrators can use this service to create keys, manage keys etc.  This signal indicates activity that enables and disables keys explicitly.  This activity has been surveyed to be a low volume event and could be considered suspicious given other activity involving the entity.  Additionally, monitoring for these events is required to achieve certain industry audit compliance.
MATCH-S00594.normalized_summary: User: {{user_username}} from source IP: {{srcDevice_ip}} performed a {{action}} action
MATCH-S00594.summary_expression: User: {{user_username}} from source IP: {{srcDevice_ip}} performed a {{action}} action

MATCH-S00595.description_expression: Detects suspicious requests to Telegram API without the usual Telegram User-Agent
MATCH-S00595.expression:
(
       dns_query like '%api.telegram.org%'
    OR dns_queryDomain = 'api.telegram.org'  
)
AND
(
    lower(http_userAgent) not like '%telegram%'
AND lower(http_userAgent) not like '%bot%'
)

MATCH-S00595.normalized_description: Detects suspicious requests to Telegram API without the usual Telegram User-Agent

MATCH-S00595.summary_expression: Observed Telegram API access from non-Telegram user agent from IP: {{srcDevice_ip}}

Several Secrets Manager API actions are included and assessed as sensitive.

metadata_vendor = 'Alibaba'
AND metadata_product = 'ActionTrail'

MATCH-S00612.description_expression: Administrative changes to the GCP Secrets Manager aren't overtly hostile, but are generally low volume and can be considered sensitive. These signals highlight when these actions occur and can be used in context of other suspicious activity to raise the risk of a hostile entity. Several Secrets Manager API actions are included and assessed as sensitive.
MATCH-S00612.expression:
metadata_vendor = 'Google'
AND metadata_product = 'Google Cloud Platform'
AND description = 'type.googleapis.com/google.cloud.audit.AuditLog'
AND (lower(action) like '%updatesecret%'
    OR lower(action) like '%disablesecret%'
    OR lower(action) like '%deletesecret%'
    OR lower(action) like '%destroysecret%'
    OR lower(action) like '%getsecret%'
    OR lower(action) like '%listsecret%')
AND NOT array_contains(listMatches, 'gcp_admin')
MATCH-S00612.name: GCP Audit Secrets Manager Activity
MATCH-S00612.normalized_description: Administrative changes to the GCP Secrets Manager aren't overtly hostile, but are generally low volume and can be considered sensitive. These signals highlight when these actions occur and can be used in context of other suspicious activity to raise the risk of a hostile entity. Several Secrets Manager API actions are included and assessed as sensitive.
