MATCH-S00614.description_expression: GCP KMS is an encryption and key management web service. Besides encrypting and decrypting data, users and administrators can use this service to create keys, manage keys etc. This signal indicates activity that enables and disables keys explicitly. This activity has been surveyed to be a low volume event and could be considered suspicious given other activity involving the entity. Additionally, monitoring for these events is required to achieve certain industry audit compliance.
MATCH-S00614.expression:
metadata_vendor = 'Google'
AND metadata_product = 'Google Cloud Platform'
AND application = 'cloudkms.googleapis.com'
AND (lower(action) like '%restorekeyversion%'
    OR lower(action) like '%enablekeyversion%'
    OR lower(action) like '%createkeyring%'
    OR lower(action) like '%createkeyasymmetricdecrypt%'
    OR lower(action) like '%createkeyasymmetricsign%'
    OR lower(action) like '%createkeysymmetricencryptdecrypt%')
AND NOT array_contains(listMatches, 'gcp_admin')

Besides encrypting and decrypting data, users and administrators can use this service to create keys, manage keys etc. This signal indicates activity that enables and disables keys explicitly. This activity has been surveyed to be a low volume event and could be considered suspicious given other activity involving the entity. MATCH-S00615.description_expression: This signal fires for all observances of the CreateServiceAccount action in the IAM event source. Hostile actors will create service accounts to persist access. Use this signal in context of other activity to determine intent.
MATCH-S00615.expression:
metadata_vendor = 'Google'
AND metadata_product = 'Google Cloud Platform'
AND description = 'type.googleapis.com/google.cloud.audit.AuditLog'
AND lower(action) like '%createserviceaccount%'
AND NOT array_contains(listMatches, 'gcp_admin')
MATCH-S00615.name: GCP Audit IAM CreateServiceAccount Observed
MATCH-S00615.normalized_description: This signal fires for all observances of the CreateServiceAccount action in the IAM event source. Hostile actors will create service accounts to persist access. Use this signal in context of other activity to determine intent.

MATCH-S00616.description_expression: A GCP request occurred to either create a new public or open bucket. While there are some use cases for GCP S3 public buckets, most are generally private. The security operations center should have a strong understanding of which buckets are allowed to be public.
MATCH-S00616.expression:
metadata_vendor = 'Google'
AND metadata_product = 'Google Cloud Platform'
AND action = 'storage.setIamPermissions'
AND fields['message.data.resource.type'] = 'gcs_bucket'
AND ((fields['message.data.protoPayload.serviceData.policyDelta.bindingDeltas{}.member'] = 'allUsers'
AND fields['message.data.protoPayload.serviceData.policyDelta.bindingDeltas{}.action'] = 'ADD')
    OR
    (fields['message.data.protoPayload.serviceData.policyDelta.bindingDeltas.1.action'] = 'ADD'
    AND fields['message.data.protoPayload.serviceData.policyDelta.bindingDeltas.1.member'] = 'allUsers'))
MATCH-S00616.name: GCP Bucket Open
MATCH-S00616.normalized_description: A GCP request occurred to either create a new public or open bucket. While there are some use cases for GCP S3 public buckets, most are generally private. The security operations center should have a strong understanding of which buckets are allowed to be public.

MATCH-S00617.description_expression: Generates a signal when the GCPloit exploitation framework is detected. This framework can be used to escalate privileges and move laterally from compromised high privilege accounts.
MATCH-S00617.expression:
metadata_vendor = 'Google'
AND metadata_product = 'Google Cloud Platform'
AND fields['message.data.protoPayload.request.function.timeout'] = '539s'
MATCH-S00617.name: GCP GCPloit Exploitation Framework Used
MATCH-S00617.normalized_description: Generates a signal when the GCPloit exploitation framework is detected. This framework can be used to escalate privileges and move laterally from compromised high privilege accounts.

MATCH-S00619.description_expression: Detect when an administrative change to a GCS Bucket has been made. This could change the retention policy or bucket lock.

MATCH-S00620.description_expression: Detect when a Cloud SQL DB has been modified.

AND NOT array_contains(listMatches, 'gcp_admin')

MATCH-S00621.normalized_description: Detect when a firewall rule is created, modified or deleted.

MATCH-S00629.commit_message: Add alias for Google Workspace to (hopefully) support G Suite as a valid Product name (#5890)
MATCH-S00629.description_expression: Identifies when a service account is disabled in Google Cloud Platform (GCP). A service account is a special type of account used by an application or a virtual machine (VM) instance, not a person. Applications use service accounts to make authorized API calls, authorized as either the service account itself, or as Google Workspace or Cloud Identity users through domain-wide delegation. An adversary may disable a service account in order to disrupt to disrupt their target's business operations.
MATCH-S00629.expression:
metadata_vendor = 'Google'
AND metadata_product = 'Google Cloud Platform'
AND action LIKE '%.DisableServiceAccount'
AND NOT array_contains(listMatches, 'gcp_admin')
MATCH-S00629.name: GCP Audit IAM DisableServiceAccount Observed
MATCH-S00629.normalized_description: Identifies when a service account is disabled in Google Cloud Platform (GCP). A service account is a special type of account used by an application or a virtual machine (VM) instance, not a person. Applications use service accounts to make authorized API calls, authorized as either the service account itself, or as Google Workspace or Cloud Identity users through domain-wide delegation. An adversary may disable a service account in order to disrupt to disrupt their target's business operations.

MATCH-S00637.description_expression: This rule triggers any time there is potentially a download from a suspicious categorized site.
MATCH-S00637.expression:
(lower(http_category) like '%malicious%'
    OR lower(http_category) like '%hacking%'
    OR lower(http_category) like '%phishing%'
    OR lower(http_category) like '%illegal%'
    OR lower(http_category) like '%exploit%')
AND (lower(http_url_path) like '%.ps1'
    OR lower(http_url_path) like '%.sys'
    OR lower(http_url_path) like '%.bat'
    OR lower(http_url_path) like '%.scr'
    OR lower(http_url_path) like '%.vbs'
    OR lower(http_url_path) like '%.vba'
    OR lower(http_url_path) like '%.dll'
    OR lower(http_url_path) like '%.exe')
MATCH-S00637.name: Possible Malicious Download
MATCH-S00637.normalized_description: This rule triggers any time there is potentially a download from a suspicious categorized site.
MATCH-S00637.normalized_summary: File download from site categorized as {{http_category}} with URL: {{http_url}}
MATCH-S00637.summary_expression: File download from site categorized as {{http_category}} with URL: {{http_url}}

MATCH-S00638.description_expression: This rule triggers any time there is a Suspicious or Malicious McAfee Web Gateway category which could indicate there is a problem with the host making the connection.
MATCH-S00638.expression:
metadata_vendor = 'McAfee'
AND metadata_product = 'Web Gateway'
AND (lower(http_category) like '%malicious%'
    OR lower(http_category) like '%hacking%'
    OR lower(http_category) like '%phishing%'
    OR lower(http_category) like '%illegal%'
    OR lower(http_category) like '%exploit%'
    OR lower(http_category) like '%uncategorized%')
MATCH-S00638.name: McAfee Web Gateway - Suspicious or Malicious Categories
MATCH-S00638.name_expression: McAfee Web Gateway - Suspicious or Malicious Categories
MATCH-S00638.normalized_description: This rule triggers any time there is a Suspicious or Malicious McAfee Web Gateway category which could indicate there is a problem with the host making the connection.
MATCH-S00638.normalized_signal_name: McAfee Web Gateway - Suspicious or Malicious Categories
MATCH-S00638.normalized_summary: Web traffic with category {{http_category}} was found for URL: {{http_url}}
MATCH-S00638.summary_expression: Web traffic with category {{http_category}} was found for URL: {{http_url}}

MATCH-S00639.description_expression: Detect when an unauthenticated request user is permitted in Kubernetes.
MATCH-S00639.expression:
metadata_vendor = 'Kubernetes'
AND metadata_product = 'Kubernetes'
AND lower(user_username) LIKE '%anonymous%'
AND http_response_statusCode BETWEEN 100 AND 299
MATCH-S00639.name: Kubernetes Anonymous Request Authorized
MATCH-S00639.normalized_description: Detect when an unauthenticated request user is permitted in Kubernetes.

MATCH-S00640.description_expression: Detect when a user is creating a pod in one of the Kubernetes default namespaces. The only users creating pods in the kube-system namespace should be cluster administrators. Furthermore, it is best practice to not run any cluster critical infrastructure in the kube-system namespace. The kube-public namespace is intended for Kubernetes objects which should be readable by unauthenticated users. Thus, a pod should likely not be created in the kube-public namespace.
MATCH-S00640.expression:
metadata_vendor = 'Kubernetes'
AND metadata_product = 'Kubernetes'
AND metadata_deviceEventId = 'create'
AND lower(resource) = 'pods'
AND lower(device_k8s_namespace) IN ('kube-system','kube-public')
MATCH-S00640.name: Kubernetes Pod Created in Kube Namespace
MATCH-S00640.name_expression: Kubernetes Pod Created in Kube Namespace
MATCH-S00640.normalized_description: Detect when a user is creating a pod in one of the Kubernetes default namespaces. The only users creating pods in the kube-system namespace should be cluster administrators. Furthermore, it is best practice to not run any cluster critical infrastructure in the kube-system namespace. The kube-public namespace is intended for Kubernetes objects which should be readable by unauthenticated users. Thus, a pod should likely not be created in the kube-public namespace.
MATCH-S00640.normalized_signal_name: Kubernetes Pod Created in Kube Namespace

MATCH-S00641.description_expression: Detect when a pod is attached to the host network. Attaching the hostNetwork permits a pod to access the node’s network adapter allowing a pod to listen to all network traffic for all pods on the node and communicate with other pods on the network namespace.
MATCH-S00641.expression:
metadata_vendor = 'Kubernetes'
AND metadata_product = 'Kubernetes'
AND metadata_deviceEventId = 'create'
AND lower(resource) = 'pods'
AND fields['message.requestObject.spec.hostNetwork'] = 'true'
MATCH-S00641.name: Kubernetes Pod Created with hostNetwork
MATCH-S00641.normalized_description: Detect when a pod is attached to the host network. Attaching the hostNetwork permits a pod to access the node’s network adapter allowing a pod to listen to all network traffic for all pods on the node and communicate with other pods on the network namespace.

MATCH-S00642.description_expression: Detect when a user is creating a service account in one of the Kubernetes default namespaces. The only users creating service accounts in the kube-system namespace should be cluster administrators. Furthermore, it is best practice to not run any cluster critical infrastructure in the kube-system namespace. The kube-public namespace is intended for kubernetes objects which should be readable by unauthenticated users. Thus, a service account should likely not be created in the kube-public namespace.
MATCH-S00642.expression:
metadata_vendor = 'Kubernetes'
AND metadata_product = 'Kubernetes'
AND metadata_deviceEventId = 'create'
AND lower(resource) = 'serviceaccounts'
AND lower(device_k8s_namespace) IN ('kube-system','kube-public')
MATCH-S00642.name: Kubernetes Service Account Created in Kube Namespace
MATCH-S00642.name_expression: Kubernetes Service Account Created in Kube Namespace

The kube-public namespace is intended for kubernetes objects which should be readable by unauthenticated users. Thus, a service account should likely not be created in the kube-public namespace.
MATCH-S00642.normalized_signal_name: Kubernetes Service Account Created in Kube Namespace

MATCH-S00643.description_expression: Detect when a service’s port is attached to the node’s IP. Exposing the service’s port to the the node’s IP allows other hosts on the network namespace to access this service.
MATCH-S00643.expression:
metadata_vendor = 'Kubernetes'
AND metadata_product = 'Kubernetes'
AND metadata_deviceEventId = 'create'
AND lower(resource) = 'services'
AND fields['message.requestObject.spec.type'] = 'NodePort'

MATCH-S00643.normalized_description: Detect when a service’s port is attached to the node’s IP. Exposing the service’s port to the the node’s IP allows other hosts on the network namespace to access this service.

MATCH-S00644.summary_expression: Action: {{action}} performed by user: {{user_username}} from IP: {{srcDevice_ip}}

MATCH-S00645.description_expression: Detect when a user attaches to a pod. A user should not need to attach to a pod. Attaching to a pod allows a user to attach to any process in a running container which may give an attacker access to sensitive data.
MATCH-S00645.expression:
metadata_vendor = 'Kubernetes'
AND metadata_product = 'Kubernetes'
AND lower(resource) = 'pods'
AND lower(changeType) = 'attach'
MATCH-S00645.name: Kubernetes User Attached to a Pod
MATCH-S00645.name_expression: Kubernetes User Attached to a Pod
MATCH-S00645.normalized_description: Detect when a user attaches to a pod. A user should not need to attach to a pod. Attaching to a pod allows a user to attach to any process in a running container which may give an attacker access to sensitive data.

MATCH-S00645.normalized_summary: User: {{user_username}} attached themselves to a pod.
MATCH-S00645.summary_expression: User: {{user_username}} attached themselves to a pod.

MATCH-S00646.description_expression: Detect when a user execs into a pod. A user should not need to exec into a pod. Execing into a pod allows a user to execute any process in container which is not already running. It is most common to execute the bash process to gain an interactive shell. If this is an attacker, they can access any data which the pod has permissions to, including secrets.
MATCH-S00646.expression:
metadata_vendor = 'Kubernetes'
AND metadata_product = 'Kubernetes'
AND lower(resource) = 'pods'
AND lower(changeType) = 'exec'
MATCH-S00646.name: Kubernetes User Exec into a Pod
MATCH-S00646.name_expression: Kubernetes User Exec into a Pod
MATCH-S00646.normalized_description: Detect when a user execs into a pod. A user should not need to exec into a pod. Execing into a pod allows a user to execute any process in container which is not already running. It is most common to execute the bash process to gain an interactive shell. If this is an attacker, they can access any data which the pod has permissions to, including secrets.
MATCH-S00646.normalized_signal_name: Kubernetes User Exec into a Pod

MATCH-S00648.description_expression: Detects kubectl used to list Kubernetes secrets. A Kubernetes secret is an object that lets users store and manage sensitive information, such as passwords and connection strings in the cluster. Secrets can be consumed by reference in the pod configuration. Attackers who have permissions to retrieve the secrets from the API server (by using the pod service account, for example) can access sensitive information that might include credentials to various services.
MATCH-S00648.name: Kubernetes ListSecrets
MATCH-S00648.normalized_description: Detects kubectl used to list Kubernetes secrets. A Kubernetes secret is an object that lets users store and manage sensitive information, such as passwords and connection strings in the cluster. Secrets can be consumed by reference in the pod configuration. Attackers who have permissions to retrieve the secrets from the API server (by using the pod service account, for example) can access sensitive information that might include credentials to various services.
MATCH-S00648.normalized_summary: User: {{user_username}} ran a command to get secrets using kubectl
MATCH-S00648.summary_expression: User: {{user_username}} ran a command to get secrets using kubectl

MATCH-S00650.description_expression: Detects kubectl used to list Kubernetes cronjobs. Kubernetes Job is a controller that creates one or more pods and ensures that a specified number of them successfully terminate. Kubernetes Job can be used to run containers that perform finite tasks for batch jobs. Kubernetes CronJob is used to schedule Jobs. Attackers may use Kubernetes CronJob for scheduling execution of malicious code that would run as a container in the cluster.
MATCH-S00650.name: Kubernetes ListCronjobs
MATCH-S00650.normalized_description: Detects kubectl used to list Kubernetes cronjobs. Kubernetes Job is a controller that creates one or more pods and ensures that a specified number of them successfully terminate. Kubernetes Job can be used to run containers that perform finite tasks for batch jobs. Kubernetes CronJob is used to schedule Jobs. Attackers may use Kubernetes CronJob for scheduling execution of malicious code that would run as a container in the cluster.
MATCH-S00650.normalized_summary: User: {{user_username}} ran a command using kubectl to get cronjobs
MATCH-S00650.summary_expression: User: {{user_username}} ran a command using kubectl to get cronjobs

MATCH-S00651.description_expression: Detects kubectl used to create Kubernetes cronjobs. Kubernetes Job is a controller that creates one or more pods and ensures that a specified number of them successfully terminate. Kubernetes Job can be used to run containers that perform finite tasks for batch jobs. Kubernetes CronJob is used to schedule Jobs. Attackers may use Kubernetes CronJob for scheduling execution of malicious code that would run as a container in the cluster.
MATCH-S00651.expression:
lower(commandLine) LIKE '%kubectl create%'
AND lower(commandLine) LIKE '%cronjob%'
MATCH-S00651.name: Kubernetes CreateCronjob
MATCH-S00651.normalized_description: Detects kubectl used to create Kubernetes cronjobs. Kubernetes Job is a controller that creates one or more pods and ensures that a specified number of them successfully terminate. Kubernetes Job can be used to run containers that perform finite tasks for batch jobs. Kubernetes CronJob is used to schedule Jobs. Attackers may use Kubernetes CronJob for scheduling execution of malicious code that would run as a container in the cluster.

MATCH-S00674.description_expression: Detects the AWS WAF UpdateWebACL API action. UpdateWebACL updates the specified WebACL. A web ACL defines a collection of rules to use to inspect and control web requests. Each rule has an action defined (allow, block, or count) for requests that match the statement of the rule. In the web ACL, you assign a default action to take (allow, block) for any request that does not match any of the rules. The rules in a web ACL can be a combination of the types Rule, RuleGroup, and managed rule group. You can associate a web ACL with one or more AWS resources to protect. The resources can be an Amazon CloudFront distribution, an Amazon API Gateway REST API, an Application Load Balancer, or an AWS AppSync GraphQL API.
MATCH-S00674.expression:
metadata_vendor = 'Amazon AWS'
AND metadata_product = 'CloudTrail'
AND application LIKE 'waf%'
AND action = 'UpdateWebACL'
AND NOT array_contains(listMatches, 'AWS_admin_ips')
AND NOT array_contains(listMatches, 'AWS_admin_users')
MATCH-S00674.name: AWS WAF Access Control List Updated
MATCH-S00674.name_expression: AWS WAF Access Control List Updated
MATCH-S00674.normalized_description: Detects the AWS WAF UpdateWebACL API action. UpdateWebACL updates the specified WebACL. A web ACL defines a collection of rules to use to inspect and control web requests. Each rule has an action defined (allow, block, or count) for requests that match the statement of the rule. In the web ACL, you assign a default action to take (allow, block) for any request that does not match any of the rules. The rules in a web ACL can be a combination of the types Rule, RuleGroup, and managed rule group. You can associate a web ACL with one or more AWS resources to protect. The resources can be an Amazon CloudFront distribution, an Amazon API Gateway REST API, an Application Load Balancer, or an AWS AppSync GraphQL API.
MATCH-S00674.normalized_signal_name: AWS WAF Access Control List Updated

MATCH-S00676.description_expression: Detects the AWS WAF UpdateRuleGroup API action. UpdateRuleGroup updates the specified RuleGroup. A rule group defines a collection of rules to inspect and control web requests that you can use in a WebACL. When you create a rule group, you define an immutable capacity limit. If you update a rule group, you must stay within the capacity. This allows others to reuse the rule group with confidence in its capacity requirements.

metadata_vendor = 'Amazon AWS'
AND metadata_product = 'CloudTrail'

MATCH-S00685.description_expression: A login was successful where the account did NOT use multi-factor authentication (MFA) to gain access. objectType = 'Authentication'
AND normalizedAction IN ('logon','domainLogon')
AND success = true
AND mfa = false

    AND metadata_product = 'CloudTrail'
    AND NOT isNull(fields['additionalEventData.SamlProviderArn']))

MATCH-S00686.description_expression: Malicious files are often encoded in an attempt to bypass security controls that would otherwise inspect the contents of said file. An attacker would then need to decode the malicious file for use on the victim machine using a utility such as certutil or the base64 command. This rule supports detection for standard decoding utilities on Unix, Windows cmd, Windows PowerShell, and MacOS.
MATCH-S00686.name: Base64 Decode in Command Line
MATCH-S00686.normalized_description: Malicious files are often encoded in an attempt to bypass security controls that would otherwise inspect the contents of said file. An attacker would then need to decode the malicious file for use on the victim machine using a utility such as certutil or the base64 command. This rule supports detection for standard decoding utilities on Unix, Windows cmd, Windows PowerShell, and MacOS.
MATCH-S00686.normalized_summary: A base64-encoded file was decoded on host {{device_hostname}} by user {{user_username}}
MATCH-S00686.summary_expression: A base64-encoded file was decoded on host {{device_hostname}} by user {{user_username}}

MATCH-S00687.description_expression: This rule monitors for usage of the default command or script names associated with penetration testing tools used on Linux machines. While these commands can be renamed to avoid detection, less sophisticated attackers will often neglect to do so.
MATCH-S00687.expression:
(commandLine rlike '(?:^|sudo\s|\||&&|;|\/bin\/)\s*(?:nmap\s|msfconsole|msfcli|wireshark|setoolkit|unshadow|aircrack-ng|airodump-ng|nikto\s|hydra\s|pw-inspector|cobaltstrike)'
OR commandLine rlike '\.\/set$|sqlmap.py|\/lynis|\/bin\/john')
AND NOT lower(commandLine) rlike 'git\s+cat-file\s+blob\s+\S+lynis\.rb'
MATCH-S00687.name: Linux Security Tool Usage
MATCH-S00687.normalized_description: This rule monitors for usage of the default command or script names associated with penetration testing tools used on Linux machines. While these commands can be renamed to avoid detection, less sophisticated attackers will often neglect to do so.
MATCH-S00687.normalized_summary: A Linux security tool was launched on host {{device_hostname}} by user {{user_username}}
MATCH-S00687.summary_expression: A Linux security tool was launched on host {{device_hostname}} by user {{user_username}}

MATCH-S00688.description_expression: This rule monitors for various methods of deleting or otherwise tampering with .bash_history files which store command history on Linux machines.
MATCH-S00688.expression:
commandLine is not null
AND (
commandLine like 'rm %.bash_history'
or commandLine like 'history -c'
or commandLine rlike 'export\s+(?:HISTFILE=/dev/null|HISTFILESIZE=0)'
or commandLine like 'unset HISTFILE'
or commandLine like 'set +o history'
)
MATCH-S00688.name: Bash History Tampering
MATCH-S00688.normalized_description: This rule monitors for various methods of deleting or otherwise tampering with .bash_history files which store command history on Linux machines.
MATCH-S00688.normalized_summary: The command history on host {{device_hostname}} was manipulated by user {{user_username}}
MATCH-S00688.summary_expression: The command history on host {{device_hostname}} was manipulated by user {{user_username}}

MATCH-S00691.description_expression: Detects productivity applications spawning regsvr32 or rundll32. This activity has been attributed to the Qbot and Bazar trojans.
MATCH-S00691.expression:
lower(baseImage) RLIKE '(regsvr|rundll)32\.exe'
AND lower(parentBaseImage) RLIKE '\b((?:winword|excel|powerpnt|mspub|visio|outlook|onenote|thunderbird|acrobat|acrord32|foxit reader|sumatrapdf|hwp)\.exe)\b'
AND NOT lower(commandLine) LIKE '%monitorprintjobstatus%'
AND NOT lower(commandLine) LIKE '%ndfrundlldiagnosewithanswerfile%'

MATCH-S00694.description_expression: Detects emails with a spam score greater than 50 using Proofpoint POD.
MATCH-S00694.expression:
metadata_vendor = 'Proofpoint'
AND metadata_product = 'Proofpoint on Demand'
AND (
    fields['filter.modules.spam.scores.classifiers.phish'] > 50
    OR fields['filter.modules.spam.scores.classifiers.malware'] > 50
    OR fields['filter.modules.spam.scores.classifiers.impostor'] > 50
    OR fields['filter.modules.spam.scores.classifiers.spam'] > 50
    OR fields['filter.modules.spam.scores.classifiers.mlx'] > 50
    )
MATCH-S00694.name: Proofpoint POD Suspicious Email
MATCH-S00694.normalized_description: Detects emails with a spam score greater than 50 using Proofpoint POD.

MATCH-S00699.description_expression: The RawAccessRead event detects when a process conducts reading operations from the drive using the \\.\ denotation. This technique is often used by malware for data exfiltration of files that are locked for reading, as well as to avoid file access auditing tools. The event indicates the source process and target device.

MATCH-S00699.normalized_description: The RawAccessRead event detects when a process conducts reading operations from the drive using the \\.\ denotation. This technique is often used by malware for data exfiltration of files that are locked for reading, as well as to avoid file access auditing tools. The event indicates the source process and target device.

MATCH-S00704.description_expression: This rule detects modifications to registry keys commonly targeted to achieve persistence.
MATCH-S00704.expression:
metadata_vendor = 'Microsoft'
AND metadata_product = 'Windows'
AND metadata_deviceEventId IN ('Microsoft-Windows-Sysmon/Operational-12','Microsoft-Windows-Sysmon/Operational-13','Microsoft-Windows-Sysmon/Operational-14','Security-4657')
AND (
    file_path LIKE '%Software\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\Run%'
    OR file_path LIKE '%Software\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\Explorer\User Shell Folders%'
    OR file_path LIKE '%Software\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\Explorer\Shell Folders%'
    OR file_path LIKE '%Software\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\Policies\Explorer\Run%'
    OR file_path LIKE '%Software\Microsoft\Windows NT\CurrentVersion\Winlogon\Userinit%'
    OR file_path LIKE '%Software\Microsoft\Windows NT\CurrentVersion\Winlogon\Shell%'
    OR file_path LIKE '%Software\Microsoft\Windows NT\CurrentVersion\Windows%'
    OR file_path LIKE '%System\CurrentControlSet\Control\Session Manager%'
OR file_path like '%\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Windows NT\CurrentVersion\Image File Execution Options\%\GlobalFlag'
OR file_path like '%\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Windows NT\CurrentVersion\SilentProcessExit\%\ReportingMode'
OR file_path like '%\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Windows NT\CurrentVersion\SilentProcessExit\%\MonitorProcess'
)
    AND NOT LOWER(file_path) LIKE '%ctfmon.exe%'
    AND NOT LOWER(file_path) LIKE '%application restart%'
    AND NOT LOWER(file_path) LIKE '%software\microsoft\windows\currentversion\runonce\uninstall%'
    AND NOT LOWER(file_path) LIKE '%squirrel%'
    AND NOT LOWER(file_path) LIKE '%\skype%'
    AND NOT LOWER(file_path) LIKE '%\lync%'
    AND NOT LOWER(file_path) LIKE '%\zoom%'
    AND NOT LOWER(file_path) LIKE '%\spotify%'
    AND NOT LOWER(file_path) LIKE '%\delete cache%'
    AND NOT LOWER(file_path) LIKE '%\gotomeeting%'
    AND NOT LOWER(file_path) LIKE '%\wab migrate%'
    AND NOT LOWER(file_path) LIKE '%\officesyncprocess%'
    AND NOT LOWER(file_path) LIKE '%\onedrivesetup%'
MATCH-S00704.name: Persistence Registry Key Modification
MATCH-S00704.normalized_description: This rule detects modifications to registry keys commonly targeted to achieve persistence.
MATCH-S00704.normalized_summary: Persistence registry key {{file_path}} modification detected on host: {{device_hostname}}
MATCH-S00704.summary_expression: Persistence registry key {{file_path}} modification detected on host: {{device_hostname}}

MATCH-S00723.description_expression: Detects commands associated with modifications to loadable kernel modules. LKMs can be used on Linux systems to accomplish persistence by modifying the kernel to execute malicious programs at boot automatically.

MATCH-S00724.description_expression: The Windows Update Agent executable, wuauclt.exe, can be abused by attackers to execute malicious code with elevated privileges by changing the DLL file loaded by the process. This technique is most commonly reported as part of phishing campaigns where the initial payload is a macro-enabled Microsoft Word document.
MATCH-S00724.name: Windows Update Agent DLL Changed
MATCH-S00724.normalized_description: The Windows Update Agent executable, wuauclt.exe, can be abused by attackers to execute malicious code with elevated privileges by changing the DLL file loaded by the process. This technique is most commonly reported as part of phishing campaigns where the initial payload is a macro-enabled Microsoft Word document.
MATCH-S00724.normalized_summary: User {{user_username}} ran a command on host {{device_hostname}} to modify the Windows Update Agent
MATCH-S00724.summary_expression: User {{user_username}} ran a command on host {{device_hostname}} to modify the Windows Update Agent

MATCH-S00725.description_expression: Compiled HTML Files (.chm) are blocked by default on modern Windows operating systems and are also vulnerable to malicious code embedding. These factors make the presence of .chm files on company assets both unusual and risky.
MATCH-S00725.expression:
lower(commandLine) RLIKE '\.chm(\s|$)'
OR lower(file_basename) LIKE '%.chm'
OR lower(file_path) LIKE '%.chm'
MATCH-S00725.name: Microsoft CHM File Observed
MATCH-S00725.normalized_description: Compiled HTML Files (.chm) are blocked by default on modern Windows operating systems and are also vulnerable to malicious code embedding. These factors make the presence of .chm files on company assets both unusual and risky.
MATCH-S00725.normalized_summary: A .chm file was observed on host {{device_hostname}} in association with user {{user_username}}
MATCH-S00725.summary_expression: A .chm file was observed on host {{device_hostname}} in association with user {{user_username}}

MATCH-S00726.description_expression: Kernel Extensions in macOS can be used by adversaries to execute malicious code with root privileges.
MATCH-S00726.name: macOS Kernel Extension Load
MATCH-S00726.normalized_description: Kernel Extensions in macOS can be used by adversaries to execute malicious code with root privileges.

MATCH-S00727.description_expression: CPL files are DLL libraries that export a CPlApplet and are normally executed via the Windows Control Panel GUI interface. Having these files executed via the 'control' command from a temp directory is a strong indicator that an attacker has renamed a malicious DLL file as a CPL file and is attempting execution.
MATCH-S00727.name: CPL File Executed from Temp Directory
MATCH-S00727.name_expression: CPL File Executed from Temp Directory
MATCH-S00727.normalized_description: CPL files are DLL libraries that export a CPlApplet and are normally executed via the Windows Control Panel GUI interface. Having these files executed via the 'control' command from a temp directory is a strong indicator that an attacker has renamed a malicious DLL file as a CPL file and is attempting execution.
MATCH-S00727.normalized_signal_name: CPL File Executed from Temp Directory
MATCH-S00727.normalized_summary: User {{user_username}} exected a CPL file from a temp directory on host {{device_hostname}}
MATCH-S00727.summary_expression: User {{user_username}} exected a CPL file from a temp directory on host {{device_hostname}}

MATCH-S00729.description_expression: The com.apple.quarantine flag is usually automatically set for downloaded files and invokes Apple's Gatekeeper program for file analysis. As such, attackers may attempt to remove this flag from malware payloads using the xattr command. Note that files downloaded via curl, external media, or from shared drives on the local network do not set the com.apple.quarantine flag.
MATCH-S00729.name: MacOS Gatekeeper Bypass
MATCH-S00729.normalized_description: The com.apple.quarantine flag is usually automatically set for downloaded files and invokes Apple's Gatekeeper program for file analysis. As such, attackers may attempt to remove this flag from malware payloads using the xattr command. Note that files downloaded via curl, external media, or from shared drives on the local network do not set the com.apple.quarantine flag.
MATCH-S00729.normalized_summary: A macOS Gatekeeper bypass has been observed on host {{device_hostname}}
MATCH-S00729.summary_expression: A macOS Gatekeeper bypass has been observed on host {{device_hostname}}

MATCH-S00730.description_expression: The Driver Signature Enforcement functionality in Windows ensures that only approved, unaltered programs are able to run on a system. Modifications to this policy can indicate an attempt to bypass these built-in protections. Tuning for this rule may be required for developer machines as code signing protections often interfere with the software development process.
MATCH-S00730.expression:
metadata_vendor = 'Microsoft'
AND metadata_product = 'Windows'
AND metadata_deviceEventId IN ('Microsoft-Windows-Sysmon/Operational-12','Microsoft-Windows-Sysmon/Operational-13','Microsoft-Windows-Sysmon/Operational-14','Security-4657')
AND lower(changeTarget) LIKE '%software\policies\microsoft\windows nt\driver signing%'
MATCH-S00730.name: Registry Modification - Code Signing
MATCH-S00730.normalized_description: The Driver Signature Enforcement functionality in Windows ensures that only approved, unaltered programs are able to run on a system. Modifications to this policy can indicate an attempt to bypass these built-in protections. Tuning for this rule may be required for developer machines as code signing protections often interfere with the software development process.
MATCH-S00730.normalized_summary: User {{user_username}} modified registry key {{changeTarget}} on host {{device_hostname}}
MATCH-S00730.summary_expression: User {{user_username}} modified registry key {{changeTarget}} on host {{device_hostname}}

MATCH-S00731.description_expression: The System Integrity Protection (SIP) feature of MacOS helps to prevent the execution of unauthorized code and disabling this feature can be an indicator of a malicious actor attempting to bypass default security features. Tuning for this rule may be necessary if software developers in the organization use MacOS and need to disable SIP to run their code.
MATCH-S00731.name: MacOS System Integrity Protection Disabled
MATCH-S00731.normalized_description: The System Integrity Protection (SIP) feature of MacOS helps to prevent the execution of unauthorized code and disabling this feature can be an indicator of a malicious actor attempting to bypass default security features. Tuning for this rule may be necessary if software developers in the organization use MacOS and need to disable SIP to run their code.
MATCH-S00731.normalized_summary: User: {{user_username}} disabled System Integrity Protecion on host: {{device_hostname}}
MATCH-S00731.summary_expression: User: {{user_username}} disabled System Integrity Protecion on host: {{device_hostname}}

MATCH-S00732.description_expression: Detects file creations or registry modifications to Windows port  monitors which can be used to gain persistence in a system process.
MATCH-S00732.expression:
(
    lower(baseImage) like '%spoolsv.exe'
AND changeTarget like '%\spool\drivers\x64\%.dll'
)
OR
    changeTarget like 'HKLM\SYSTEM\CurrentControlSet\Control\Print\Monitors%'
MATCH-S00732.name: Windows Port Monitor Modification
MATCH-S00732.normalized_description: Detects file creations or registry modifications to Windows port  monitors which can be used to gain persistence in a system process.
MATCH-S00732.normalized_summary: Port Monitor DLL detected on host: {{device_hostname}}
MATCH-S00732.summary_expression: Port Monitor DLL detected on host: {{device_hostname}}

MATCH-S00733.description_expression: Observes for modifications to registry entries defining print processors for the print spooler service. The print spooler runs with  system level permissions and this technique can be used to establish persistence.
MATCH-S00733.name: Registry Modification - Print Processors
MATCH-S00733.normalized_description: Observes for modifications to registry entries defining print processors for the print spooler service. The print spooler runs with  system level permissions and this technique can be used to establish persistence.

MATCH-S00735.description_expression: Trust Providers and Subject Interface Packages (SIPs) can be modified via the Windows Registry to allow attackers to execute malicious code with heightened permissions and less oversight. This rule monitors for changes to several Registry keys containing values that modify these settings.
MATCH-S00735.expression:
metadata_vendor = 'Microsoft'
AND metadata_product = 'Windows'
AND metadata_deviceEventId IN ('Microsoft-Windows-Sysmon/Operational-12','Microsoft-Windows-Sysmon/Operational-13','Microsoft-Windows-Sysmon/Operational-14','Security-4657')
AND lower(changeTarget) RLIKE 'software\\(?:microsoft\\cryptography\\oid|wow6432node\\microsoft\\cryptography\\oid|microsoft\\cryptography\\providers\\trust|wow6432node\\microsoft\\cryptography\\providers\\trust)'
MATCH-S00735.name: Registry Modification - SIP or Trust Provider
MATCH-S00735.name_expression: Registry Modification - SIP or Trust Provider
MATCH-S00735.normalized_description: Trust Providers and Subject Interface Packages (SIPs) can be modified via the Windows Registry to allow attackers to execute malicious code with heightened permissions and less oversight. MATCH-S00735.normalized_signal_name: Registry Modification - SIP or Trust Provider
MATCH-S00735.normalized_summary: User {{user_username}} modified registry key {{changeTarget}} on host {{device_hostname}}
MATCH-S00735.summary_expression: User {{user_username}} modified registry key {{changeTarget}} on host {{device_hostname}}

MATCH-S00736.description_expression: Native Window utilities are often employed by attackers to execute malicious code in order to abuse the elevated privileges of these processes and to blend in with normal system activity. This rule monitors for outbound TCP/UDP connections spawning from installutil.exe as this could indicate a second-stage payload acquisition. Note that sysmon event ID 3 does not record ICMP connections.
MATCH-S00736.expression:
metadata_deviceEventId = 'Microsoft-Windows-Sysmon/Operational-3'
AND lower(file_path) RLIKE '\binstallutil.exe'
AND dstDevice_ip_isInternal = false

MATCH-S00753.description_expression: Detects modifications to files and registry entries related to Microsoft Office Template Macros. These can be abused by an adversary to gain persistence.
MATCH-S00753.expression:
changeTarget like '%Users\%\AppData\Roaming\Microsoft\Templates\Normal.dotm'
OR changeTarget like '%Users\%\AppData\Roaming\Microsoft\Excel\XLSTART\PERSONAL.XLSB'
OR changeTarget like '%HKCU\software\microsoft\office\%\word\options\GlobalDotName%'
MATCH-S00753.name: Potential Microsoft Office Template Abuse
MATCH-S00753.normalized_description: Detects modifications to files and registry entries related to Microsoft Office Template Macros. These can be abused by an adversary to gain persistence.
MATCH-S00753.normalized_summary: Potential Office Template Abuse detected on host: {{device_hostname}}
MATCH-S00753.summary_expression: Potential Office Template Abuse detected on host: {{device_hostname}}

MATCH-S00754.description_expression: Observes for modifications to the Microsoft Office Test registry key. This key allows for an arbitrary DLL to be executed on Office launch. This can be used by an adversary to establish persistence.
MATCH-S00754.expression:
lower(changeTarget) like '%hkey_current_user\software\microsoft\office test\special\perf%'
OR lower(changeTarget) like '%hkey_local_machine\software\microsoft\office test\special\perf%'
MATCH-S00754.name: Registry Modification - Microsoft Office Test Function Registry Entry
MATCH-S00754.name_expression: Registry Modification - Microsoft Office Test Function Registry Entry
MATCH-S00754.normalized_description: Observes for modifications to the Microsoft Office Test registry key. This key allows for an arbitrary DLL to be executed on Office launch. This can be used by an adversary to establish persistence.
MATCH-S00754.normalized_signal_name: Registry Modification - Microsoft Office Test Function Registry Entry
MATCH-S00754.normalized_summary: Observed Office Test Registry Modification on Host: {{device_hostname}}
MATCH-S00754.summary_expression: Observed Office Test Registry Modification on Host: {{device_hostname}}

MATCH-S00755.description_expression: Detects Outlook Form creation.  Outlook forms can be used to execute code on a compromised machine and establish persistence.
MATCH-S00755.name: Outlook Form Creation
MATCH-S00755.normalized_description: Detects Outlook Form creation.  Outlook forms can be used to execute code on a compromised machine and establish persistence.
MATCH-S00755.normalized_summary: Observed Outlook Form creation on host: {{device_hostname}}
MATCH-S00755.summary_expression: Observed Outlook Form creation on host: {{device_hostname}}

MATCH-S00756.description_expression: Detects modifications to the Microsoft Outlook Homepage. Outlook Homepage is a legacy feature that can be leveraged by an adversary to insert malicious code and establish persistence.
MATCH-S00756.expression:
lower(changeTarget) like '%hkcu\software\microsoft\office\%\outlook\security\%'
OR lower(changeTarget) like '%hkcu\software\microsoft\office\%\outlook\today\%'
OR lower(changeTarget) like '%hkcu\software\microsoft\office\%\outlook\webview\%'
MATCH-S00756.name: Outlook Homepage Modification
MATCH-S00756.normalized_description: Detects modifications to the Microsoft Outlook Homepage. Outlook Homepage is a legacy feature that can be leveraged by an adversary to insert malicious code and establish persistence.
MATCH-S00756.normalized_summary: Outlook Homepage Modification detected on host: {{device_hostname}}
MATCH-S00756.summary_expression: Outlook Homepage Modification detected on host: {{device_hostname}}

MATCH-S00757.description_expression: Detects a registry modification that enables the creation of Outlook mail rules that can run scripts. This functionality is disabled by default. The addition of the EnableUnsafeClientMailRules registry entry is highly suspicious. This feature can be used by an adversary to establish persistence.
MATCH-S00757.name: Unsafe Outlook Rule Creation Enabled
MATCH-S00757.name_expression: Potential Malicious Outlook Mail Rule Creation
MATCH-S00757.normalized_description: Detects a registry modification that enables the creation of Outlook mail rules that can run scripts. This functionality is disabled by default. The addition of the EnableUnsafeClientMailRules registry entry is highly suspicious. This feature can be used by an adversary to establish persistence.
MATCH-S00757.normalized_signal_name: Potential Malicious Outlook Mail Rule Creation
MATCH-S00757.normalized_summary: Unsafe Outlook Rule Creation Enabled on host: {{device_hostname}}
MATCH-S00757.summary_expression: Unsafe Outlook Rule Creation Enabled on host: {{device_hostname}}

MATCH-S00758.description_expression: Detects changes to the CrashControl registry key, this can be used to disable crash dumps as an anti-forensic technique.
MATCH-S00758.name: CrashControl Registry Modification
MATCH-S00758.normalized_description: Detects changes to the CrashControl registry key, this can be used to disable crash dumps as an anti-forensic technique.
MATCH-S00758.normalized_summary: User {{user_username}} modified registry key {{changeTarget}} on host {{device_hostname}}
MATCH-S00758.summary_expression: User {{user_username}} modified registry key {{changeTarget}} on host {{device_hostname}}

MATCH-S00760.description_expression: Detects when Win32_PingStatus is invoked to ping a device.
MATCH-S00760.expression:
metadata_vendor = 'Microsoft'
AND metadata_product = 'Windows'
AND metadata_deviceEventId = 'Microsoft-Windows-WMI-Activity/Operational-5860'
AND lower(commandLine) LIKE '%select * from win32_pingstatus where address=%'
MATCH-S00760.name: WMI Ping Sweep
MATCH-S00760.normalized_description: Detects when Win32_PingStatus is invoked to ping a device.
MATCH-S00760.normalized_summary: A WMI Ping Sweep has been detected on host: {{device_hostname}}
MATCH-S00760.summary_expression: A WMI Ping Sweep has been detected on host: {{device_hostname}}

MATCH-S00761.description_expression: Detects the Volume Shadow Copy service being stopped using net stop. This activity is commonly seen in wiper malware and ransomware attacks.
MATCH-S00761.expression:
lower(commandLine) LIKE '%net stop vss%'
OR lower(commandLine) LIKE '%net stop swprv%'
MATCH-S00761.name: Volume Shadow Copy Service Stopped
MATCH-S00761.normalized_description: Detects the Volume Shadow Copy service being stopped using net stop. This activity is commonly seen in wiper malware and ransomware attacks.
MATCH-S00761.normalized_summary: The Volume Shadow Copy Service has been stopped on host: {{device_hostname}}
MATCH-S00761.summary_expression: The Volume Shadow Copy Service has been stopped on host: {{device_hostname}}

MATCH-S00762.description_expression: Detects executable file extension written to the PolicyDefinitions directory. This activity has been associated with destructive malware
MATCH-S00762.name: Unusual Staging Directory - PolicyDefinitions
MATCH-S00762.normalized_description: Detects executable file extension written to the PolicyDefinitions directory. This activity has been associated with destructive malware
MATCH-S00762.normalized_summary: User: {{user_username}} has executed an unusual staging directory on host: {{device_hostname}}
MATCH-S00762.summary_expression: User: {{user_username}} has executed an unusual staging directory on host: {{device_hostname}}

MATCH-S00763.description_expression: Detects additions to Microsoft Office add-ins on a host. Add-ins can be used by an adversary to gain poersistence as they are executed on Office applications being launched.
MATCH-S00763.expression:
lower(changeTarget) like '%appdata\roaming\microsoft\word\startup\%.wll'
OR
    lower(changeTarget) like 'hkcu\software\microsoft\office\%\%\addins\%'
OR
    lower(changeTarget) like '%appdata\microsoft\addins\%.xll'
OR
    lower(changeTarget) like '%appdata\microsoft\addins\%.dll'
OR
    lower(changeTarget) like '%hkcu\software\microsoft\office\%\excel\options\open%'
MATCH-S00763.name: Microsoft Office Add-In Persistence
MATCH-S00763.normalized_description: Detects additions to Microsoft Office add-ins on a host. Add-ins can be used by an adversary to gain poersistence as they are executed on Office applications being launched.
MATCH-S00763.normalized_summary: Detected Office Add-In Addition activity on host: {{device_hostname}}
MATCH-S00763.summary_expression: Detected Office Add-In Addition activity on host: {{device_hostname}}

MATCH-S00764.commit_message: TRIDENT-15913 Clarify S3 bucket misconfiguration rule name and summary
MATCH-S00764.description_expression: Detects when GetPublicAccessBlock returns NoSuchPublicAccessBlockConfiguration, indicating the public access block has all values are set to false or the feature is disabled.
MATCH-S00764.expression:
metadata_vendor = 'Amazon AWS'
AND metadata_product = 'CloudTrail'
AND action IN ('GetPublicAccessBlock','GetBucketPublicAccessBlock')
AND fields['errorCode'] = 'NoSuchPublicAccessBlockConfiguration'
MATCH-S00764.name: AWS CloudTrail - S3 Bucket Public Access Block Disabled
MATCH-S00764.name_expression: AWS CloudTrail - An S3 Bucket Public Access Block is Disabled
MATCH-S00764.normalized_description: Detects when GetPublicAccessBlock returns NoSuchPublicAccessBlockConfiguration, indicating the public access block has all values are set to false or the feature is disabled.
MATCH-S00764.normalized_signal_name: AWS CloudTrail - An S3 Bucket Public Access Block is Disabled
MATCH-S00764.normalized_summary: An S3 Bucket Public Access Block is Disabled. {{action}} command run by User: {{user_username}} with IP: {{srcDevice_ip}} User: {{user_username}}
MATCH-S00764.summary_expression: An S3 Bucket Public Access Block is Disabled. {{action}} command run by User: {{user_username}} with IP: {{srcDevice_ip}} User: {{user_username}}

MATCH-S00765.description_expression: A session impersonation access event has occurred. This most likely indicates Okta support or administrative access has occurred and should be reviewed and verified.
MATCH-S00765.expression:
metadata_vendor = 'Okta'
AND metadata_product = 'Single Sign-On'
AND metadata_deviceEventId IN ('user.session.impersonation.initiate','user.session.impersonation.grant')
MATCH-S00765.name: Okta Credential Access User Impersonation
MATCH-S00765.normalized_description: A session impersonation access event has occurred. This most likely indicates Okta support or administrative access has occurred and should be reviewed and verified.
MATCH-S00765.normalized_summary: User impersonation session initiated from IP {{srcDevice_ip}}
MATCH-S00765.summary_expression: User impersonation session initiated from IP {{srcDevice_ip}}

MATCH-S00766.description_expression: This rule detects multi-factor authentication (MFA) being disabled for a user. An adversary or malicious insider may deactivate MFA in order to bypass security controls. The Okta_Admins match list should be created and populated with administrator users permitted to perform this activity.
MATCH-S00766.expression:
metadata_vendor = 'Okta'
AND metadata_product = 'Single Sign-On'
AND metadata_deviceEventId = 'user.mfa.factor.deactivate'
AND NOT array_contains(listMatches,'Okta_Admins')
MATCH-S00766.name: Okta MFA Deactivated for User
MATCH-S00766.normalized_description: This rule detects multi-factor authentication (MFA) being disabled for a user. An adversary or malicious insider may deactivate MFA in order to bypass security controls. The Okta_Admins match list should be created and populated with administrator users permitted to perform this activity.

MATCH-S00767.description_expression: An accounts multi-factor authentication (MFA) device has been reset. This can indicate an attacker attempting to bypass or intercept MFA for an account which they have already compromised.
MATCH-S00767.expression:
metadata_vendor = 'Okta'
AND metadata_product = 'Single Sign-On'
AND metadata_deviceEventId = 'user.mfa.factor.reset_all'
MATCH-S00767.name: Okta MFA Device Reset
MATCH-S00767.normalized_description: An accounts multi-factor authentication (MFA) device has been reset. This can indicate an attacker attempting to bypass or intercept MFA for an account which they have already compromised.

MATCH-S00768.description_expression: Attempts to bypass MFA can be indicative of malicious actors having compromised the password for an account but not the corresponding MFA token and such attempts should be audited for legitimacy.
MATCH-S00768.expression:
metadata_vendor = 'Okta'
AND metadata_product = 'Single Sign-On'
AND metadata_deviceEventId = 'user.mfa.attempt_bypass'
MATCH-S00768.name: Okta MFA Bypass Attempt
MATCH-S00768.normalized_description: Attempts to bypass MFA can be indicative of malicious actors having compromised the password for an account but not the corresponding MFA token and such attempts should be audited for legitimacy.
MATCH-S00768.normalized_summary: MFA bypass attempt for user {{user_username}} from ip {{srcDevice_ip}}
MATCH-S00768.summary_expression: MFA bypass attempt for user {{user_username}} from ip {{srcDevice_ip}}

MATCH-S00769.description_expression: An Okta accounts primary email address has been updated. An adversary may modify an Okta accounts email address to evade detection by users or to gather information about the organization and establish persistence through credential resets.
MATCH-S00769.expression:
metadata_vendor = 'Okta'
AND metadata_product = 'Single Sign-On'
AND metadata_deviceEventId = 'user.account.update_primary_email'
MATCH-S00769.name: Okta Account Primary Email Address Update
MATCH-S00769.name_expression: Okta Account Primary Email Address Update
MATCH-S00769.normalized_description: An Okta accounts primary email address has been updated. An adversary may modify an Okta accounts email address to evade detection by users or to gather information about the organization and establish persistence through credential resets.
MATCH-S00769.normalized_signal_name: Okta Account Primary Email Address Update
MATCH-S00769.normalized_summary: Primary email address changed for user {{user_username}}
MATCH-S00769.summary_expression: Primary email address changed for user {{user_username}}

MATCH-S00770.description_expression: Detects when a disabled account attempts to log into Salesforce
MATCH-S00770.expression:
metadata_vendor = 'Salesforce'
AND metadata_product = 'Salesforce'
AND metadata_deviceEventId = 'Login'
AND fields['LOGIN_STATUS'] = 'LOGIN_ERROR_USER_INACTIVE'

MATCH-S00770.name_expression: Salesforce Login Attempt from Disabled Account
MATCH-S00770.normalized_description: Detects when a disabled account attempts to log into Salesforce

MATCH-S00770.summary_expression: Salesforce Login Attempt from Disabled Account: {{user_username}} IP: {{srcDevice_ip}}

MATCH-S00771.description_expression: Wave Download events represent downloads made from lens explorations and dashboard widgets in the Tableau CRM user interface. A Wave Download event type is captured when a user downloads images ( .png ), Microsoft Excel data ( .xls ), or comma-separated values ( .csv ) files. Salesforce admin match lists are exclude in this rule in an attempt to reduce the false positives due to expected admin activity. These match lists should be created and populated with Salesforce admin usernames and IPs.
MATCH-S00771.expression:
metadata_vendor = 'Salesforce'
AND metadata_product = 'Salesforce'
AND metadata_deviceEventId = 'WaveDownload'
AND NOT array_contains(listMatches, 'salesforce_admin_ips')
AND NOT array_contains(listMatches, 'salesforce_admin_users')
MATCH-S00771.name: Salesforce WaveDownload Event
MATCH-S00771.normalized_description: Wave Download events represent downloads made from lens explorations and dashboard widgets in the Tableau CRM user interface. A Wave Download event type is captured when a user downloads images ( .png ), Microsoft Excel data ( .xls ), or comma-separated values ( .csv ) files. Salesforce admin match lists are exclude in this rule in an attempt to reduce the false positives due to expected admin activity. These match lists should be created and populated with Salesforce admin usernames and IPs.
MATCH-S00771.normalized_summary: Salesforce WaveDownload Event: {{description}} with User: {{user_username}} IP: {{srcDevice_ip}}
MATCH-S00771.summary_expression: Salesforce WaveDownload Event: {{description}} with User: {{user_username}} IP: {{srcDevice_ip}}

MATCH-S00773.description_expression: Generates severity 0 signals for LoginAs Salesforce events. The purpose of this rule is to track LoginAs activity for both the source and target user to provide additional context for Insights. The third party match list exclusion should be populated to exclude known permitted third party domains from triggering this rule. Its common for third party support accounts to use the LoginAs function. Salesforce admin match lists are exclude in this rule in an attempt to reduce the false positives due to expected admin activity. These match lists should be created and populated with Salesforce admin usernames and IPs.
MATCH-S00773.expression:
metadata_vendor = 'Salesforce'
AND metadata_product = 'Salesforce'
AND metadata_deviceEventId = 'LoginAs'
AND NOT array_contains(listMatches, 'authorized_third_party_domains')
AND NOT array_contains(listMatches, 'salesforce_admin_ips')
AND NOT array_contains(listMatches, 'salesforce_admin_users')
MATCH-S00773.name: Salesforce LoginAs Event
MATCH-S00773.normalized_description: Generates severity 0 signals for LoginAs Salesforce events. The purpose of this rule is to track LoginAs activity for both the source and target user to provide additional context for Insights. The third party match list exclusion should be populated to exclude known permitted third party domains from triggering this rule. Its common for third party support accounts to use the LoginAs function. Salesforce admin match lists are exclude in this rule in an attempt to reduce the false positives due to expected admin activity. These match lists should be created and populated with Salesforce admin usernames and IPs.
MATCH-S00773.normalized_summary: Salesforce LoginAs Event from Source User: {{user_username}} IP: {{srcDevice_ip}} to Target User: {{targetUser_username}}
MATCH-S00773.summary_expression: Salesforce LoginAs Event from Source User: {{user_username}} IP: {{srcDevice_ip}} to Target User: {{targetUser_username}}

This rule is used to monitor this activity as adversaries could abuse this to escalate privileges. Salesforce admin match lists are exclude in this rule in an attempt to reduce the false positives due to expected admin activity. These match lists should be created and populated with Salesforce admin usernames and IPs.
MATCH-S00774.expression:
metadata_vendor = 'Salesforce'
AND metadata_product = 'Salesforce'

AND NOT array_contains(listMatches, 'salesforce_admin_users')

MATCH-S00784.description_expression: Promiscuous mode causes a network interface to send all traffic frames it receives to the host's CPU rather than only the frames designated for the receiver. This is required for several packet sniffing tools, such as tcpdump or Wireshark, and should be scrutinized for legitimacy. Network administrators may need to be tuned out of this rule.
MATCH-S00784.expression:
metadata_vendor = 'Linux'
AND metadata_product = 'Linux OS Syslog'
AND metadata_deviceEventId = 'promiscuous-mode-change'
AND action = 'entered'
MATCH-S00784.name: Linux Host Entered Promiscuous Mode
MATCH-S00784.normalized_description: Promiscuous mode causes a network interface to send all traffic frames it receives to the host's CPU rather than only the frames designated for the receiver. This is required for several packet sniffing tools, such as tcpdump or Wireshark, and should be scrutinized for legitimacy. Network administrators may need to be tuned out of this rule.

MATCH-S00811.description_expression: Monitors for exploitation of CVE-2022-30190 as indicated by a Microsoft Office executable spawning msdt.exe. More information on this vulnerability can be found at the Microsoft Security Response Center: https://msrc-blog.microsoft.com/2022/05/30/guidance-for-cve-2022-30190-microsoft-support-diagnostic-tool-vulnerability/
MATCH-S00811.expression:
(
    lower(parentBaseImage) RLIKE 'winword.exe|excel.exe|powerpnt.exe'
AND lower(baseImage) LIKE '%msdt.exe'
)
OR
(
    lower(baseImage) RLIKE 'winword.exe|excel.exe|powerpnt.exe'
AND lower(commandLine) LIKE '%msdt.exe%'
)
MATCH-S00811.name: MS Office Product Spawning Msdt.exe - CVE-2022-30190
MATCH-S00811.name_expression: MS Office Product Spawning Msdt.exe - CVE-2022-30190
MATCH-S00811.normalized_description: Monitors for exploitation of CVE-2022-30190 as indicated by a Microsoft Office executable spawning msdt.exe. More information on this vulnerability can be found at the Microsoft Security Response Center: https://msrc-blog.microsoft.com/2022/05/30/guidance-for-cve-2022-30190-microsoft-support-diagnostic-tool-vulnerability/
MATCH-S00811.normalized_signal_name: MS Office Product Spawning Msdt.exe - CVE-2022-30190
MATCH-S00811.normalized_summary: A Microsoft Office product was observed spawning msdt.exe on host {{device_hostname}}
MATCH-S00811.summary_expression: A Microsoft Office product was observed spawning msdt.exe on host {{device_hostname}}

MATCH-S00812.description_expression: Monitors for exploitation of CVE-2022-30190 as indicated by the Microsoft Support Diagnostic Tool being observed with the BrowseForFile command. More information on this vulnerability can be found at the Microsoft Security Response Center: https://msrc-blog.microsoft.com/2022/05/30/guidance-for-cve-2022-30190-microsoft-support-diagnostic-tool-vulnerability/
MATCH-S00812.expression:
(lower(commandLine) LIKE '%ms-msdt:/id%'
OR lower(commandLine) LIKE '%ms-msdt:-id%')
AND lower(commandLine) LIKE '%browseforfile%'
AND lower(commandLine) LIKE '%pcwdiagnostic%'
MATCH-S00812.name: Microsoft Support Diagnostic Tool with BrowseForFile - CVE-2022-30190
MATCH-S00812.name_expression: Microsoft Support Diagnostic Tool with BrowseForFile - CVE-2022-30190
MATCH-S00812.normalized_description: Monitors for exploitation of CVE-2022-30190 as indicated by the Microsoft Support Diagnostic Tool being observed with the BrowseForFile command. MATCH-S00812.summary_expression: Microsoft Support Diagnostic Tool with BrowseForFile on host {{device_hostname}}

MATCH-S00814.description_expression: Monitors for exploitation of CVE-2022-30190 as indicated by an abnormal child process for sdiagnhost.exe. More information on this vulnerability can be found at the Microsoft Security Response Center: https://msrc-blog.microsoft.com/2022/05/30/guidance-for-cve-2022-30190-microsoft-support-diagnostic-tool-vulnerability/
MATCH-S00814.expression:
(lower(parentBaseImage) LIKE '%sdiagnhost.exe'
AND NOT commandLine IS NULL)
AND NOT lower(baseImage) LIKE '%conhost.exe'
AND NOT lower(baseImage) LIKE '%csc.exe'
AND NOT lower(baseImage) LIKE '%w32tm.exe'
AND NOT lower(baseImage) LIKE '%ping.exe'
AND NOT lower(baseImage) LIKE '%regsvr32.exe'
AND NOT lower(baseImage) LIKE '%makecab.exe'
AND NOT lower(baseImage) LIKE '%ipconfig.exe'
AND NOT lower(baseImage) LIKE '%route.exe'
AND NOT lower(baseImage) LIKE '%netsh.exe'
AND NOT lower(baseImage) LIKE '%sfc.exe'
AND NOT lower(baseImage) LIKE '%sc.exe'
AND NOT lower(baseImage) LIKE '%bitsadmin.exe'
AND NOT lower(baseImage) LIKE '%net.exe'
AND NOT lower(baseImage) LIKE '%werfault.exe'
AND NOT lower(baseImage) LIKE '%wpr.exe'
AND NOT lower(baseImage) LIKE '%chrome.exe'
MATCH-S00814.name: Abnormal Child Process - sdiagnhost.exe - CVE-2022-30190

MATCH-S00816.commit_message: TLAB-1357 Correct Match List Items For MATCH-S00816
MATCH-S00816.description_expression: Detects an interactive login to a domain controller. Direct access in this manner is rarely necessary and should be closely audited given the potential impact of a compromised domain controller.
MATCH-S00816.expression:
metadata_vendor = 'Microsoft' 
AND metadata_product = 'Windows' 
AND metadata_deviceEventId = 'Security-4624' 
AND logonType IN ('Interactive', 'RemoteInteractive', 'CachedInteractive', 'CachedRemoteInteractive') 
AND array_contains(listMatches, 'domain_controller_hostnames')
MATCH-S00816.name: Interactive Logon to Domain Controller
MATCH-S00816.normalized_description: Detects an interactive login to a domain controller. Direct access in this manner is rarely necessary and should be closely audited given the potential impact of a compromised domain controller.
MATCH-S00816.normalized_summary: Interactive logon on domain controller: {{device_hostname}} with account: {{user_username}}
MATCH-S00816.summary_expression: Interactive logon on domain controller: {{device_hostname}} with account: {{user_username}}

MATCH-S00817.commit_message: Updated summary and descriptions for clarity
MATCH-S00817.description_expression: Successful Device Code authentication flows result in the issuance of an Azure Primary Refresh Token which can be used to access, enumerate or - if the relevant permissions exist - to modify Azure resources.
MATCH-S00817.expression:
metadata_vendor = 'Microsoft'
AND metadata_product = "Azure"
AND lower(fields['properties.authenticationProtocol']) = "devicecode"
AND errorCode = "0"
AND lower(fields['properties.appDisplayName']) RLIKE '(powershell|microsoft graph)'
MATCH-S00817.name: Suspicious Azure Active Directory Device Code Authentication
MATCH-S00817.name_expression: Suspicious Azure Active Directory Device Code Authentication
MATCH-S00817.normalized_description: Successful Device Code authentication flows result in the issuance of an Azure Primary Refresh Token which can be used to access, enumerate or - if the relevant permissions exist - to modify Azure resources.
MATCH-S00817.normalized_signal_name: Suspicious Azure Active Directory Device Code Authentication
MATCH-S00817.normalized_summary: A successful & suspcious Device Code authentication flow was completed to access the Azure resource: {{application}} for the user: {{user_username}}
MATCH-S00817.summary_expression: A successful & suspcious Device Code authentication flow was completed to access the Azure resource: {{application}} for the user: {{user_username}}

MATCH-S00818.description_expression: Azure Primary Refresh Tokens (PRT) can be used to authenticate  to Azure Active Directory and Azure compute resources. This rule looks for a PRT token successfully issued via a non-interactive login and when a suspicious User Agent is in use.
MATCH-S00818.expression:
metadata_vendor = 'Microsoft'
AND metadata_product = "Azure"
AND logonType = "NonInteractiveUserSignInLogs" 
AND lower(fields['properties.userAgent']) RLIKE '(windowspowershell|python-requests|go-http-client|azurehound)'
AND errorCode = "0"
AND lower(fields['properties.incomingTokenType']) = 'primaryrefreshtoken'
AND lower(application) != 'microsoft cloud app security'
AND lower(application) != 'microsoft azure cli'
MATCH-S00818.name: Azure PRT Token Issued via Non Interactive Login
MATCH-S00818.name_expression: Azure PRT Token Issued via Non Interactive Login
MATCH-S00818.normalized_description: Azure Primary Refresh Tokens (PRT) can be used to authenticate  to Azure Active Directory and Azure compute resources. This rule looks for a PRT token successfully issued via a non-interactive login and when a suspicious User Agent is in use.
MATCH-S00818.normalized_signal_name: Azure PRT Token Issued via Non Interactive Login
MATCH-S00818.normalized_summary: A suspicious Azure Primary Refersh Token (PRT) was issued via non-interactive login to {{user_username}} for the {{application}} resource.
MATCH-S00818.summary_expression: A suspicious Azure Primary Refersh Token (PRT) was issued via non-interactive login to {{user_username}} for the {{application}} resource.

MATCH-S00819.description_expression: Chromium (Microsoft Edge or Google Chrome) browsers can be started with a remote debugging port. Threat actors may then connect to this debugging port to enumerate browser information or steal session data including browser cookies.
MATCH-S00819.name: Chromium Process Started With Debugging Port
MATCH-S00819.name_expression: Chromium Process Started With Debugging Port
MATCH-S00819.normalized_description: Chromium (Microsoft Edge or Google Chrome) browsers can be started with a remote debugging port. Threat actors may then connect to this debugging port to enumerate browser information or steal session data including browser cookies.
MATCH-S00819.normalized_signal_name: Chromium Process Started With Debugging Port
MATCH-S00819.normalized_summary: The Chromium process {{baseImage}} was started via {{parentBaseImage}} with remote debugging enabled.
MATCH-S00819.summary_expression: The Chromium process {{baseImage}} was started via {{parentBaseImage}} with remote debugging enabled.

MATCH-S00820.description_expression: When connecting to various cloud services using Azure PowerShell, Az PowerShell, AWS CLI, Google Cloud CLI or Kubeconfig, credential material may be left on the file system. Threat actors may read or exfiltrate this credential material in order to gain unauthroized access to various cloud resources from on premises hosts.
MATCH-S00820.expression:
metadata_vendor = 'Microsoft'
AND metadata_product = 'Windows'
AND metadata_deviceEventId = 'Security-4663'
AND file_path RLIKE '\.azure\\msal_|\.aws\\credentials|\\gcloud\\credentials.db|\.kube\\config'
AND baseImage != "\Azure\CLI2\python.exe"
AND baseImage != "C:\Windows\explorer.exe"
AND baseImage != "C:\Program Files\Windows Defender Advanced Threat Protection\MsSense.exe"
AND baseImage != "C:\Program Files\Everything\Everything.exe"
MATCH-S00820.name: Cloud Credential File Accessed
MATCH-S00820.normalized_description: When connecting to various cloud services using Azure PowerShell, Az PowerShell, AWS CLI, Google Cloud CLI or Kubeconfig, credential material may be left on the file system. Threat actors may read or exfiltrate this credential material in order to gain unauthroized access to various cloud resources from on premises hosts.
MATCH-S00820.normalized_summary: The file: {{file_path}} potentially containing cloud credentials (AWS, Kubeconfig, Azure, GCP) was accessed on {{device_hostname}} by user {{user_username}} via the process {{baseImage}}
MATCH-S00820.summary_expression: The file: {{file_path}} potentially containing cloud credentials (AWS, Kubeconfig, Azure, GCP) was accessed on {{device_hostname}} by user {{user_username}} via the process {{baseImage}}

MATCH-S00821.description_expression: Chromium history files contain a wealth of information regarding browser usage patterns. Threat actors may enumerate Chromium history, bookmarks and cookies in order to extract sensitive information from systems.
MATCH-S00821.expression:
metadata_vendor = 'Microsoft'
AND metadata_product = 'Windows'
AND metadata_deviceEventId = 'Security-4663'
AND (file_path RLIKE 'Microsoft\\Edge\\User.Data\\Default\\History'
OR file_path RLIKE 'Google\\Chrome\\User.Data\\Default\\History')
AND NOT (baseImage like '%explorer.exe'
    OR baseImage like '%chrome.exe'
    OR baseImage like '%msedge.exe'
    OR baseImage like '%Windows Defender Advanced Threat Protection\MsSense.exe'
)
MATCH-S00821.name: Chromium Browser History Access by Non-Browser Process
MATCH-S00821.name_expression: Chromium Browser History Access by Non-Browser Process
MATCH-S00821.normalized_description: Chromium history files contain a wealth of information regarding browser usage patterns. Threat actors may enumerate Chromium history, bookmarks and cookies in order to extract sensitive information from systems.
MATCH-S00821.normalized_signal_name: Chromium Browser History Access by Non-Browser Process
MATCH-S00821.normalized_summary: Non-browser process {{baseImage}} has accessed the Chromium history file {{file_path}} on {{device_hostname}}
MATCH-S00821.summary_expression: Non-browser process {{baseImage}} has accessed the Chromium history file {{file_path}} on {{device_hostname}}

MATCH-S00822.commit_message: TRIDENT-16485 Potential Microsoft Office In-Memory Token Theft
MATCH-S00822.description_expression: Office applications and Web Browsers connecting to Office services can potentially store credential material in their memory space. This event indicates that a process has accessed the memory space of an Office process that may potentially contain token material.
MATCH-S00822.expression:
metadata_deviceEventId = 'Microsoft-Windows-Sysmon/Operational-10'
and toLowerCase(fields["EventData.TargetImage"]) matches /(microsoft office\\root|teams.exe|application\\msedge|chrome.exe)/
and (fields["EventData.GrantedAccess"] = "0x1fffff" OR fields["EventData.GrantedAccess"] = "0x1f3fff")
and (toLowerCase(fields["EventData.CallTrace"]) rlike "dbgcore.dll" OR toLowerCase(fields["EventData.CallTrace"]) rlike "unknown")
and fields["EventData.SourceImage"] !matches /(AppData\\Local\\Microsoft\\Teams\\)/
MATCH-S00822.name: Potential Microsoft Office In-Memory Token Theft
MATCH-S00822.name_expression: Potential Microsoft Office In-Memory Token Theft
MATCH-S00822.normalized_description: Office applications and Web Browsers connecting to Office services can potentially store credential material in their memory space. This event indicates that a process has accessed the memory space of an Office process that may potentially contain token material.
MATCH-S00822.normalized_signal_name: Potential Microsoft Office In-Memory Token Theft
MATCH-S00822.normalized_summary: {{device_hostname}} has logged a suspicious process access event potentially indicating in-memory credential or token theft from an Office application.
MATCH-S00822.summary_expression: {{device_hostname}} has logged a suspicious process access event potentially indicating in-memory credential or token theft from an Office application.

MATCH-S00823.commit_message: TRIDENT-16826 Exposed AWS SQS Queue Created
MATCH-S00823.description_expression: An AWS Simple Queue Service (SQS) can be created with a custom access policy, this signal triggers when a policy is applied to an SQS queue which allows global access from the internet with an IP range of 0.0.0.0/32 (all)
MATCH-S00823.expression:
metadata_vendor = 'Amazon AWS'
AND metadata_product = 'CloudTrail'

AND lower(application) = "sqs.amazonaws.com"

MATCH-S00823.name: Exposed AWS SQS Queue Created

MATCH-S00823.normalized_summary: {{user_username}} has applied an access policy to an AWS Simple Queue Service (SQS) with an IP range that allows global access
MATCH-S00823.summary_expression: {{user_username}} has applied an access policy to an AWS Simple Queue Service (SQS) with an IP range that allows global access

MATCH-S00866.description_expression: Privileged containers have all capabilities of the host machine. These privileged containers may perform actions directly on the host that they are running on. Ensure that this event is expected and occurs from a user account or IP address that normally works with privileged containers within the cluster. Customers are encouraged to set up an exclusion list for spec.securitycontext.capabilities for pods that are frequently going to be managed with privileged escalation.
MATCH-S00866.expression:
cloud_provider = "AWS"
AND cloud_service = "EKS"
AND action = "create"
AND (fields["message.annotations.podsecuritypolicy.policy.k8s.io/admit-policy"] = "eks.privileged" 
OR fields["message.annotations.podsecuritypolicy.policy.k8s.io/validate-policy"] = "eks.privileged"
OR fields["message.requestObject.spec.containers.1.securityContext.privileged"] = true)
MATCH-S00866.name: Privileged Pod Created on AWS EKS
MATCH-S00866.name_expression: Privileged Pod Created on AWS EKS by {{srcDevice_ip}}
MATCH-S00866.normalized_description: Privileged containers have all capabilities of the host machine. These privileged containers may perform actions directly on the host that they are running on. Ensure that this event is expected and occurs from a user account or IP address that normally works with privileged containers within the cluster. Customers are encouraged to set up an exclusion list for spec.securitycontext.capabilities for pods that are frequently going to be managed with privileged escalation.
MATCH-S00866.normalized_signal_name: Privileged Pod Created on AWS EKS by {{srcDevice_ip}}
MATCH-S00866.normalized_summary: Privileged Pod Created on AWS EKS by {{srcDevice_ip}}
MATCH-S00866.summary_expression: Privileged Pod Created on AWS EKS by {{srcDevice_ip}}

MATCH-S00884.commit_message: TLAB-747 macOS - Suspicious Python PIP Execution - update
MATCH-S00884.description_expression: This alert utilizes Jamf telemetry and looks for suspicious osascript paramaters within a a Python PIP execution. Python-pip is capabale of executing JavaScript code via osascript which can be abused by threat actors to backdoor Python packages.
MATCH-S00884.expression:
metadata_vendor = "Jamf"
and metadata_product = "Jamf"
and metadata_deviceEventId = "AUE_EXECVE"
and lower(baseImage) matches /(osascript)/
and lower(commandLine) matches /(javascript)/
and fields["exec_env.env_compiled"] matches /(pip\-req\-tracker)/
MATCH-S00884.name: macOS - Suspicious Python PIP Execution
MATCH-S00884.name_expression: macOS - Suspicious Python PIP Execution on {{device_hostname}} by {{user_username}}
MATCH-S00884.normalized_description: This alert utilizes Jamf telemetry and looks for suspicious osascript paramaters within a a Python PIP execution. Python-pip is capabale of executing JavaScript code via osascript which can be abused by threat actors to backdoor Python packages.
MATCH-S00884.normalized_signal_name: macOS - Suspicious Python PIP Execution on {{device_hostname}} by {{user_username}}
MATCH-S00884.normalized_summary: macOS - Suspicious Python PIP Execution on {{device_hostname}} by {{user_username}}
MATCH-S00884.summary_expression: macOS - Suspicious Python PIP Execution on {{device_hostname}} by {{user_username}}

MATCH-S00885.description_expression: This rule utilizes Jamf telemetry and looks for a successful screen sharing connection to a macOS host. The username that performed the connection is located within the "texts.1" field - ensure that this remote connection activity is authorized.
MATCH-S00885.expression:
metadata_vendor = "Jamf"
and metadata_product = "Jamf"
and metadata_deviceEventId = "AUE_auth_user"
and parentBaseImage = "/sbin/launchd"
and baseImage = "/System/Library/CoreServices/RemoteManagement/screensharingd.bundle/Contents/MacOS/screensharingd"
and errorCode = 0

MATCH-S00885.name_expression: macOS - Screen Sharing Session Established on {{device_hostname}}
