OUTLIER-S00024.description_expression: An outlier in GetItem events to a DynamoDB resource within an hour time period has been detected. Ensure that the user performing these actions has business justification for modifying DynamoDB tables and instances. Consider excluding authorized users from this signal or tweaking the minimum count value if this signal is triggering too often. Data events from DynamoDB are required in order for this signal to function.
OUTLIER-S00024.match_expression:
metadata_vendor = "Amazon AWS"
AND application = "dynamodb.amazonaws.com"
AND fields["eventCategory"] = "Data"
AND action = "GetItem"
OUTLIER-S00024.name: AWS DynamoDB Outlier in GetItem Events from User
OUTLIER-S00024.name_expression: Spike in DynamoDB GetItem events from User: {{user_username}}
OUTLIER-S00024.normalized_description: An outlier in GetItem events to a DynamoDB resource within an hour time period has been detected. Ensure that the user performing these actions has business justification for modifying DynamoDB tables and instances. Consider excluding authorized users from this signal or tweaking the minimum count value if this signal is triggering too often. Data events from DynamoDB are required in order for this signal to function.
OUTLIER-S00024.normalized_signal_name: Spike in DynamoDB GetItem events from User: {{user_username}}
OUTLIER-S00024.normalized_summary: An outlier in PutItem events from a user: {{user_username}}  within an hour time period has been detected
OUTLIER-S00024.summary_expression: An outlier in PutItem events from a user: {{user_username}}  within an hour time period has been detected

OUTLIER-S00025.description_expression: This rule utilizes an hourly baseline to detect an outlier in the number of denied PutObject access events to an S3 bucket. AWS Data events are necessary for this signal to function. Denied PutObject access events can stem from IAM policies or bucket policies. Look at the user, role, IP address from the events in order to determine whether this activity is expected. In certain cases, access denied events to S3 can also result in unexpected AWS charges.
OUTLIER-S00025.match_expression:
metadata_vendor = "Amazon AWS"
and application = "s3.amazonaws.com"
and action = "PutObject"
and (fields["errorMessage"] = "Access Denied" OR fields["errorCode"] = "AccessDenied")
OUTLIER-S00025.name: AWS S3 Outlier in PutObject Denied Events
OUTLIER-S00025.name_expression: Spike in AWS S3 PutObject Denied Events
OUTLIER-S00025.normalized_description: This rule utilizes an hourly baseline to detect an outlier in the number of denied PutObject access events to an S3 bucket. AWS Data events are necessary for this signal to function. Denied PutObject access events can stem from IAM policies or bucket policies. Look at the user, role, IP address from the events in order to determine whether this activity is expected. In certain cases, access denied events to S3 can also result in unexpected AWS charges.
OUTLIER-S00025.normalized_signal_name: Spike in AWS S3 PutObject Denied Events
OUTLIER-S00025.normalized_summary: An outlier in AWS S3 PutObject Denied Events was detected for: {{user_username}}
OUTLIER-S00025.summary_expression: An outlier in AWS S3 PutObject Denied Events was detected for: {{user_username}}

OUTLIER-S00026.commit_message: Added logging requirements of GitHub Enterprise to the description of GitHub rules released 6 Dec 2024
OUTLIER-S00026.description_expression: Detects an outlier in the number of distinct user agent strings for a GitHub user. Unusual user agent strings for a user account could indicate account takeover. This detection was developed utilizing GitHub Enterprise Audit logging. Refer to https://help.sumologic.com/docs/integrations/app-development/github/#collecting-logs-for-github for instructions on ingesting GitHub Enterprise Audit logs.
OUTLIER-S00026.match_expression:
metadata_product = 'GitHub Enterprise Audit'
AND http_userAgent IS NOT NULL
OUTLIER-S00026.name: GitHub - Outlier in Distinct User Agent Strings by User
OUTLIER-S00026.name_expression: GitHub - Outlier in Distinct User Agent Strings for {{user_username}}
OUTLIER-S00026.normalized_description: Detects an outlier in the number of distinct user agent strings for a GitHub user. Unusual user agent strings for a user account could indicate account takeover. This detection was developed utilizing GitHub Enterprise Audit logging. Refer to https://help.sumologic.com/docs/integrations/app-development/github/#collecting-logs-for-github for instructions on ingesting GitHub Enterprise Audit logs.
OUTLIER-S00026.normalized_signal_name: GitHub - Outlier in Distinct User Agent Strings for {{user_username}}
OUTLIER-S00026.normalized_summary: Unusual distinct user agent string by {{user_username}}
OUTLIER-S00026.summary_expression: Unusual distinct user agent string by {{user_username}}

OUTLIER-S00027.commit_message: Added logging requirements of GitHub Enterprise to the description of GitHub rules released 6 Dec 2024
OUTLIER-S00027.description_expression: Detects an unusual number of repository clones for a user. Unusual repository cloning could indicate data exfiltration or discovery. This detection was developed utilizing GitHub Enterprise Audit logging. Refer to https://help.sumologic.com/docs/integrations/app-development/github/#collecting-logs-for-github for instructions on ingesting GitHub Enterprise Audit logs.
OUTLIER-S00027.match_expression:
metadata_product = 'GitHub Enterprise Audit'
AND action in ('git.clone','repo.download_zip')
OUTLIER-S00027.name: GitHub - Outlier in Repository Cloning or Downloads

OUTLIER-S00028.name_expression: GitHub - {{user_username}}  has Performed an Unusual Number of Removal Actions
OUTLIER-S00028.normalized_description: Detects a higher than usual number of removal actions undertaken by a user. OUTLIER-S00028.summary_expression: An unusual number of remove actions was performed by the user: {{user_username}}

OUTLIER-S00030.description_expression:
Context:
An Attacker with sufficient administrative access to Azure DevOps (ADO) may abuse this access to destroy existing resources by deleting pools. 

Detection:
This detection identifies statistical outliers in user behavior for the number of pools deleted in an hourly window. 

Recommended Actions: 
If an alert occurs, investigate the actions taken by the account to determine if this is normal operation of deleting pools or if this suspicious activity. 

Tuning Recommendations:
Determine if the baseline basis should be hourly or daily based on normal activity in your organization. 
If the detection is proving to be too sensitive to the number of pools deleted, adjust the floor value (currently 3) to a number that is less sensitive but within reason. Use Sumo Search using a count and the _timeslice function to aggregate on the number of pools deleted within the hourly (or daily) periods to find what is an acceptable level of activity to not alert on.
OUTLIER-S00030.match_expression:
metadata_vendor = "Microsoft"
AND metadata_product = "Azure DevOps Auditing"
AND metadata_deviceEventId = "AzureDevOpsAuditEvent"
AND action = "Library.AgentPoolDeleted"
OUTLIER-S00030.name: Azure DevOps - Outlier in Pools Deleted Rapidly
OUTLIER-S00030.nameExpression: Azure DevOps - Outlier in Agent Pools Deleted in an Hour
OUTLIER-S00030.name_expression: Azure DevOps - Outlier in Agent Pools Deleted in an Hour
OUTLIER-S00030.normalized_description:
Context:
An Attacker with sufficient administrative access to Azure DevOps (ADO) may abuse this access to destroy existing resources by deleting pools. 

Detection:
This detection identifies statistical outliers in user behavior for the number of pools deleted in an hourly window. 

Recommended Actions: 
If an alert occurs, investigate the actions taken by the account to determine if this is normal operation of deleting pools or if this suspicious activity. 

Tuning Recommendations:
Determine if the baseline basis should be hourly or daily based on normal activity in your organization. 
If the detection is proving to be too sensitive to the number of pools deleted, adjust the floor value (currently 3) to a number that is less sensitive but within reason. Use Sumo Search using a count and the _timeslice function to aggregate on the number of pools deleted within the hourly (or daily) periods to find what is an acceptable level of activity to not alert on.
OUTLIER-S00030.normalized_signal_name: Azure DevOps - Outlier in Agent Pools Deleted in an Hour
OUTLIER-S00030.normalized_summary: User: {{user_username}} has deleted an abnormal amount of Agent Pools within an hour
OUTLIER-S00030.summary_expression: User: {{user_username}} has deleted an abnormal amount of Agent Pools within an hour

OUTLIER-S00031.description_expression: [Disabled by Default] Detects unusual amounts of inbound data transfers to S3 buckets (requires AWS Data events). Verify if the user, role, and IP address associated with this activity are authorized. This rule is disabled by default due to potential alert volume. Before enabling, consider excluding authorized users with regular large transfers via match lists, and adjust floor value and model sensitivity as needed.
OUTLIER-S00031.match_expression:
metadata_vendor = "Amazon AWS"
AND application = "s3.amazonaws.com"
AND action = "PutObject"
AND fields["eventCategory"] = "Data"
AND !isBlank(fields["additionalEventData.bytesTransferredIn"])
OUTLIER-S00031.name: Outlier in Data Transferred into an S3 Bucket by User
OUTLIER-S00031.name_expression: Outlier in Data Transferred into an S3 Bucket by User
OUTLIER-S00031.normalized_description: [Disabled by Default] Detects unusual amounts of inbound data transfers to S3 buckets (requires AWS Data events). Verify if the user, role, and IP address associated with this activity are authorized. This rule is disabled by default due to potential alert volume. Before enabling, consider excluding authorized users with regular large transfers via match lists, and adjust floor value and model sensitivity as needed.
OUTLIER-S00031.normalized_signal_name: Outlier in Data Transferred into an S3 Bucket by User
OUTLIER-S00031.normalized_summary: Outlier in Data Transferred into an S3 Bucket by {{user_username}}
OUTLIER-S00031.summary_expression: Outlier in Data Transferred into an S3 Bucket by {{user_username}}

THRESHOLD-S00003.description: Attackers will often perform reconnaissance against customer environments to better understand resources on the network. In doing this behavior they are usually blocked by firewall rules while performing their discovery. This rule looks for a single source IP address network traffic by AWS security groups to at least 10 different destination IP addresses within a 5-minute window.
THRESHOLD-S00003.expression:
metadata_vendor = 'Amazon AWS'
AND metadata_product = 'VpcFlowLogs'
AND dstPort <= 1024
AND srcDevice_ip_isInternal = true
AND dstDevice_ip_isInternal = true
AND NOT dstPort IN (53,80,443,8080,137,138,139)
AND NOT array_contains(listMatches, 'proxy_servers')
AND NOT array_contains(listMatches, 'lan_scanner_exception_ips')
AND NOT array_contains(listMatches, 'nms_ips')
AND NOT array_contains(listMatches, 'vuln_scanners')
THRESHOLD-S00003.name: Amazon VPC - Network Scan
THRESHOLD-S00003.normalized_description: Attackers will often perform reconnaissance against customer environments to better understand resources on the network. In doing this behavior they are usually blocked by firewall rules while performing their discovery. This rule looks for a single source IP address network traffic by AWS security groups to at least 10 different destination IP addresses within a 5-minute window.
THRESHOLD-S00003.normalized_summary: Potential network scan activity detected from {{srcDevice_ip}}
THRESHOLD-S00003.summary_expression: Potential network scan activity detected from {{srcDevice_ip}}

THRESHOLD-S00004.description: Attackers will often perform reconnaissance against customer environments to better understand resources on the network. In doing this behavior they are usually blocked by firewall rules while performing their discovery. This rule looks for a single source IP address network traffic by AWS security groups to multiple distinct destination port numbers within a short time window.
THRESHOLD-S00004.expression:
metadata_vendor = 'Amazon AWS'
AND metadata_product = 'VpcFlowLogs'
AND dstPort <= 1024
AND srcDevice_ip_isInternal = true
AND dstDevice_ip_isInternal = true
AND NOT dstPort IN (53,80,443,8080,137,138,139)
AND NOT array_contains(listMatches, 'proxy_servers')
AND NOT array_contains(listMatches, 'lan_scanner_exception_ips')
AND NOT array_contains(listMatches, 'nms_ips')
AND NOT array_contains(listMatches, 'vuln_scanners')
THRESHOLD-S00004.name: Amazon VPC - Port Scan
THRESHOLD-S00004.normalized_description: Attackers will often perform reconnaissance against customer environments to better understand resources on the network. In doing this behavior they are usually blocked by firewall rules while performing their discovery. This rule looks for a single source IP address network traffic by AWS security groups to multiple distinct destination port numbers within a short time window.
THRESHOLD-S00004.normalized_summary: Potential port scanning activity detected from {{srcDevice_ip}}
THRESHOLD-S00004.summary_expression: Potential port scanning activity detected from {{srcDevice_ip}}

THRESHOLD-S00009.description: Directory traversal is an attempt by an attacker to access files located on the host which are not intended to be returned by the web server. For example, attackers seeking usernames/passwords for the host will focus on paths like ../../etc/passwd, ../../../etc/shadow, etc. When successful, a directory traversal attack results in the attacker gaining access to sensitive information and identifying a mechanism of future attack. When unsuccessful, directory traversal is an indication of ongoing external reconnaissance.
THRESHOLD-S00009.expression: http_url LIKE '%../../%' AND dstDevice_ip_isInternal = true AND toString(http_response_statusCode) NOT LIKE '2%' AND (listMatches IS NULL OR NOT array_contains(listMatches, 'vuln_scanners'))
THRESHOLD-S00009.name: Directory Traversal - Unsuccessful
THRESHOLD-S00009.normalized_description: Directory traversal is an attempt by an attacker to access files located on the host which are not intended to be returned by the web server. For example, attackers seeking usernames/passwords for the host will focus on paths like ../../etc/passwd, ../../../etc/shadow, etc. When successful, a directory traversal attack results in the attacker gaining access to sensitive information and identifying a mechanism of future attack. When unsuccessful, directory traversal is an indication of ongoing external reconnaissance.
THRESHOLD-S00009.normalized_summary: unsuccessful directory traversal detected from source IP: {{srcDevice_ip}}
THRESHOLD-S00009.summary_expression: unsuccessful directory traversal detected from source IP: {{srcDevice_ip}}

THRESHOLD-S00015.description: HTTP web services provide response codes to client requests. The response code numbers in the 400s are used to indicate a client related error and response code numbers in the 500s represent server related errors. This rule looks for a web client receiving a large frequency of web errors within a short period of time. It is unusual for a web client to cause this many errors in a short period of time. Common occurrences for this behavior is scanning/probing activity or scripted web clients which are now encountering errors due to a misconfiguration or recent change. This rule alerts when a host external to the monitored network triggers the threshold.
THRESHOLD-S00015.expression:
srcDevice_ip_isInternal = false
AND dstDevice_ip_isInternal = true
AND http_response_statusCode BETWEEN 400 AND 599
THRESHOLD-S00015.name: HTTP Response Error Spike - External
THRESHOLD-S00015.normalized_description: HTTP web services provide response codes to client requests. The response code numbers in the 400s are used to indicate a client related error and response code numbers in the 500s represent server related errors. This rule looks for a web client receiving a large frequency of web errors within a short period of time. It is unusual for a web client to cause this many errors in a short period of time. Common occurrences for this behavior is scanning/probing activity or scripted web clients which are now encountering errors due to a misconfiguration or recent change. This rule alerts when a host external to the monitored network triggers the threshold.
THRESHOLD-S00015.normalized_summary: High number of external HTTP response errors from IP: {{srcDevice_ip}} to {{dstDevice_ip}}
THRESHOLD-S00015.summary_expression: High number of external HTTP response errors from IP: {{srcDevice_ip}} to {{dstDevice_ip}}

THRESHOLD-S00016.description: HTTP web services provide response codes to client requests. The response code numbers in the 400s are used to indicate a client related error and response code numbers in the 500s represent server related errors. This rule looks for a web client receiving a large frequency of web errors within a short period of time. It is unusual for a web client to cause this many errors in a short period of time. Common occurrences for this behavior is scanning/probing activity or scripted web clients which are now encountering errors due to a misconfiguration or recent change. This rule alerts when a host on the monitored network triggers the threshold.
THRESHOLD-S00016.expression:
srcDevice_ip_isInternal = true
AND http_response_statusCode BETWEEN 400 AND 599
AND NOT array_contains(listMatches, 'proxy_servers')
THRESHOLD-S00016.name: HTTP Response Error Spike - Internal
THRESHOLD-S00016.normalized_description: HTTP web services provide response codes to client requests. The response code numbers in the 400s are used to indicate a client related error and response code numbers in the 500s represent server related errors. This rule looks for a web client receiving a large frequency of web errors within a short period of time. It is unusual for a web client to cause this many errors in a short period of time. Common occurrences for this behavior is scanning/probing activity or scripted web clients which are now encountering errors due to a misconfiguration or recent change. This rule alerts when a host on the monitored network triggers the threshold.
THRESHOLD-S00016.normalized_summary: High number of internal HTTP response errors from IP: {{srcDevice_ip}} to {{dstDevice_ip}}
THRESHOLD-S00016.summary_expression: High number of internal HTTP response errors from IP: {{srcDevice_ip}} to {{dstDevice_ip}}

THRESHOLD-S00018.description: The MS-LSAT Remote Protocol provides a number of RPC calls that can be used to map security principal SIDs to usernames. Attackers could use this technique to perform username enumeration and identify accounts on targeted systems.
THRESHOLD-S00018.expression:
metadata_product = 'Bro'
AND metadata_deviceEventId = 'dce_rpc'
AND bro_dceRpc_operation IN ('LsaLookupSids','LsaLookupSids2','LsaLookupSids3','LsarLookupSids','LsarLookupSids2','LsarLookupSids3')
THRESHOLD-S00018.name: MS-LSAT Username Enumeration
THRESHOLD-S00018.normalized_description: The MS-LSAT Remote Protocol provides a number of RPC calls that can be used to map security principal SIDs to usernames. Attackers could use this technique to perform username enumeration and identify accounts on targeted systems.

THRESHOLD-S00026.description: This signal logic is designed to catch repetitive attempts to call (and presumably attempt to auth via) login pages for drupal, wordpress, and jira.
THRESHOLD-S00026.expression:
http_method = 'POST' AND 
(http_url_path like '%user/login%' OR 
http_url_path like '%wp-admin%' OR 
http_url_path like '%login.jsp%')
THRESHOLD-S00026.name: Possible Credential Abuse
THRESHOLD-S00026.normalized_description: This signal logic is designed to catch repetitive attempts to call (and presumably attempt to auth via) login pages for drupal, wordpress, and jira.
THRESHOLD-S00026.normalized_summary: Multiple logon attempts to login pages for Drupal, Wordpress, and Jira from IP: {{srcDevice_ip}}
THRESHOLD-S00026.summary_expression: Multiple logon attempts to login pages for Drupal, Wordpress, and Jira from IP: {{srcDevice_ip}}

THRESHOLD-S00028.description: The subject and issuer of the SSL certificate match the pattern for certificates used by TOR connections.
THRESHOLD-S00028.expression: bro_ssl_subject rlike '^CN=www[^,=]+$' AND bro_ssl_issuer rlike '^CN=www[^,=]+$' AND NOT bro_ssl_issuer = 'CN=www.example.com'
THRESHOLD-S00028.name: Possible TOR Connection
THRESHOLD-S00028.normalized_description: The subject and issuer of the SSL certificate match the pattern for certificates used by TOR connections.

THRESHOLD-S00031.description: An attacker is making a brute force attempt to gain access to an RDP server.
THRESHOLD-S00031.expression:
metadata_deviceEventId = 'rdp'
and bro_rdp_cookie != ''
and bro_rdp_result != 'Success'
and not array_contains(listMatches,'vuln_scanners')
THRESHOLD-S00031.name: RDP Brute Force Attempt
THRESHOLD-S00031.normalized_description: An attacker is making a brute force attempt to gain access to an RDP server.
THRESHOLD-S00031.normalized_summary: RDP brute force attempt from IP: {{srcDevice_ip}}
THRESHOLD-S00031.summary_expression: RDP brute force attempt from IP: {{srcDevice_ip}}

THRESHOLD-S00032.description: This rule looks for failed SMB login attempts.
THRESHOLD-S00032.name: SMB Brute Force Attempt
THRESHOLD-S00032.normalized_description: This rule looks for failed SMB login attempts.
THRESHOLD-S00032.normalized_summary: Failed SMB login attempt from IP {{srcDevice_ip}}
THRESHOLD-S00032.summary_expression: Failed SMB login attempt from IP {{srcDevice_ip}}

THRESHOLD-S00033.description: This rule looks for a host scanning other SMB hosts for specific commands similar to WannaCry
THRESHOLD-S00033.expression:
bro_smb_command.name IN ('LOGOFF_ANDX', 
                         'TREE_CONNECT_ANDX',
                         'SESSION_SETUP', 
                         'TRANSACTION') 
AND NOT     
(
    array_contains(listMatches, 'lan_scanner_exception_ips') OR 
    array_contains(listMatches, 'vuln_scanners')
)
THRESHOLD-S00033.name: SMB Scanning Detected
THRESHOLD-S00033.normalized_description: This rule looks for a host scanning other SMB hosts for specific commands similar to WannaCry

THRESHOLD-S00034.description: Many SSH authentication failures from the same source IP in a short period of time can signal a brute-force attack.
THRESHOLD-S00034.expression: metadata_product = 'Bro' and metadata_deviceEventId = 'ssh' and dstDevice_ip_isInternal = true and bro_ssh_authSuccess = false AND NOT (array_contains(listMatches, 'lan_scanner_exception_ips') OR array_contains(listMatches, 'vuln_scanners'))
THRESHOLD-S00034.name: SSH Authentication Failures
THRESHOLD-S00034.normalized_description: Many SSH authentication failures from the same source IP in a short period of time can signal a brute-force attack.
THRESHOLD-S00034.normalized_summary: Multiple SSH authentication failures from IP: {{srcDevice_ip}}
THRESHOLD-S00034.summary_expression: Multiple SSH authentication failures from IP: {{srcDevice_ip}}

THRESHOLD-S00036.description: The DNS request/response was empty or refused. This may be an indication of DNS tunneling. (Excludes IPv4/IPv6 multicast DNS and LLMNR traffic).
THRESHOLD-S00036.expression: ((dns_query = '' AND dns_returnCode = '') OR dns_returnCode = 'REFUSED') AND NOT (dstDevice_ip IN ('224.0.0.251','224.0.0.252','ff02::fb','ff02::1:3')) AND (listMatches IS NULL OR NOT (array_contains(listMatches, 'dns_servers')))
THRESHOLD-S00036.name: Too many empty/refused DNS queries
THRESHOLD-S00036.normalized_description: The DNS request/response was empty or refused. This may be an indication of DNS tunneling. (Excludes IPv4/IPv6 multicast DNS and LLMNR traffic).
THRESHOLD-S00036.normalized_summary: Multiple empty or refused DNS requests/responses from IP {{srcDevice_ip}}
THRESHOLD-S00036.summary_expression: Multiple empty or refused DNS requests/responses from IP {{srcDevice_ip}}

THRESHOLD-S00040.description: This rule detects attempted or successful connections to the standard service port for DoT services. DNS over TLS (RFC 7858, DoT) is a name resolution service that allows clients to resolve DNS records over encrypted and validated connections. DoT operates over standards compliant TLS and is specified to operate over port 853/tcp. In some environments this may be abused as a method to bypass security and policy controls. Some malicious actors leverage DoT to tunnel DNS traffic over TLS, and research has demonstrated the ability to carry out other DNS related abuse such as malware C2 over DoT as well.
THRESHOLD-S00040.expression:
ipProtocol = 'tcp' AND dstPort = 853 AND srcDevice_ip_isInternal = true AND (
    dstDevice_ip_isInternal = false
    OR array_contains(listMatches, 'proxy_servers_dst')
    )
AND NOT (array_contains(listMatches, 'guest_networks') OR array_contains(listMatches, 'dns_servers'))
THRESHOLD-S00040.name: Possible DNS over TLS (DoT) Activity
THRESHOLD-S00040.normalized_description: This rule detects attempted or successful connections to the standard service port for DoT services. DNS over TLS (RFC 7858, DoT) is a name resolution service that allows clients to resolve DNS records over encrypted and validated connections. DoT operates over standards compliant TLS and is specified to operate over port 853/tcp. In some environments this may be abused as a method to bypass security and policy controls. Some malicious actors leverage DoT to tunnel DNS traffic over TLS, and research has demonstrated the ability to carry out other DNS related abuse such as malware C2 over DoT as well.
THRESHOLD-S00040.normalized_summary: Possible DNS over TLS from source IP: {{srcDevice_ip}}
THRESHOLD-S00040.summary_expression: Possible DNS over TLS from source IP: {{srcDevice_ip}}

THRESHOLD-S00041.description: Websense blocked a large amount of activity originating from a single host within a short period of time.
THRESHOLD-S00041.expression:
metadata_vendor = 'Forcepoint' 
and metadata_product = 'Web Security' 
and action = 'blocked'
and device_ip != '0.0.0.0'
THRESHOLD-S00041.name: Websense - Blocked Activity Threshold
THRESHOLD-S00041.normalized_description: Websense blocked a large amount of activity originating from a single host within a short period of time.
THRESHOLD-S00041.normalized_summary: Large amount of activity blocked from IP: {{device_ip}}
THRESHOLD-S00041.summary_expression: Large amount of activity blocked from IP: {{device_ip}}

THRESHOLD-S00044.description: Adversaries may make use of Domain Generation Algorithms (DGAs) to dynamically identify a destination for command and control traffic rather than relying on a list of static IP addresses or domains. This has the advantage of making it much harder for defenders to block, track, or take over the command and control channel, as there could potentially be thousands of domains that malware can check for instructions. This technique is described in https://attack.mitre.org/techniques/T1483/.
THRESHOLD-S00044.expression: dns_returnCode = 'NXDOMAIN' AND dns_queryType IN ('A','AAAA') AND srcDevice_ip_isInternal = true AND NOT (array_contains(listMatches, 'vuln_scanners') OR array_contains(listMatches, 'guest_networks') OR array_contains(listMatches, 'business_domains') OR array_contains(listMatches, 'business_hostnames') OR array_contains(listMatches, 'proxy_servers') OR array_contains(listMatches, 'nat_ips'))
THRESHOLD-S00044.name: DNS DGA Lookup Behavior - NXDOMAIN Responses
THRESHOLD-S00044.normalized_description: Adversaries may make use of Domain Generation Algorithms (DGAs) to dynamically identify a destination for command and control traffic rather than relying on a list of static IP addresses or domains. This has the advantage of making it much harder for defenders to block, track, or take over the command and control channel, as there could potentially be thousands of domains that malware can check for instructions. This technique is described in https://attack.mitre.org/techniques/T1483/.
THRESHOLD-S00044.normalized_signal_name: DNS DGA Lookup Behavior - NXDOMAIN Responses

THRESHOLD-S00048.description: Traffic was observed leaving your network destined to some countries outside the United States within a time frame. This rule is shipped disabled by default as is intended for environments based in the United States with very tight network restriction policies.
THRESHOLD-S00048.expression:
srcDevice_ip_isInternal = TRUE
and dstDevice_ip_isInternal = FALSE
and dstDevice_ip_countryCode != 'US'
THRESHOLD-S00048.name: Outbound Traffic to Countries Outside the United States
THRESHOLD-S00048.normalized_description: Traffic was observed leaving your network destined to some countries outside the United States within a time frame. This rule is shipped disabled by default as is intended for environments based in the United States with very tight network restriction policies.
THRESHOLD-S00048.normalized_signal_name: Outbound Traffic to Countries Outside the United States
THRESHOLD-S00048.normalized_summary: Traffic to countries outside the United States
THRESHOLD-S00048.summary_expression: Traffic to countries outside the United States

THRESHOLD-S00051.description: An IAM account sent multiple requests to perform a wide distinct number of AWS actions in a short time frame while receiving the error code AccessDenied. This could indicate an account attempting to enumerate their access across the AWS account.
THRESHOLD-S00051.expression:
metadata_vendor = 'Amazon AWS'
AND metadata_product = 'CloudTrail'
AND fields['errorCode'] = 'AccessDenied'
AND fields['userIdentity.arn']  like '%:iam:%'
AND device_ip != 'AWS Internal'
AND device_ip not like '%.amazonaws.com'
THRESHOLD-S00051.name: AWS CloudTrail - IAM User Generating AccessDenied Errors Across Multiple Actions
THRESHOLD-S00051.normalized_description: An IAM account sent multiple requests to perform a wide distinct number of AWS actions in a short time frame while receiving the error code AccessDenied. This could indicate an account attempting to enumerate their access across the AWS account.
THRESHOLD-S00051.normalized_signal_name: AWS CloudTrail - IAM User Generating AccessDenied Errors Across Multiple Actions
THRESHOLD-S00051.normalized_summary: AWS API Call attempts denied by user: {{user_username}} 10+ times
THRESHOLD-S00051.summary_expression: AWS API Call attempts denied by user: {{user_username}} 10+ times

THRESHOLD-S00059.description: Detects multiple network share access attempts from one internal host to another. Attackers will often scan the network for open network shares in order to pivot between internal hosts.
THRESHOLD-S00059.expression:
metadata_vendor = 'Microsoft'
AND metadata_product = 'Windows'
AND metadata_deviceEventId = 'Security-5140'
AND NOT srcDevice_ip = '127.0.0.1'
AND NOT resource like '%Printer%'
THRESHOLD-S00059.name: Network Share Scan
THRESHOLD-S00059.normalized_description: Detects multiple network share access attempts from one internal host to another. Attackers will often scan the network for open network shares in order to pivot between internal hosts.
THRESHOLD-S00059.normalized_summary: Multiple network share access attempts from IP: {{srcDevice_ip}}
THRESHOLD-S00059.summary_expression: Multiple network share access attempts from IP: {{srcDevice_ip}}

THRESHOLD-S00060.description: Detects multiple network share access attempts from one internal host to another for a single share. Attackers will often scan the network for specific open network shares, such as ADMIN$ used for PSEXEC, in order to pivot between internal hosts.
THRESHOLD-S00060.expression:
metadata_vendor = 'Microsoft'
AND metadata_product = 'Windows'
AND metadata_deviceEventId = 'Security-5140'
AND NOT srcDevice_ip = '127.0.0.1'
AND 
    (
    resource in ('IPC$', 'ADMIN$','C$')
    OR resource like '%\IPC$'
    OR resource like '%\ADMIN$'
    OR resource like '%\C$'
    )
THRESHOLD-S00060.name: Network Share Sweep
THRESHOLD-S00060.normalized_description: Detects multiple network share access attempts from one internal host to another for a single share. Attackers will often scan the network for specific open network shares, such as ADMIN$ used for PSEXEC, in order to pivot between internal hosts.
THRESHOLD-S00060.normalized_summary: Network share: {{resource}} access attempt to multiple hosts from IP: {{srcDevice_ip}}
THRESHOLD-S00060.summary_expression: Network share: {{resource}} access attempt to multiple hosts from IP: {{srcDevice_ip}}

THRESHOLD-S00061.description: When attempting to pivot within an internal AD network, attacks will query the Domain Controllers for passwords stored within group policy files. This is typically done by querying the SYSVOL share.  A host querying the SYSVOL share is suspicious activity and could be indicative of this type of attack.
THRESHOLD-S00061.expression:
metadata_vendor = 'Microsoft'
AND metadata_product = 'Windows'
AND metadata_deviceEventId = 'Security-5140'
AND NOT srcDevice_ip = '127.0.0.1'
AND (
    LOWER(resource) like '%\sysvol'
    OR
    LOWER(resource) = 'sysvol'
    )
THRESHOLD-S00061.name: SYSVOL Share Sweep
THRESHOLD-S00061.normalized_description: When attempting to pivot within an internal AD network, attacks will query the Domain Controllers for passwords stored within group policy files. This is typically done by querying the SYSVOL share.  A host querying the SYSVOL share is suspicious activity and could be indicative of this type of attack.

THRESHOLD-S00062.description: Potentially detects an attacker attempting to enumerate active users on the network. Attacks will use enumeration tools such as Bloodhound that will quickly query the domain controller by submitting multiple Kerberos ticket requests with forged device names to gather user and group information for those devices.
THRESHOLD-S00062.expression:
metadata_vendor = 'Microsoft'
AND metadata_product = 'Windows'
AND metadata_deviceEventId = 'Security-4769'
AND (fields['insertionstrings_f09'] = '0x0'
    OR success = true)
AND NOT user_username like '%$%'
THRESHOLD-S00062.name: Active Directory Domain Enumeration
THRESHOLD-S00062.normalized_description: Potentially detects an attacker attempting to enumerate active users on the network. Attacks will use enumeration tools such as Bloodhound that will quickly query the domain controller by submitting multiple Kerberos ticket requests with forged device names to gather user and group information for those devices.
THRESHOLD-S00062.normalized_summary: Potential Active Directory enumeration attempt by user: {{user_username}} on host: {{device_hostname}}
THRESHOLD-S00062.summary_expression: Potential Active Directory enumeration attempt by user: {{user_username}} on host: {{device_hostname}}

THRESHOLD-S00064.description: User Account Created and Deleted in 24 Hours. The temporary existence of a user account may be suspicious activity.
THRESHOLD-S00064.expression:
metadata_vendor = 'Microsoft'
AND metadata_product = 'Windows'
AND metadata_deviceEventId IN ('Security-4720', 'Security-4726')
AND NOT changeTarget LIKE 'lenovo_%'
THRESHOLD-S00064.name: User Account Created and Deleted in 24 Hours
THRESHOLD-S00064.normalized_description: User Account Created and Deleted in 24 Hours. The temporary existence of a user account may be suspicious activity.
THRESHOLD-S00064.normalized_signal_name: User Account Created and Deleted in 24 Hours
THRESHOLD-S00064.normalized_summary: User account: {{changeTarget}} create and deleted within 24 hours
THRESHOLD-S00064.summary_expression: User account: {{changeTarget}} create and deleted within 24 hours

THRESHOLD-S00065.description: A user performed a significant number of Windows interactive logins to multiple destination hosts in the past 24 hours. This behavior can be expected for some accounts, such as administrators in a Windows environment. Tuning this rule is highly recommended to filter out usernames where applicable.
THRESHOLD-S00065.expression:
metadata_vendor = 'Microsoft'
AND metadata_product = 'Windows'
AND metadata_deviceEventId = 'Security-4624'
AND logonType in ('Interactive','RemoteInteractive','CachedInteractive','2','10','11')
AND user_username not in ('')
AND NOT array_contains(listMatches, 'admin_accounts')
THRESHOLD-S00065.name: Windows - Excessive User Interactive Logons Across Multiple Hosts
THRESHOLD-S00065.normalized_description: A user performed a significant number of Windows interactive logins to multiple destination hosts in the past 24 hours. This behavior can be expected for some accounts, such as administrators in a Windows environment. Tuning this rule is highly recommended to filter out usernames where applicable.
THRESHOLD-S00065.normalized_signal_name: Windows - Excessive User Interactive Logons Across Multiple Hosts
THRESHOLD-S00065.normalized_summary: The account {{user_username}}  was observed interactively logging into 10+ unique destination hosts within the past 24 hours.
THRESHOLD-S00065.summary_expression: The account {{user_username}}  was observed interactively logging into 10+ unique destination hosts within the past 24 hours.

THRESHOLD-S00067.description: An attack against CVE-2020-1472 may create thousands of NetrServerReqChallenge & NetrServerAuthenticate3 requests in a short amount of time. https://github.com/SecuraBV/CVE-2020-1472
THRESHOLD-S00067.expression:
metadata_product = 'Bro' 
and bro_dceRpc_endpoint = 'netlogon'
and (bro_dceRpc_operation = 'NetrServerAuthenticate3' or bro_dceRpc_operation = 'NetrServerReqChallenge')
THRESHOLD-S00067.name: ZeroLogon Privilege Escalation Behavior
THRESHOLD-S00067.normalized_description: An attack against CVE-2020-1472 may create thousands of NetrServerReqChallenge & NetrServerAuthenticate3 requests in a short amount of time. https://github.com/SecuraBV/CVE-2020-1472
THRESHOLD-S00067.normalized_summary: Detected ZeroLogon privilege escalation behavior from IP: {{srcDevice_ip}} to IP: {{dstDevice_ip}}
THRESHOLD-S00067.summary_expression: Detected ZeroLogon privilege escalation behavior from IP: {{srcDevice_ip}} to IP: {{dstDevice_ip}}

THRESHOLD-S00074.description: This rule is designed to detect excessive firewall blocks within a shortened time frame. Customers will need to adjust the threshold of this rule to align with their environment's normal vs abnormal firewall traffic patterns.
THRESHOLD-S00074.expression:
objectType IN ('NetworkFlow','Network','NetworkHTTP')
AND lower(action) IN ('deny','drop','reject','block','denied','denied by acl','prevent')
THRESHOLD-S00074.name: Excessive Firewall Denies
THRESHOLD-S00074.normalized_description: This rule is designed to detect excessive firewall blocks within a shortened time frame. Customers will need to adjust the threshold of this rule to align with their environment's normal vs abnormal firewall traffic patterns.
THRESHOLD-S00074.normalized_summary: Excessive firewall denies for source IP: {{srcDevice_ip}}
THRESHOLD-S00074.summary_expression: Excessive firewall denies for source IP: {{srcDevice_ip}}

THRESHOLD-S00075.description: Kerberoasting is an attack method that allows an attacker to crack the passwords of service accounts in Active Directory offline and without fear of detection. This is facilitated by requesting service tickets that have data encrypted with weak encryption types (typically RC4). This technique is described in https://attack.mitre.org/techniques/T1208/.
THRESHOLD-S00075.expression:
metadata_vendor = 'Microsoft'
AND metadata_product = 'Windows'
AND metadata_deviceEventId = 'Security-4769'
AND application NOT LIKE '%$'
AND lower(application) != 'krbtgt'
AND (lower(fields['EventData.TicketOptions']) = '0x40810000'
    OR lower(fields['insertionstrings_f05']) = '0x40810000')
AND (fields['EventData.TicketEncryptionType'] IN ('0x17','0x18')
    OR fields['insertionstrings_f06'] IN ('0x17','0x18'))
AND NOT (array_contains(listMatches, 'downgrade_krb5_etype_authorized_users'))
THRESHOLD-S00075.name: Too Many Kerberos Encryption Downgrade SPNs (Kerberoasting)
THRESHOLD-S00075.normalized_description: Kerberoasting is an attack method that allows an attacker to crack the passwords of service accounts in Active Directory offline and without fear of detection. This is facilitated by requesting service tickets that have data encrypted with weak encryption types (typically RC4). This technique is described in https://attack.mitre.org/techniques/T1208/.
THRESHOLD-S00075.normalized_signal_name: Too Many Kerberos Encryption Downgrade SPNs (Kerberoasting)
THRESHOLD-S00075.normalized_summary: Multiple Kerberos Encryption Downgrade SPNs from host {{srcDevice_ip}} in association with user {{user_username}}
THRESHOLD-S00075.summary_expression: Multiple Kerberos Encryption Downgrade SPNs from host {{srcDevice_ip}} in association with user {{user_username}}

THRESHOLD-S00077.description: Observes for multiple Windows account lockouts in a short period on a single endpoint. This could indicate password guessing or brute force activity.
THRESHOLD-S00077.name: Multiple Windows Account Lockouts On Endpoint
THRESHOLD-S00077.normalized_description: Observes for multiple Windows account lockouts in a short period on a single endpoint. This could indicate password guessing or brute force activity.
THRESHOLD-S00077.normalized_signal_name: Multiple Windows Account Lockouts On Endpoint
THRESHOLD-S00077.normalized_summary: Multiple account lockouts detected on host: {{device_hostname}}
THRESHOLD-S00077.summary_expression: Multiple account lockouts detected on host: {{device_hostname}}

THRESHOLD-S00078.description: Observes for a large number of DNS 'Any' queries which may be indicative of a Distributed Denial of Service Attack (DDoS)
THRESHOLD-S00078.name: High Volume of DNS 'Any' Queries
THRESHOLD-S00078.normalized_description: Observes for a large number of DNS 'Any' queries which may be indicative of a Distributed Denial of Service Attack (DDoS)
THRESHOLD-S00078.normalized_summary: Large number of DNS 'Any' queries to host : {{dstDevice_hostname}}
THRESHOLD-S00078.summary_expression: Large number of DNS 'Any' queries to host : {{dstDevice_hostname}}

THRESHOLD-S00079.description: This rule detects port scanning activity from external actors against public facing assets.
THRESHOLD-S00079.expression:
objectType LIKE 'Network%'
AND srcDevice_ip_isInternal = False
AND ((dstPort>=1 AND dstPort<=1024) OR dstPort=3389)
THRESHOLD-S00079.name: Inbound Port Scan
THRESHOLD-S00079.normalized_description: This rule detects port scanning activity from external actors against public facing assets.
THRESHOLD-S00079.normalized_summary: External port scan from IP: {{srcDevice_ip}} detected
THRESHOLD-S00079.summary_expression: External port scan from IP: {{srcDevice_ip}} detected

THRESHOLD-S00080.description: This rule detects port scanning activity from one internal IP address to another, possibly indicating an attacker enumerating the network for lateral movement.
THRESHOLD-S00080.expression:
objectType = 'Network'
AND srcDevice_ip_isInternal = True
AND dstDevice_ip_isInternal = True
AND ((dstPort>=1 AND dstPort<=1024) OR dstPort=3389)
AND NOT dstPort IN (53,80,443,137,138,139)
AND NOT array_contains(listMatches, 'proxy_servers')
AND NOT array_contains(listMatches, 'lan_scanner_exception_ips')
AND NOT array_contains(listMatches, 'nms_ips')
AND NOT array_contains(listMatches, 'vuln_scanners')
THRESHOLD-S00080.name: Internal Port Scan
THRESHOLD-S00080.normalized_description: This rule detects port scanning activity from one internal IP address to another, possibly indicating an attacker enumerating the network for lateral movement.

THRESHOLD-S00081.description: This rule detects port scanning activity from one internal IP address to numerous others on the same destination port, possibly indicating an attacker enumerating the network for lateral movement.
THRESHOLD-S00081.expression:
objectType = 'Network'
AND srcDevice_ip_isInternal = True
AND dstDevice_ip_isInternal = True
AND ((dstPort>=1 AND dstPort<=1024) OR dstPort=3389)
AND NOT array_contains(listMatches, 'proxy_servers')
AND NOT array_contains(listMatches, 'lan_scanner_exception_ips')
AND NOT array_contains(listMatches, 'nms_ips')
AND NOT array_contains(listMatches, 'vuln_scanners')
THRESHOLD-S00081.name: Internal Port Sweep
THRESHOLD-S00081.normalized_description: This rule detects port scanning activity from one internal IP address to numerous others on the same destination port, possibly indicating an attacker enumerating the network for lateral movement.

THRESHOLD-S00085.description: Observes for a firewall blocking a large amount traffic from a single host in a short period of time. This may be indicative of C2 traffic
THRESHOLD-S00085.expression:
objectType like 'Network%'
AND srcDevice_ip_isInternal = true
AND (
    dstDevice_ip_isInternal = false
    OR array_contains(listMatches, 'proxy_servers_dst')
    )
AND success = false
AND dstPort <= 1024
AND NOT dstPort IN (53,80,443,8080)
AND NOT array_contains(listMatches, 'lan_scanner_exception_ips')
AND NOT array_contains(listMatches, 'vuln_scanners')
THRESHOLD-S00085.name: Excessive Outbound Firewall Blocks
THRESHOLD-S00085.normalized_description: Observes for a firewall blocking a large amount traffic from a single host in a short period of time. This may be indicative of C2 traffic
THRESHOLD-S00085.normalized_summary: Firewall blocked traffic from IP: {{srcDevice_ip}} at a high rate within a 5 minute window
THRESHOLD-S00085.summary_expression: Firewall blocked traffic from IP: {{srcDevice_ip}} at a high rate within a 5 minute window

THRESHOLD-S00086.description: Adversaries may use an existing, legitimate external Web service to exfiltrate data rather than their primary command and control channel.
THRESHOLD-S00086.name: Slack - Mass Download Events
THRESHOLD-S00086.normalized_description: Adversaries may use an existing, legitimate external Web service to exfiltrate data rather than their primary command and control channel.

THRESHOLD-S00087.description: Detects the same sessionID used from a new IP for the same user in a short period of time.
THRESHOLD-S00087.expression:
metadata_vendor = 'Slack'
AND NOT device_ip = '127.0.0.1'
AND NOT 
(
device_ip_asnOrg like 'Amazon.com%'
OR device_ip_asnNumber = 16509
)
THRESHOLD-S00087.name: Slack - Possible Session Hijacking
THRESHOLD-S00087.normalized_description: Detects the same sessionID used from a new IP for the same user in a short period of time.

THRESHOLD-S00088.description: This signal identifies GCP API GET and LIST actions that when observed in combination could indicate an actors intent to enumerate the environment. These events are generally benign, and occur during normal operations. Use this signal as context around an unfolding security story.
THRESHOLD-S00088.expression:
metadata_vendor = 'Google'
AND metadata_product = 'Google Cloud Platform'
AND description = 'type.googleapis.com/google.cloud.audit.AuditLog'
AND (lower(action) like '%.get%'
    OR lower(action) like '%.list%')
THRESHOLD-S00088.name: GCP Audit Reconnaissance Activity
THRESHOLD-S00088.normalized_description: This signal identifies GCP API GET and LIST actions that when observed in combination could indicate an actors intent to enumerate the environment. These events are generally benign, and occur during normal operations. Use this signal as context around an unfolding security story.
THRESHOLD-S00088.normalized_summary: User: {{user_username}} performed GCP recon related actions
THRESHOLD-S00088.summary_expression: User: {{user_username}} performed GCP recon related actions

THRESHOLD-S00089.description: An IAM account sent multiple requests to perform a wide distinct number of GCP Cloud actions in a short time frame while receiving the error codes. This could indicate an account attempting to enumerate their access across the GCP account.
THRESHOLD-S00089.expression:
metadata_vendor = 'Google'
AND metadata_product = 'Google Cloud Platform'
AND fields['message.data.protoPayload.response.error.code'] IN ('403','401','501','404','400')
THRESHOLD-S00089.name: GCP Audit Unauthorized API Calls
THRESHOLD-S00089.normalized_description: An IAM account sent multiple requests to perform a wide distinct number of GCP Cloud actions in a short time frame while receiving the error codes. This could indicate an account attempting to enumerate their access across the GCP account.
THRESHOLD-S00089.normalized_summary: User: {{user_username}} attempted to perform multiple distinct actions with failure
THRESHOLD-S00089.summary_expression: User: {{user_username}} attempted to perform multiple distinct actions with failure

THRESHOLD-S00090.description: Attackers will often perform reconnaissance against customer environments to better understand resources on the network. This rule looks for a single source IP scanning for the same port across multiple destinations.
THRESHOLD-S00090.expression:
metadata_vendor = 'Google'
AND metadata_product = 'Google Cloud Platform'
AND (metadata_deviceEventId like 'vpc_flows-%'
    OR metadata_deviceEventId like 'firewall-%')
AND dstPort <= 1024
AND srcDevice_ip_isInternal = true
AND dstDevice_ip_isInternal = true
AND NOT dstPort IN (53,80,443,8080,137,138,139)
AND NOT array_contains(listMatches, 'proxy_servers')
AND NOT array_contains(listMatches, 'lan_scanner_exception_ips')
AND NOT array_contains(listMatches, 'nms_ips')
AND NOT array_contains(listMatches, 'vuln_scanners')
THRESHOLD-S00090.name: GCP Port Sweep
THRESHOLD-S00090.normalized_description: Attackers will often perform reconnaissance against customer environments to better understand resources on the network. This rule looks for a single source IP scanning for the same port across multiple destinations.

THRESHOLD-S00095.description: Detects multiple failed login attempts from a single source with unique usernames over a 24 hour timeframe. This is designed to catch both slow and quick password spray type attacks. The threshold and time frame can be adjusted based on the customer's environment.
THRESHOLD-S00095.expression:
objectType = 'Authentication'
AND normalizedAction = 'logon'
AND success = false
AND NOT
(
    metadata_deviceEventId = 'Security-4776'
    AND (array_contains(listMatches, 'domain_controllers'))
)
AND NOT
(
    metadata_vendor IN ('Microsoft','Intersect Alliance')
    AND metadata_product IN ('Windows','Snare Enterprise Agent for Windows')
    AND user_username like '%$%'
)
AND NOT
(
    metadata_vendor = 'Microsoft'
    AND metadata_product = 'Azure'
    AND fields['resultType'] = '700082'
)
AND NOT array_contains(listMatches, 'vuln_scanners')
AND 
(
    srcDevice_hostname IS NOT NULL
)
THRESHOLD-S00095.name: Password Attack from Host
THRESHOLD-S00095.normalized_description: Detects multiple failed login attempts from a single source with unique usernames over a 24 hour timeframe. This is designed to catch both slow and quick password spray type attacks. The threshold and time frame can be adjusted based on the customer's environment.

objectType = 'Authentication'
AND normalizedAction = 'logon'
AND success = false
AND NOT (
metadata_deviceEventId = 'Security-4776' AND (array_contains(listMatches, 'domain_controllers')
)
)
AND NOT
    (metadata_vendor = 'Microsoft'
    AND metadata_product = 'Azure'
    AND fields['resultType'] = '700082')
AND NOT
    (metadata_vendor IN ('Microsoft','Intersect Alliance')
    AND metadata_product IN ('Windows','Snare Enterprise Agent for Windows')
    AND user_username like '%$%')
AND NOT array_contains(listMatches, 'vuln_scanners')
THRESHOLD-S00096.name: Brute Force Attempt

THRESHOLD-S00096.summary_expression: Multiple failed login attempts for user: {{user_username}}

THRESHOLD-S00097.commit_message: TLAB-2098: exclude user_username of system from impossible travel rules (#5855)
THRESHOLD-S00097.description: This signal triggers when there are two successful logins from the same user with different country codes indicating possible credential theft. It is recommended to add filtering criteria to the expression to reduce false positives, such as known VPN addresses.
THRESHOLD-S00097.expression:
objectType = 'Authentication'
AND normalizedAction IN ('logon','domainLogon')
AND success = true
AND NOT lower(user_username) = 'system'
THRESHOLD-S00097.name: Impossible Travel - Successful
THRESHOLD-S00097.normalized_description: This signal triggers when there are two successful logins from the same user with different country codes indicating possible credential theft. It is recommended to add filtering criteria to the expression to reduce false positives, such as known VPN addresses.

THRESHOLD-S00097.summary_expression: Successful impossible travel logon detected for user: {{user_username}}

THRESHOLD-S00098.expression:
objectType = 'Authentication'
AND normalizedAction IN ('logon','domainLogon')
AND success = false
AND NOT lower(user_username) = 'system'

THRESHOLD-S00099.description: Observes for long URLs with possible SQL commands within them, an indication of SQL injection activity
THRESHOLD-S00099.expression:
(
   length(http_url) > 1024
OR length(http_userAgent) > 200
)
AND lower(http_method) IN (post,patch,put)
AND lower(http_url_path) rlike '(alter%20table|create%20table|create%20index|create%20view|delete%20|drop%20|%20exists|exec%20|group%20by|having%20|insert%20into|%20join|select%20|xp_cmdshell)'
THRESHOLD-S00099.name: Long URL Containing SQL Commands
THRESHOLD-S00099.normalized_description: Observes for long URLs with possible SQL commands within them, an indication of SQL injection activity
THRESHOLD-S00099.normalized_summary: SQL commands found in long URL visited from IP: {{srcDevice_ip}}
THRESHOLD-S00099.summary_expression: SQL commands found in long URL visited from IP: {{srcDevice_ip}}

THRESHOLD-S00100.commit_message: Update Sharepoint User Downloaded Excessive Documents
THRESHOLD-S00100.description: This rule detects when a large number of documents are downloaded in a short timeframe. This behavior may be indicative of programmatic means being used to retrieve all data within the repository.
THRESHOLD-S00100.expression:
metadata_vendor = 'Microsoft'
AND metadata_product = 'Office 365'
AND metadata_deviceEventId IN ('SharePointFileOperation','6')
AND fields['Operation'] = 'FileDownloaded'
 AND NOT resource = 'OneDrive'
 AND NOT lower(file_basename) RLIKE '(\.asp|\.cer|\.cfm|\.crdownload|\.csr|\.css|\.dcr|\.htm|\.html|\.js|\.jsp|\.php|\.rss|\.xhtml|\.jpg|\.jpeg|\.png|\.one)$'
AND NOT user_username = 'app@sharepoint'
AND NOT lower(http_userAgent) LIKE 'onedrive%'
THRESHOLD-S00100.name: Sharepoint - Excessive Documents Downloaded
THRESHOLD-S00100.normalized_description: This rule detects when a large number of documents are downloaded in a short timeframe. This behavior may be indicative of programmatic means being used to retrieve all data within the repository.
THRESHOLD-S00100.normalized_summary: Sharepoint - Excessive Documents Downloaded from {{device_ip}} by {{user_username}}
THRESHOLD-S00100.summary_expression: Sharepoint - Excessive Documents Downloaded from {{device_ip}} by {{user_username}}

THRESHOLD-S00108.description: Detects when an entity downloads multiple documents or attachments in a short timeframe. This type of activity is indicative of exfiltration. Salesforce admin match lists are exclude in this rule in an attempt to reduce the false positives due to expected admin activity. These match lists should be created and populated with Salesforce admin usernames and IPs.
THRESHOLD-S00108.expression:
metadata_vendor = 'Salesforce'
AND metadata_product = 'Salesforce'
AND metadata_deviceEventId = 'DocumentAttachmentDownloads'
AND NOT array_contains(listMatches, 'salesforce_admin_ips')
AND NOT array_contains(listMatches, 'salesforce_admin_users')
THRESHOLD-S00108.name: Salesforce Excessive Documents Downloaded
THRESHOLD-S00108.normalized_description: Detects when an entity downloads multiple documents or attachments in a short timeframe. This type of activity is indicative of exfiltration. Salesforce admin match lists are exclude in this rule in an attempt to reduce the false positives due to expected admin activity. These match lists should be created and populated with Salesforce admin usernames and IPs.
THRESHOLD-S00108.normalized_summary: Salesforce Excessive Documents Downloaded from User: {{user_username}} IP: {{srcDevice_ip}}
THRESHOLD-S00108.summary_expression: Salesforce Excessive Documents Downloaded from User: {{user_username}} IP: {{srcDevice_ip}}

THRESHOLD-S00109.description:
Detects get requests against Vaults, Secrets, and Keys of 25 or greater in a 24 hour period. This may indicate credential access.

 Certain AppId's may need to be excluded if they are expected to be regularly making these requests. Azure Resource Graph's AppId is excluded already.
THRESHOLD-S00109.expression:
metadata_vendor = 'Microsoft'
AND metadata_product = 'Azure'
AND description in ('VaultGet','SecretGet', 'KeyGet')
AND success = true
AND fields['properties.httpStatusCode'] = '200'
AND NOT fields['identity.claims.appid'] = '509e4652-da8d-478d-a730-e9d4a1996ca4'
THRESHOLD-S00109.name: Azure - Excessive Key Vault Get Requests
THRESHOLD-S00109.normalized_description:
Detects get requests against Vaults, Secrets, and Keys of 25 or greater in a 24 hour period. This may indicate credential access.

 Certain AppId's may need to be excluded if they are expected to be regularly making these requests. Azure Resource Graph's AppId is excluded already.
THRESHOLD-S00109.normalized_signal_name: Azure - Excessive Key Vault Get Requests
THRESHOLD-S00109.normalized_summary: Excessive Azure Key Vault Get operations detected from user: {{user_username}} with IP: {{device_ip}} in a 24 hour period.
THRESHOLD-S00109.summary_expression: Excessive Azure Key Vault Get operations detected from user: {{user_username}} with IP: {{device_ip}} in a 24 hour period.

THRESHOLD-S00114.description: HTTP web services provide response codes to client requests. The response code numbers in the 400s are used to indicate a client related error and response code numbers in the 500s represent server related errors. This rule looks for a AWS EKS cluster receiving a large frequency of web errors within a short period of time. It is unusual for a web client to cause this many errors in a short period of time. Common occurrences for this behavior is scanning/probing activity or scripted web clients which are now encountering errors due to a misconfiguration or recent change. This rule alerts when a host on the monitored network triggers the threshold.
THRESHOLD-S00114.expression:
cloud_provider = "AWS"
AND metadata_vendor = 'Amazon AWS'
AND cloud_service = "EKS"
AND http_response_statusCode BETWEEN 400 AND 599
THRESHOLD-S00114.name: HTTP Response Error Spike to AWS EKS
THRESHOLD-S00114.normalized_description: HTTP web services provide response codes to client requests. The response code numbers in the 400s are used to indicate a client related error and response code numbers in the 500s represent server related errors. This rule looks for a AWS EKS cluster receiving a large frequency of web errors within a short period of time. It is unusual for a web client to cause this many errors in a short period of time. Common occurrences for this behavior is scanning/probing activity or scripted web clients which are now encountering errors due to a misconfiguration or recent change. This rule alerts when a host on the monitored network triggers the threshold.
THRESHOLD-S00114.normalized_signal_name: HTTP Response Error Spike to AWS EKS
THRESHOLD-S00114.normalized_summary: High number of HTTP response errors from an AWS EKS Cluster from {{srcDevice_ip}}
THRESHOLD-S00114.summary_expression: High number of HTTP response errors from an AWS EKS Cluster from {{srcDevice_ip}}

    srcDevice_ip IS NOT NULL
)
THRESHOLD-S00116.name: Password Attack from IP
THRESHOLD-S00116.normalized_description: Detects multiple failed login attempts from a single source with unique usernames over a 24 hour timeframe. This is designed to catch both slow and quick password spray type attacks. The threshold and time frame can be adjusted based on the customer's environment.

THRESHOLD-S00514.description: This rule looks for an intrusion product detecting an internal IP sending different exploits to another IP in a short timeframe.
THRESHOLD-S00514.expression:
threat_ruleType = 'intrusion'
AND srcDevice_ip_isInternal = true
AND NOT normalizedSeverity = 0
AND NOT array_contains(listMatches, 'vuln_scanners')
THRESHOLD-S00514.name: Intrusion Scan - Targeted
THRESHOLD-S00514.normalized_description: This rule looks for an intrusion product detecting an internal IP sending different exploits to another IP in a short timeframe.

THRESHOLD-S00515.description: This rule looks for an intrusion product detecting an internal IP sending the same exploit to multiple IPs in a short timeframe.
THRESHOLD-S00515.expression:
threat_ruleType = 'intrusion'
AND srcDevice_ip_isInternal = true
AND NOT normalizedSeverity = 0
AND NOT array_contains(listMatches, 'vuln_scanners')
THRESHOLD-S00515.name: Intrusion Sweep
THRESHOLD-S00515.normalized_description: This rule looks for an intrusion product detecting an internal IP sending the same exploit to multiple IPs in a short timeframe.

THRESHOLD-S00517.description: Detects the same malware signature on multiple hosts in a short timeframe. This indicates malware may be spreading in the environment.
THRESHOLD-S00517.expression:
threat_ruleType = 'malware'
AND NOT lower(threat_name) like '%pua%'
AND NOT lower(threat_name) like '%pup%'
THRESHOLD-S00517.name: Malware Outbreak
THRESHOLD-S00517.normalized_description: Detects the same malware signature on multiple hosts in a short timeframe. This indicates malware may be spreading in the environment.

THRESHOLD-S00520.description: Single host with multiple malware infections with the same signature in a short timeframe.
THRESHOLD-S00520.expression:
threat_ruleType = 'malware'
AND NOT lower(threat_name) like '%pua%'
AND NOT lower(threat_name) like '%pup%'
THRESHOLD-S00520.name: Persistent Malware Infection
THRESHOLD-S00520.normalized_description: Single host with multiple malware infections with the same signature in a short timeframe.
THRESHOLD-S00520.normalized_summary: Persistent malware with signature: {{threat_name}} on host: {{device_hostname}}
THRESHOLD-S00520.summary_expression: Persistent malware with signature: {{threat_name}} on host: {{device_hostname}}

THRESHOLD-S00608.description: An IAM account sent multiple requests to perform a wide distinct number of Alibaba Cloud actions in a short time frame while receiving error codes. This could indicate an account attempting to enumerate their access across the Alibaba account.
THRESHOLD-S00608.expression:
metadata_vendor = 'Alibaba'
AND metadata_product = 'ActionTrail'
AND fields['eventType'] = 'ApiCall'
AND (lower(fields['errorCode']) like '%forbidden%'
    OR lower(fields['errorCode']) like '%notqualified%')
THRESHOLD-S00608.name: Alibaba ActionTrail Unauthorized API Calls
THRESHOLD-S00608.normalized_description: An IAM account sent multiple requests to perform a wide distinct number of Alibaba Cloud actions in a short time frame while receiving error codes. This could indicate an account attempting to enumerate their access across the Alibaba account.
THRESHOLD-S00608.normalized_summary: User: {{user_username}} from source IP: {{srcDevice_ip}} attempted multiple actions resulting in error codes
THRESHOLD-S00608.summary_expression: User: {{user_username}} from source IP: {{srcDevice_ip}} attempted multiple actions resulting in error codes
